author: sofakng

impact.effect_if_absent: The mt76 driver will bind to Xbox Wireless Dongle USB devices (045e:02e6 and 045e:02fe) before the xone driver can claim them. The mt76 driver treats these as Wi-Fi NICs and will fail to initialize them correctly, preventing wireless Xbox controllers/headsets from functioning. The xone package (BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y) will be present but unable to bind to these devices.

notes:
CARRY PATCH — Removes USB device IDs 045e:02e6 and 045e:02fe from mt76x2u_device_table[]. These IDs refer to Xbox Wireless Dongle devices (old and new variants), which implement the Xbox GIP (Game Input Protocol) for wireless controllers/headsets — they are NOT Wi-Fi NICs despite the device ID overlap. Vanilla 6.18.38 still claims these IDs in mt76 (drivers/net/wireless/mediatek/mt76/mt76x2/usb.c:26-27), causing the mt76 driver to bind before xone can. The xone driver has its own device table claiming the same IDs (transport/dongle.c in xone codebase: 045e:02e6 'old dongle', 045e:02fe 'new dongle', plus two others). Without this patch, mt76 will win the driver-matching race and the Xbox Wireless Dongle cannot function. Failure is SILENT: the device appears to be recognized but produces no input events. The forward-ported patch (0027-mt76x2u-release-xbox-adapter-ids.patch) is byte-identical removal but adds an explanatory comment to prevent accidental reintroduction during future mt76 syncs. Patch applies cleanly to 6.18.38 with no conflicts. Provenance doc correctly identifies this as a carry (not upstream); xone is carried as BR2_PACKAGE_XONE (a Buildroot out-of-tree driver package sourced from dlundqvist/xone, the actively-maintained fork). Original commit author sofakng, forward-ported by MCF during P1.9 task.

sonnet-verified: PASS (with one citation fix). Re-ran `git show` on fork commit 817ace70b (confirmed: removes exactly the two USB_DEVICE(0x045e,...) lines, 0 added/2 removed) and `grep -n 045e /mnt/source/linux/drivers/net/wireless/mediatek/mt76/mt76x2/usb.c` -- vanilla 6.18.38 still claims 045e:02e6 and 045e:02fe at lines 26-27 verbatim, matching the record's quote exactly. Cross-checked the actual pinned xone build output (/mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/xone-f2aa9fe.../transport/dongle.c:1548-1549) and confirmed it independently claims the identical two IDs plus two more (045e:02f9, 045e:091e) -- the 'xone claims them' assertion is grounded in the live build artifact, not just documentation. defconfig BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y and BR2_PACKAGE_XOW_FIRMWARE=y confirmed set. Carry-mapping to 0027-mt76x2u-release-xbox-adapter-ids.patch confirmed correct. FIXED: provenance_doc_ref incorrectly labeled this 'class D/E' -- re-checked the section headers in patch-provenance.md and this commit's row (line 374) falls inside section 3.4 'Class D -- HID/input, still out-of-tree', not section 3.5 'Class D/E -- xone (re-source, 7 commits)' (line 377), which covers a disjoint set of 7 vendoring commits for the xone driver itself. Corrected to 'Class D' and added precise line numbers. Also tightened userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref with a direct Main_MiSTer path:line citation (input.cpp:5171, :5180) since the original cited no live file/line, only a general description.

author: Sorgelig

depends_on: BR2_PACKAGE_XONE (out-of-tree xone GIP driver must be enabled to handle excluded devices)

affected_hardware: Xbox One chatpad and other GIP accessories
impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox One/Series controllers (wired and wireless via dongle) fail to bind correctly; xpad driver would claim GIP-capable devices, preventing xone driver from handling them, resulting in broken input or driver conflicts

notes: SILENT REGRESSION CANDIDATE: This commit is essential for proper multi-driver coexistence in MiSTer's input subsystem. It excludes 6 Xbox One/Series controller device IDs from in-tree xpad driver (commented out of xpad_device[] table) and adds GIP protocol detection in xpad_probe() to return -ENODEV, allowing the out-of-tree xone driver (package/xone) to claim these devices instead. Vanilla 6.18.38 xpad.c still includes these device IDs in the table and lacks the GIP exclusion logic entirely—vanilla xpad binds GIP-capable controllers directly. If this commit is dropped, MiSTer's build contains BOTH xpad (in-kernel) and xone (kmod package) competing for the same device IDs, causing undefined driver binding behavior or broken Xbox One/Series input support. The exclusion is critical for xone's proper function since the two drivers handle the same hardware via different protocols (xone understands Xbox GIP, xpad does not). This is a carry candidate: the logic must remain to maintain the current xpad/xone boundary, and xone is explicitly enabled in mister_de10nano_defconfig (BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y). Tested in 0017 patch application and verified against xone package comments mentioning the xpad exclusion requirement.
sonnet-verified: carry-mapping into 0017 confirmed exact: fork commit comments out the same 6 xpad_device[] entries (045e:02d1/02dd/02e3/02ea/0b00/0b12) and adds the identical bInterfaceSubClass==0x47/bInterfaceProtocol==0xd0 -ENODEV check in xpad_probe(); device ID list matches the applied patch verbatim; BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y confirmed in configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:594; userspace_coupling.coupled=false confirmed (no xone/GIP references anywhere in Main_MiSTer)
subject: xpad: exclude GIP-capable controllers.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (which re-implements this commit's enhancements plus 12 follow-ups on top of vanilla 144616a80889)

forward_port.conflicts: None. Vanilla 6.18.38 has basic de10nano.dts at intel/socfpga/socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts. Fork's enhancements (aa8afe1 + follow-ups) were re-authored into patch 0004, applied atop vanilla's file. Path changed from flat arch/arm/boot/dts/ to vendor-subdirectory structure between 5.15 (fork) and 6.18 (vanilla).

impact.effect_if_absent: If dropped, MiSTer-specific device tree features this commit itself introduces are lost: aliases/ethernet0, earlyprintk bootarg, regulator_3_3v, the leds/hps_led0 gpio-leds node (prerequisite for the brightness_hw_changed sysfs attribute added later by patch 0029/b62efee23), full 12-value gmac1 skew tuning with phy-mode="rgmii" (vs. vanilla's 6-value rgmii-id tuning), i2c0 with the adxl345 accelerometer, and usb1 host mode. Vanilla's minimal de10nano.dts provides basic boot but is insufficient for MiSTer platform support. Note: this commit does not itself touch spi0/spi1 or i2c2 (those are separate commits/records: 333d49b95 and c5066763c respectively) despite being grouped under the same carried patch 0004.

license_provenance: Altera Corporation (C) 2015 copyright header; GPL v2 license text (inline)
notes: Original fork commit (Aug 2021) predates vanilla's de10nano support (v6.14, Feb 2025). The file aa8afe1 created is MUCH more comprehensive than vanilla's minimal version: includes aliases/ethernet0 (load-bearing for U-Boot MAC injection), regulator_3_3v, leds/hps_led0 (ABI requirement for Main_MiSTer SD-activity LED), complete gmac1 skew tuning (12 values vs. vanilla's 5, phy-mode rgmii vs. vanilla's rgmii-id), usb1 host-mode, additional i2c/spi configuration. Vanilla's version (144616a80889) provides only basic boot support and explicitly includes an FPGA device-tree-overlay placeholder (soc/fpga section) that aa8afe1 does not have. NOT upstream: aa8afe1's enhancements are fork-specific MiSTer platform requirements. Disposition 'carried' because vanilla's equivalent is insufficient; this commit (plus 12 follow-ups) is evolved into patch 0004 for 6.18 forward-port. Failure mode 'silent': dropping this without compensation leaves the board bootable but non-functional for MiSTer use (missing regulators, LEDs, USB, proper Ethernet skew, MAC address setup). sonnet-verified: corrected — vanilla 144616a80889 SHA/path/date confirmed via `git log` in /mnt/source/linux; two errors fixed: (1) vanilla gmac1 skew-property count was miscounted as 5, actual count is 6 (rxd0/1/2/3-skew-ps + txen-skew-ps + rxdv-skew-ps), corrected in upstream.vanilla_quote and impact.effect_if_absent (fork's 12-value count was already correct); (2) userspace_coupling cited brightness.cpp/spidev1.0 and video.cpp HDMI-probe as evidence for an 'hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed' interface claim, but brightness.cpp is an unrelated laptop-kit SPI screen-brightness driver (spi1) that this commit's diff does not touch at all (verified: no spi0/spi1 node in aa8afe1094's dts diff), and grep of Main_MiSTer for adxl345/accelerometer/ethernet0/mac_address (the things this commit actually adds) returns zero hits -- the prior citation was a false match, likely from the word 'brightness' colliding with the unrelated brightness.cpp filename. Rewrote userspace_coupling to correctly frame this commit's role as a structural prerequisite for the LED (record b62efee23) and i2c-bus-count (record c5066763c) couplings rather than a direct Main_MiSTer reference. Confirmed the fork's leds/hps_led0 node uses linux,default-trigger="heartbeat" at this commit; the carried patch 0004's final state uses trigger="mmc0", which comes from a later follow-up commit in the same lineage, not from aa8afe1094 itself -- consistent with the record's own 'plus 12 follow-ups' framing in dependencies.superseded_by.

author: Sorgelig

forward_port.conflicts: low -- the fork's two hunks (new `else if (lmp_subver == 0x2512) is_fake = true;` branch, and narrowing the Barrot pm_runtime workaround to `bcdDevice == 0x8891 && (lmp_subver==0x2512 || (lmp_subver==0x1012 && hci_rev==0x0810 && hci_ver==BT_4_0))`) land in a function vanilla has otherwise only touched via quirk-flag renames (set_bit -> hci_set_quirk) and additive quirks; a clean small patch is plausible but must be re-verified quirk-name-for-quirk-name against 6.18.38, and the pm_runtime-workaround narrowing conflicts philosophically with vanilla's still-current 'apply to every is_fake controller, it's harmless' comment at btusb.c ~2565

affected_hardware: CSR-vendor-ID (0a12) fake/clone Bluetooth dongles self-reporting lmp_subver 0x2512
impact.effect_if_absent: A CSR-vendor-ID (0a12) fake dongle that self-reports manufacturer=10, hci_rev==lmp_subver (both 0x2512), and an lmp_subver of 0x2512 is NOT flagged is_fake by any vanilla 6.18.38 branch: it passes the manufacturer/hci_rev-consistency check (because it self-reports consistently), and 0x2512 (9490 decimal) exceeds every ranged check's upper bound, including the top BT4.0 tier <= 0x22bb (8891 decimal) -- so it falls through all six is_fake branches untouched. Result: none of the six CSR-clone quirks (HCI_QUIRK_BROKEN_STORED_LINK_KEY, HCI_QUIRK_BROKEN_ERR_DATA_REPORTING, HCI_QUIRK_BROKEN_FILTER_CLEAR_ALL, HCI_QUIRK_NO_SUSPEND_NOTIFIER, HCI_QUIRK_BROKEN_READ_VOICE_SETTING, HCI_QUIRK_BROKEN_READ_PAGE_SCAN_TYPE) and none of the reset/discovery quirk-clears are applied, AND the Barrot-8041a02 pm_runtime force-suspend/wake bulk-RX workaround is never triggered for this device -- it is treated as a genuine, well-behaved CSR controller. If the real chip is in fact a broken clone, this manifests as controller lockups, broken link-key storage, or a Bluetooth adapter that never receives bulk-RX data (keyboard/mouse HID-over-BT) until manually suspended -- exactly the class of bug upstream is still separately patching for the neighboring bcdDevice=0x8891/0a12:0001 dongle family as late as commit 2c1dda2acc41 (2024-10-16, 'Fix regression with fake CSR controllers 0a12:0001', bug report literally shows bcdDevice=88.91 i.e. 0x8891).

notes: VERIFIED: vanilla 6.18.38's CSR detection genuinely never mentions or numerically covers lmp_subver==0x2512 (confirmed by `git grep 0x2512` across drivers/bluetooth/ in /mnt/source/linux -- zero hits -- and by `git log --all -S'0x2512' -- drivers/bluetooth/btusb.c` in vanilla -- zero commits, ever). Traced the full six-branch if/elif chain in btusb_setup_csr (manufacturer/hci_rev-mismatch check, five ranged lmp_subver<=X && hci_ver>Y checks topping out at <=0x22bb/BT4.0, and one bcdDevice==0x0134-specific check): 0x2512 (9490) numerically exceeds every range ceiling including the highest (0x22bb = 8891), so the ranged checks cannot catch it regardless of hci_ver. The only way vanilla would still flag such a device is branch 1 (manufacturer != 10 OR hci_rev != lmp_subver) -- which requires the dongle to actively misreport manufacturer or have an inconsistent hci_rev/lmp_subver pair. The MiSTer fork placed its explicit `lmp_subver == 0x2512` check as the LAST elif, after all ranged checks and the bcdDevice==0x0134 check -- strong circumstantial evidence the original author observed a real dongle in the field that passed branch 1 (self-consistent manufacturer=10, hci_rev==lmp_subver) and every ranged check, and needed a bespoke catch. This cannot be verified as certain without the physical dongle (no lmp_subver value is visible in USB descriptors alone), hence verification.confidence is 'medium' not 'high' and the disposition stays needs-verification per task instructions, but the STRUCTURAL claim (vanilla's ranges cannot numerically reach 0x2512) is proven with high confidence via direct grep/read of the live 6.18.38 source -- not a hardware question. NOTE ON PRIOR RECORD: the previous pass's 'CRITICAL MISMATCH' framing against patch-provenance.md was itself a misreading -- patch-provenance.md line 347 and the Q5 entry (line 1180) ALREADY correctly flag this exact gap ('6.18 does not explicitly handle lmp_subver==0x2512 ... 0x2512 falls outside 6.18's <=0x22bb range test ... flag for P3.13 hardware test'), so agrees_with_provenance_doc is corrected to true; the doc's headline table label of 'superseded' is a loose summary but its own caveat column states the same conclusion this record reaches. Prior record also miscounted vanilla's ranged checks as 'four' (it is five: <=0x034e, <=0x0529, <=0x0c5c, <=0x1899, <=0x22bb) and cited stale line numbers -- corrected here to :2481-2521 (verified live via grep in this pass). SEPARATE FROM the 0x2512 detection gap: the fork commit ALSO narrows the Barrot-8041a02 pm_runtime-suspend workaround from 'apply to every is_fake device' (vanilla's still-current behavior, unchanged in 6.18.38, with an explicit code comment defending the broad/generic approach as 'harmless') down to a specific bcdDevice==0x8891 gate. This is a genuine behavioral divergence, not a bug -- vanilla may be over-applying an unnecessary pm_runtime force-suspend/resume cycle (200ms sleep) to CSR clones that don't need it; whether that is harmless (as vanilla claims) or has side effects on non-Barrot clones is itself unverified and outside this task's scope, but should be noted for the carry-decision discussion. CARRY-DECISION: if MiSTer deployments include 0a12:0001-family or similarly-branded fake CSR BT dongles reporting lmp_subver 0x2512, this is a one-line, low-risk carry (the `else if (lmp_subver == 0x2512) is_fake = true;` branch alone, appended after 6.18.38's existing bcdDevice==0x0134 elif) that restores detection without touching vanilla's now-richer quirk set. Recommend: carry candidate, gate final accept/reject on P3.13 hardware test (does any owned/tested MiSTer BT dongle actually report lmp_subver 0x2512?) as patch-provenance.md Q5 already proposes -- this verification pass found no reason to override that plan, only firmer evidence for it. | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): detection half carried as 0036 (exact lmp_subver==0x2512 branch); the fork's Barrot pm_runtime narrowing is superseded by 6.18's reworked CSR handling. Hardware confirmation on a real 0x2512 clone still welcome.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md line 347 (table row for b02a4a011) and line 1180 (Q5, open questions)
sonnet-verified: corrected — vanilla 6.18.38 genuinely never covers lmp_subver==0x2512 (structurally proven, zero grep/log hits); prior record was directionally right but mislabeled as a provenance-doc mismatch when the doc already flagged this gap, and undercounted vanilla ranged checks; kept needs-verification (hardware-gated carry candidate) with corrected quotes, line numbers, device-ID inference, and agrees_with_provenance_doc flipped true.
subject: btusb: support for more CSR clones.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Flydigi Vader 4 Pro controller reports incorrect button codes over Bluetooth D-Input mode, mapping various extra buttons (grips, 4th face button, MODE) as generic consumer-page keys (KEY_ENTER, KEY_TV, KEY_CHANNELUP, etc.) instead of expected gamepad codes. Without driver remapping, buttons are unusable.

license_provenance: GPL-2.0-or-later; copyright Alexey Melnikov (2026); driver authored as in-tree code; no external upstream project forked
notes: New HID driver for Flydigi Vader 4 Pro over Bluetooth. The controller reports button codes as generic consumer-page keys over BT D-Input mode; this driver's .event hook intercepts and remaps them to proper gamepad codes: KEY_ENTER→BTN_Z, BTN_0→BTN_C, KEY_CHANNELUP→BTN_GRIPL2, KEY_TV→BTN_GRIPL, KEY_CHANNELDOWN→BTN_GRIPR, KEY_EJECTCD→BTN_GRIPR2, KEY_CAMERA→BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY3, KEY_RED→BTN_MODE. Forward-port to 6.18.38 confirmed: BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPR2 button codes are mainline UAPI since v6.18 (commit 97c01e65ef4c) with byte-identical numeric values (0x224–0x227); no UAPI conflicts. Patch 0013-hid-flydigi-vader.patch applies cleanly to 6.18 with improved code formatting from fork commit. CONFIG_HID_VADER4=m enabled in our linux.config. No userspace coupling detected in Main_MiSTer. Silent failure mode: controller simply won't work correctly without the driver, no build/boot error.
sonnet-verified: confirmed clean — device ID d7d7:0041 verified against fork's HID_BLUETOOTH_DEVICE() call. Driver body diffed line-by-line against 0013-hid-flydigi-vader.patch: only whitespace/brace-style cleanup (indentation, split if/return, added blank lines), no functional changes — consistent with the record's own 'improved code formatting' characterization. BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR2 = 0x224-0x227 confirmed byte-identical in vanilla include/uapi/linux/input-event-codes.h:605-608, sourced from commit 97c01e65ef4c which is present in the v6.18.38 history. userspace_coupling.coupled=false confirmed by grep — no 'vader', 'd7d7', or BTN_GRIPx references anywhere in Main_MiSTer. CONFIG_HID_VADER4=m confirmed in linux.config:359. License/author distinction (committer Sorgelig vs. code author Alexey Melnikov) verified against driver's SPDX/copyright header and MODULE_AUTHOR. No amendments needed beyond adding this verdict.
subject: input: add HID driver to fix Flydigi Vader 4 Pro mapping in D-Input over Bluetooth.

author: Sorgelig

depends_on: 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (defines hps_led0 device in device tree; explicit comment 'ABI: Main_MiSTer polls /sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed')

forward_port.effort: trivial (byte-identical hunks; struct gpio_led_data, gpio_led_set(), and create_gpio_led() unchanged since 5.15 in 6.18.38)

affected_hardware: DE10-Nano (hps_led0 is the on-board LED wired to mmc0 activity trigger by 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch)
impact.effect_if_absent: On-screen disk-activity LED indicator (driven by polling /sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed) becomes non-functional; mmc activity no longer visible to user.

notes: CRITICAL: Silent failure mode — dropping this patch silently breaks a load-bearing user-visible feature (disk-activity indicator). The patch is explicitly required by Main_MiSTer's input polling loop (input.cpp:6270-6274) to signal mmc activity. Vanilla 6.18.38 has the led_classdev_notify_brightness_hw_changed infrastructure (led-class.c:158-166) but gpio_led driver does NOT call it; the infrastructure exists because other drivers (e.g., hid-lg-g15, hid-playstation) use it elsewhere. This is a justified carry: feature needed by MiSTer's userspace that vanilla's gpio_led doesn't implement. Patch-provenance.md F-2 explicitly documents the consumer and affirms the P1.9 ruling 'Do not drop.' The carried patch includes detailed provenance header citing the origin SHA, forward-port verification (byte-identical hunks), and the Main_MiSTer coupling. sonnet-verified: corrected — fork diff (prev_value tracking, led_classdev_notify_brightness_hw_changed() call, LED_BRIGHT_HW_CHANGED flag) confirmed byte-identical against carried patch 0029; vanilla gpio_led_set() at drivers/leds/leds-gpio.c:38-59 confirmed to lack all three (exact line range verified via grep -n); led_classdev_notify_brightness_hw_changed() infrastructure confirmed at led-class.c:158-166 exactly as cited. Main_MiSTer citations input.cpp:4052 (LED_MONITOR define) and input.cpp:6270 (POLLPRI poll check) confirmed exact. One citation error fixed: input.cpp:5146-5147 was described as opening with 'O_RDONLY | O_NONBLOCK'; the actual flag in the source is O_RDONLY | O_CLOEXEC — corrected. DISKLED_ON call itself is at input.cpp:6275 (inside the block starting at the 6270 poll check), now noted as a range.
provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md F-2 (class F-2 carry); explicit P1.9 ruling: 'Consumer confirmed: Main_MiSTer polls brightness_hw_changed to drive the on-screen disk-activity LED. Do not drop.'
subject: hps_led: enable brightness change notification.

author: HGD73

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech K400 Plus keyboards (046d:404D) would not be recognized as HIDPP_QUIRK_CLASS_K400 devices. They would function as basic HID mice but would silently lose K400-specific features: Fn key inversion disable (causing media keys to require Fn hold), and tap-to-click disable module parameter support.

notes: ALERT: This commit appears in a GROUPED row of patch-provenance.md (line 367) alongside fc8f3c2c6. The grouped row contains two logically related but distinct commits: fc8f3c2c6 (adds FN inversion disable feature) + b745ce6d9 (adds K400 Plus device ID to enable that feature). My assigned commit (b745ce6d9) is the device-table entry addition only; it contains no actual FN disable code — that code comes from fc8f3c2c6. This is a pure 3-line device entry addition: reformats existing K400 entry to single line, then adds K400 Plus entry with same quirk. If fc8f3c2c6 is dropped, this device entry alone provides no FN disable functionality (silent regression). Carried in patch 0019 with clean apply — forward-port applied to 6.18.38 as part of P1.9 by Michael C. Ferguson. Not in vanilla 6.18.38 (grep devices/hid/hid-logitech-hidpp.c line 4559-4561 shows only 0x4024 K400 entry, no 0x404D). CORRECTION (sonnet review): patch 0019's header explicitly documents both origin SHAs (fc8f3c2c69dc64da15d5546e3af0780e0218d7e0 and b745ce6d9f24bea7024bd364d4b16f484f9997ab); confirmed by reading the applied patch file directly. Note also that the carried patch keeps the original K400 entry in its 2-line form (does not collapse it to one line as the fork's own combined diff does) — a cosmetic difference only, not b745ce6d9's own hunk, and not semantically significant.

author: Sorgelig

forward_port.conflicts: Vanilla 6.18.38 uses a hardcoded 5-element player_ids array (indexed 1-5, no index 0) and modulo arithmetic (`player_id % ARRAY_SIZE(player_ids)`), not the fork's explicit boundary check. The fork's expanded 6-element array (with player_ids[0]=0 for off) and explicit boundary check are already absent in vanilla. This commit would require not only vanilla's fundamental player-LED architecture to be replaced with the fork's, but also the expansion to 6/7 players. Not forward-portable without wholesale LED subsystem re-implementation.

impact.effect_if_absent: Kernel-capability-only: the DualSense LED classdev's 6-element player_ids[] table (index 6 = BIT(4)|BIT(0)) and max_brightness=6 boundary check would not exist, so writing 6 to the `:player_id` LED would be rejected/clamped rather than lighting a 6th-player pattern. Zero user-visible effect today: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2702 hardcodes `set_led(led_path, ":player_id", (num > 5) ? 0 : num)`, so it never writes 6 to this LED regardless of whether the kernel would accept it — the earlier num>7 clamp at input.cpp:2695 only bounds the DS4 color_code[] fallback path, not this write. No MiSTer user can reach player slot 6 on a DualSense today, with or without this commit.

notes:
SONNET ESCALATION VERIFICATION — every claim re-derived from scratch:

1. FORK DIFF (`git show b76b4bc6a` in /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer, +5/-4, drivers/hid/hid-playstation.c only): grows `static const int player_ids[6]` to `player_ids[7]`, appending `BIT(4) | BIT(0)` as the new index-6 pattern; changes the clamp `if(player_id > 5) player_id = 0;` to `if(player_id > 6) player_id = 0;`; and raises `ds->led.max_brightness` from 5 to 6 in ds_leds_create(). Indices 0-5 (off + players 1-5) are untouched byte-for-byte — this commit purely appends a 7th table entry and widens two bounds checks. Confirmed exact diff quoted verbatim above.

2. VANILLA (/mnt/source/linux, HEAD detached at v6.18.38, `git status` clean): hid-playstation.c:1701-1709 hardcodes `static const int player_ids[5] = { BIT(2), BIT(3)|BIT(1), BIT(4)|BIT(2)|BIT(0), BIT(4)|BIT(3)|BIT(1)|BIT(0), BIT(4)|BIT(3)|BIT(2)|BIT(1)|BIT(0) }` (re-grepped and re-quoted directly, exact line-number and byte match to what was already on record) with `u8 player_id = ds->base.player_id % ARRAY_SIZE(player_ids)` — a 5-slot, 0-indexed, modulo-wrapping design with NO off/BIT(4)|BIT(0) 7th slot and no forward-portable expansion point. `git log --all -S'player_ids[6]' -S'player_ids[7]'` and `-S'BIT(4) | BIT(0)'` over the full vanilla history return nothing reachable from v6.18.38. equivalence=contradicted confirmed correct.

3. MAIN_MISTER (re-grepped input.cpp fresh): update_num_hw() at input.cpp:2692 opens `if (num > 7) num = 7;` (line 2695) — but that clamp only bounds the DS4 `color_code[8][3]` FALLBACK array used in the else-branch (input.cpp:2709-2726) when the `:player_id` LED write fails. The actual `:player_id` write, line 2702, is separately and unconditionally clamped: `set_led(led_path, ":player_id", (num > 5) ? 0 : num)`. This clamp is independent of and unaffected by anything in b76b4bc6a — it was already `> 5` before this commit existed and remains so today. No occurrence of 6 being passed to this specific LED write exists anywhere in Main_MiSTer (grepped '> 6', 'player.*6', 'max_brightness', all of input.cpp:2692-2729). CONFIRMED: player slot 6 is unreachable from shipped userspace, independent of whether this kernel commit is carried.

4. DEPENDENCY / CARRY-DECISION COUPLING: b76b4bc6a's diff hunks are pure extensions of the `player_ids[]` array and `ds->led` classdev that f84543926 (`dualsense: add player id led control.`, full sha f84543926371d7911bff2d3a12daa5d325e4c2d9) introduced — confirmed by diffing f84543926's version of dualsense_set_player_leds()/ds_leds_create() against this commit's before-state, same symbols, same LED classdev, no intervening commit touches player_ids/max_brightness (checked 60e08955f and 0d60c3482 diffs directly: 60e08955f only edits mute-LED code inside the same function neighborhood — no player_ids/max_brightness hunks; 0d60c3482, the lightbar commit, greps clean for player_ids/player_id/max_brightness — genuinely unrelated feature, touches only :red/:green/:blue lightbar LEDs). Corrected dependencies.depends_on to list ONLY f84543926 (full sha) — the prior record's inclusion of 0d60c3482 was not substantiated by any functional link and is dropped here; 60e08955f's own record (dependencies.depends_on=[]) independently corroborates that lightbar/mute are not part of this dependency chain. carry decision rides on f84543926; if carried, player-6 extension is trivial to include; impact today none (no userspace reaches player 6).

5. GROUPING / MISCLASSIFICATION: patch-provenance.md:337 (Class C table, re-read fresh) groups f84543926, 0d60c3482, 60e08955f, b76b4bc6a under two vanilla SHAs (8c0ab553b072, 8e5198a12d64) claiming all four are 'pre-upstream backports' now covered. f84543926's own record (records/f84543926371d7911bff2d3a12daa5d325e4c2d9.json) independently confirms equivalence=contradicted/disposition=misclassified for the base :player_id classdev — vanilla's 5-LED-keyed-to-gamepad-device architecture (8c0ab553b072) is a genuinely different, Main_MiSTer-incompatible interface. Since b76b4bc6a is a direct, single-purpose extension of that same classdev/array (not an independent feature), and vanilla has no 6/7-player expansion mechanism at all, b76b4bc6a inherits the same misclassification: provenance's 'dropped-upstream' claim does not hold for this commit either. disposition=misclassified confirmed correct.

6. SEVERITY/FAILURE_MODE CORRECTION: prior record had severity='feature-loss', copying the framing from the base f84543926 commit (which DOES have current userspace coupling — Main_MiSTer actively writes :player_id for players 1-5 and silently falls back to a different visual cue on vanilla, a real behavior change today). This commit is different in kind: it ONLY adds capability for slot 6, which input.cpp:2702's hardcoded `(num > 5) ? 0 : num` clamp means Main_MiSTer never requests, with or without this commit being present. There is zero behavioral difference for any MiSTer user today, carried or not — corrected severity 'feature-loss' -> 'cosmetic' (matching the precedent set by the sibling 60e08955f/mute record, which used the same 'real kernel capability, zero current consumer, LED-only feature category' reasoning to land on 'cosmetic' rather than 'feature-loss' or 'none'). failure_mode='silent' confirmed correct and unchanged: dropping this produces no build/boot error on vanilla or in the fork — it's an inert table-size difference.

7. No carried_patch: grepped board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/*.patch for player_id/player_leds/max_brightness — only 0012-hid-fanatec.patch matches (max_brightness=1 for an unrelated Fanatec wheel LED, confirmed by its `--- a/drivers/hid/Kconfig` header — different driver entirely). Not carried.

sonnet-verified: corrected — dependency chain narrowed to f84543926 only (0d60c3482 dropped, unsubstantiated), severity downgraded feature-loss -> cosmetic to reflect zero current userspace reach (input.cpp:2702 clamps this LED to 0-5 independent of this commit), disposition=misclassified and equivalence=contradicted both confirmed correct against a fresh vanilla re-derivation; carry decision for this commit rides entirely on f84543926's carry decision. | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): folded into 0033 (7-entry pattern table incl. player-6 + clamp).
subject: dualsense: leds config for player 6.

userspace_coupling.interface: LED class device `<hid-device-id>:player_id` brightness (fork extends valid range from 0-5 to 0-6 by growing player_ids[] from 6 to 7 elements and raising the clamp from `player_id > 5` to `player_id > 6` / max_brightness from 5 to 6). Kernel-side change only; Main_MiSTer not updated to use player 6.
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2702 `if (set_led(led_path, ":player_id", (num > 5) ? 0 : num))` hardcodes a 0-5 clamp on this specific LED write, independent of the file's earlier `if (num > 7) num = 7;` at input.cpp:2695 (which only bounds the DS4 color_code[8][3] fallback array). Grepped input.cpp:2692-2729 (update_num_hw, the only writer of `:player_id`) and the whole tree for 'player.*6', 'max_brightness', '> 6' — no code path anywhere in Main_MiSTer ever passes 6 to this LED. coupled=false because this commit is pure kernel-side infrastructure with zero shipped userspace consumer.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: CORRECTED (sonnet verification): none. uart1: serial@ffc03000 in vanilla 6.18's socfpga.dtsi (arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/socfpga.dtsi:921-932) carries NO 'status' property, and neither did the fork's own socfpga.dtsi at this commit's parent (verified: `git show c4d12c768^:arch/arm/boot/dts/socfpga.dtsi` also has no status on uart1). Per drivers/of/base.c:476-484 (__of_device_is_available: 'True if the status property is absent or set to okay'), an absent status property means the node is already enabled by default -- confirmed live by vanilla's own &uart0 override (socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts:87-95), which sets clock-frequency but no status, and is undisputedly the working boot console. So this commit's sole content (`&uart1 { status = "okay"; };`) was already a no-op even in the original 5.15 fork: uart1 was enabled by DT default both before and after. Our carried patch 0004's &uart1 stanza (lines 310-313) contains ONLY the DMA-delete-property lines (see 7d2df2d2d/record), no status property -- and uart1 is still enabled, via the same default-enabled mechanism, not because 0004 re-adds an explicit enable. Dropping this commit's specific content from history would change nothing observable.

notes:
ESCALATE: This commit is part of a grouped row in patch-provenance.md:310 containing 13 commits (aa8afe109, e40563ae1, 2548c2978, 6827e7644, 6c2d53934, 246984fce, 1337de1fd, c4d12c768, 7d2df2d2d, c5066763c, 071d9092e, f52690120, 077c2c317). The group was consolidated into single patch 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch during forward-port to 6.18 (P1.7). Original commit adds 4 lines (just &uart1 { status = "okay"; }). Carried patch 0004's &uart1 stanza (lines 310-313) only has the DMA property deletion (/delete-property/ dmas; /delete-property/ dma-names) from the LATER commit 7d2df2d2d -- it does NOT re-add an explicit status=okay. UART1 is defined in vanilla socfpga.dtsi:921-932 but with no status property at all (see sonnet-verified correction below), and vanilla's board dts likewise never sets status on uart0/uart1 (only clock-frequency). CONFIG_SERIAL_8250_NR_UARTS=2 in our config supports 2 UARTs. Main_MiSTer userspace citations verified: Main_MiSTer/fpga_base_addr_ac5.h:25 has `#define SOCFPGA_UART1_ADDRESS 0xffc03000` (matches uart1's reg = <0xffc03000 ...> in socfpga.dtsi:921 exactly) and Main_MiSTer/menu.cpp has MENU_UART1 (line 142 enum, referenced lines 2996/3567/3574/3665) for UART-mode menu state -- both citations confirmed to exist as claimed.

sonnet-verified CORRECTION (severity downgrade): impact.severity was 'feature-loss', now 'none'. Investigated whether dropping this specific commit's content actually loses functionality. Checked drivers/of/base.c:476-484 (__of_device_is_available): a device tree node with no 'status' property is treated as available/enabled by default -- this is standard DT semantics, not a MiSTer-specific quirk. uart1 in vanilla 6.18's socfpga.dtsi has NO status property (confirmed by direct read of the file), and critically, neither did the FORK's own socfpga.dtsi at this commit's immediate parent (`git -C /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer show c4d12c768^:arch/arm/boot/dts/socfpga.dtsi` also shows no status on uart1). This means the commit's entire diff (`&uart1 { status = "okay"; };`) was already a no-op in the 5.15 fork at the time it was written -- uart1 was enabled by DT default both immediately before and immediately after this commit. Confirmed the mechanism works as claimed by checking vanilla's own &uart0 board-file override (socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts:87-95), which sets clock-frequency but deliberately never sets status, and is unambiguously the working boot console (stdout-path = 'serial0:115200n8', serial0 alias = &uart0) -- proof the omit-status-property-means-enabled path is real and load-bearing elsewhere in the same file. Our 0004 patch's &uart1 override likewise has no status property and uart1 is enabled in our build via the identical default mechanism, not because this commit's content was carried forward. Net effect: the disposition/carried_patch/carried_mode mapping is still defensible in the loose sense that 0004 does contain an &uart1 node and uart1 IS enabled in our final devicetree, matching the fork's outcome -- but the specific claim that dropping this commit would produce 'feature-loss' is not supported; dropping it changes nothing observable, in the fork or in our carry. The load-bearing UART1 change in this commit group is actually 7d2df2d2d's DMA-property deletion, a separate commit/record.

sonnet-verified: carry-mapping confirmed (0004 patch does contain an &uart1 override, satisfying the mechanical 'is this commit's target functionality present in 0004' question), Main_MiSTer citations confirmed exact, but impact/severity corrected from feature-loss to none per the DT-default-enabled analysis above.

sonnet-verified: CARRY-MAPPING CONFIRMED, IMPACT CORRECTED (feature-loss -> none, evidence-backed) -- disposition/carried_patch/carried_mode/Main_MiSTer citations all hold; effect_if_absent was overstated.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: i2c2 bus remains disabled; ADV7513 HDMI transmitter at address 0x39 cannot be probed; HDMI output silently fails to initialize with no error message

notes: CRITICAL: i2c2 enables the third and final i2c adapter required for Main_MiSTer HDMI support. The original commit adds &i2c2 { status="okay"; clock-frequency=<100000>; speed-mode=<0>; }. The carried patch 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch includes this node but DROPS the speed-mode property (line reads &i2c2 { clock-frequency=<100000>; status="okay"; }), a re-implementation choice because: (1) speed-mode is not a recognized i2c property in 6.18 i2c bindings (Documentation/devicetree/bindings/i2c/), (2) no i2c driver reads it, and (3) would be silently ignored anyway. File path changed during 6.18 forward-port from fork's old location arch/arm/boot/dts/socfpga_cyclone5_de10_nano.dts (no vendor subdir) to 6.18 canonical path arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts. Grouped with 13 other DTS commits in provenance.md (one row, SHA range aa8afe109..077c2c317), but this analysis is commit-specific per Phase 1 protocol. sonnet-verified: corrected — fork diff (+6 lines: &i2c2 { status="okay"; clock-frequency=<100000>; speed-mode=<0>; }) confirmed via `git show c5066763c`; carried patch 0004's &i2c2 node (clock-frequency=<100000>; status="okay";, speed-mode dropped) confirmed at line ~190-193 of 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch; grep of vanilla i2c bindings docs found zero occurrences of "speed-mode", supporting the drop rationale. One citation-precision fix: smbus.cpp:214-222 corrected to smbus.cpp:212-219 — i2c_open() actually starts at line 212 (not 214), the force_bus>2 guard is at line 216, and the return -1 is at line 219; the patch's own inline comment (0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch line ~157) independently cites "smbus.cpp:214" which is also imprecise (line 214 is `char str[16];`, not the guard) but within the same function body. video.cpp:1448 (i2c_open(0x39,...) call in hdmi_config_init()) confirmed exact.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3, row grouping c5066763c with 13 other DTS commits (aa8afe109..077c2c317)

author: Sorgelig

forward_port.effort: minimal — device-table macro renamed (USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_ELITE2_CONTROLLER→USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE, same numeric ID 0x0b22); BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2 unconditionally available in 6.18 input-event-codes.h so #ifndef guards removed

affected_hardware: Xbox Elite 2 Controller (Bluetooth connection only; wired/USB connection already gets native paddle support from vanilla xpad.c, VID:PID 045e:0b00)
impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox Elite 2 controller Bluetooth paddle buttons (BTN_GRIPL/L2/R/R2) become unavailable to userspace; generic HID reports them as unmapped consumer usages and trigger-scale switch

notes:
This commit adds MS_QUIRK_ELITE2_PADDLES handling to hid-microsoft driver for Xbox Elite 2 over Bluetooth (device ID 0x0b22, branded USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE in 6.18). The fork's original commit defined BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2 with #ifndef guards (now dead code in 6.18 where these are unconditional in input-event-codes.h). Paddle decoding: MS_XBE2_PADDLE_USAGE (HID_UP_CONSUMER | 0x0081) bitmask value → BIT(0)=BTN_GRIPR, BIT(1)=BTN_GRIPR2, BIT(2)=BTN_GRIPL, BIT(3)=BTN_GRIPL2. Trigger-scale usage (0x0099) is ignored entirely. Vanilla 6.18.38 lists the Elite 2 device but with only MS_QUIRK_FF (force feedback), no paddle handling — hence equivalence='contradicted' (patch adds functionality absent upstream). Carried patch verified to match fork commit (0016-hid-microsoft-elite2-paddles.patch line 1: 'From c784a6856d73d78cfd37e4bd0011b186523e31c5'). Forward-port is clean with device-table macro rename only. Failure mode is silent (paddles simply become unmapped HID usages if patch is dropped). NOTE: vanilla's xpad.c (drivers/input/joystick/xpad.c) already natively supports Xbox Elite Series 2 paddles for the WIRED USB connection (VID:PID 045e:0b00, MAP_PADDLES, identical BTN_GRIPR/R2/L/L2 codes and identical bit assignment for the '4.x firmware' packet format at xpad.c:1208-1212) -- this commit's value-add is specifically for the BLUETOOTH connection path (hid-microsoft.c, PID 0x0b22), which xpad.c does not cover. This nuance was previously undocumented and does not change disposition/severity but sharpens 'affected_hardware' to 'Bluetooth-connected Elite 2 only'.

sonnet-verified: (1) BIT-mapping re-derived and CONFIRMED byte-exact against `git show c784a6856d73d78cfd37e4bd0011b186523e31c5 -- drivers/hid/hid-microsoft.c`: BIT(2)=BTN_GRIPL, BIT(3)=BTN_GRIPL2, BIT(0)=BTN_GRIPR, BIT(1)=BTN_GRIPR2 -- matches. (2) Device ID 045E:0B22 confirmed (USB_VENDOR_ID_MICROSOFT=0x045e at hid-ids.h:984; USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE=0x0b22 at hid-ids.h:1013); vanilla_file_line hid-microsoft.c:458-459 spot-checked exact. (3) provenance_doc_ref was WRONG and has been corrected: patch-provenance.md line 1 is unrelated header prose, not a table row, and no row for this commit says 'landed (P1.9)' -- the actual row is line 364 (§3.4 Class D) and reads '(new)' only, unlike the NSO Famicom row immediately above it which does say '✅ landed (P1.9-esc)'. (4) userspace_coupling was WRONG and has been corrected to coupled=false: 'Main_MiSTer/MiSTer.ini:2177' does not exist -- MiSTer.ini is only 406 lines total; the real xbe2_shift comment is at line 386. 'Main_MiSTer/joymapping.cpp:map_paddle_btn()' is unrelated -- that function assigns a 'paddle' ROLE (an MSX/PC-Engine-style analog-paddle game input, tagged '|P' in a core's button-name string) to whichever physical button/axis a core requests, and has nothing to do with Xbox Elite 2's rear grip paddles; grep for GRIPL/GRIPR/0x224-0x227 across Main_MiSTer found ZERO hits in joymapping.cpp or anywhere else outside the ini comment. The only real related mechanism is Main_MiSTer's QUIRK_SHIFT/xbe2_shift chatpad-key-remap feature (input.cpp:5523 `if (input[n].vid==0x045e && input[n].pid==0x0b00) input[n].quirk=QUIRK_SHIFT;`, input.cpp:5810-5811), but it is gated on PID 0x0b00 -- the WIRED Elite 2, handled entirely by xpad.c, a different driver that this commit does not touch. Since this commit patches only the Bluetooth device-table entry (PID 0x0b22), xbe2_shift's QUIRK_SHIFT gate never fires for a Bluetooth-connected Elite 2, so there is no genuine Main_MiSTer-specific coupling to this specific commit; the paddle buttons are simply generic BTN_* codes mappable like any other button in the OSD (same pattern as gamecube-adapter and NSO-Famicom in this batch). Escalation-worthy per worker-instructions (asserted coupling without valid path:line citation) -- now corrected.
provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md:364, §3.4 Class D (row reads "(new)", not "landed" -- see sonnet-verified note)

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: MiSTer's frame reader (FPGA video output window at 0x22000000) would not be accessible as /dev/fb0; Main_MiSTer cannot configure video mode via /sys/module/MiSTer_fb/parameters/mode; video output fails silently with no error

license_provenance: GPL v2 (license header: 'This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms and conditions of the GNU General Public License, version 2')
notes: CARRIED PATCH — NOT UPSTREAM. MiSTer-specific driver for FPGA frame-reader (soft-IP module in DE10-Nano fabric). No mainline equivalent exists or would be accepted as-is (Sorgelig, patch header). Forward-ported from v5.15 to v6.18.38 by Michael C. Ferguson (2026-07-12). The patch includes necessary 6.18 API compatibility changes: (1) fb_read/fb_write/fb_mmap fallback removal (v6.8: 8813e86f6d82) resolved with __FB_DEFAULT_IOMEM_OPS_RDWR and __FB_DEFAULT_IOMEM_OPS_MMAP (iomem variants required — window has no struct pages); (2) FBINFO_FLAG_DEFAULT removal (v6.6: 0444fa357c16); (3) platform_driver::remove() void return (v6.11: 0edb555a65d1); (4) -Wmissing-prototypes enforcement (v6.8: 0fcb70851fbf); (5) memremap() error handling (returns NULL, not ERR_PTR). ABI STABLE: FBIO_WAITFORVSYNC is byte-identical across 5.15 and 6.18; ioctl contract cannot drift. Driver maps FPGA memory region (0x22000000, 8MB) with write-through caching; first 4KB holds pseudo-palette, pixels at offset 4096. Supports multiple color formats (1555, 565, 8-bit, 8888). Platform driver with device-tree binding; VSYNC interrupt (IRQ 40, GIC SPI) triggers frame_count increment and wakes interruptible waitqueue for ioctl. CONFIG_FB_MISTER=y in board/mister/de10nano/linux.config; CONFIG_FB_DEVICE=y (new 6.x) required for /dev/fb0 creation. CARRIES REGRESSED FUNCTIONALITY: Patch-provenance.md notes two forward-port hazards were found via testing (not predicted): platform_driver::remove() void, -Wmissing-prototypes. No issues found in this forward-port; applied cleanly. Main_MiSTer coupling: mode parameter is userspace ABI — shell scripts and video.cpp write it; removing or renaming would break video output. DTS node handled separately in 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch. sonnet-verified: corrected — carry-mapping, patch content, DTS reg/interrupts, CONFIG_FB_MISTER=y, and forward-port API-churn claims (fb_io_* ops, FBINFO_FLAG_DEFAULT removal, void remove(), -Wmissing-prototypes, memremap() NULL-check) all confirmed against the live checkouts; the userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref line number for the mode-param write was wrong (video.cpp:3881 is get_file_fromdir(), unrelated) and has been corrected to the actual call sites at video.cpp:3548 and video.cpp:4390.
subject: Implement MiSTer frame buffer device.

userspace_coupling.interface: /dev/fb0 (standard fbdev node), FBIO_WAITFORVSYNC ioctl (_IOW('F', 0x20, __u32), standard mainline ABI identical in 5.15 and 6.18), /sys/module/MiSTer_fb/parameters/mode (module_param_cb, mode 0664 RW, format: 'format rb width height stride')
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/video.cpp:3859,3867 (ioctl FBIO_WAITFORVSYNC in vs_wait()); video.cpp:3548 (fb_write_module_params(): fopen/fprintf to /sys/module/MiSTer_fb/parameters/mode) and video.cpp:4390 (sprintf+system("echo ... >/sys/module/MiSTer_fb/parameters/mode")) — corrected by sonnet-verified audit; the prior '~L3881+' citation pointed at get_file_fromdir(), an unrelated function

author: Martin Donlon

depends_on: af424a410749 usb: dwc2: alloc dma aligned buffer for isoc split in (vanilla 5.15+6.18)

impact.effect_if_absent: Split-IN transfers (interrupt, bulk, control) with unaligned wMaxPacketSize silently drop received data when the URB buffer is not DMA-aligned. Data corruption occurs without any error message or failure indication.

notes:
GENUINE BUG FIX, not a MiSTer quirk. Vanilla 6.18.38 contains only a partial fix: align_buf data copy happens exclusively in dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in() (for isoc transfers only). This commit moves the copy to dwc2_hc_xfercomp_intr() to apply to ALL split-IN transfers (interrupt, bulk, control). Problem: when wMaxPacket is not 4-byte-aligned (e.g., 2, 6), data from the DMA bounce buffer was lost for non-isoc endpoints. Martin Donlon's patch is a clean, well-reasoned fix that should be submitted upstream independently. Forward-port to 6.18 is mechanical—no API changes between 5.15 and 6.18 in dwc2_hc_xfercomp_intr() or dwc2_get_actual_xfer_length(). Patch is already carried as 0028 with full provenance header. SILENT FAILURE: loss of data from USB devices with non-standard wMaxPacketSize is not reported—applications would see corrupted transfers or stalled endpoints.

sonnet-verified: PASS (with one field fix). Re-ran `git show` on fork commit d7adb20b4 and read /mnt/source/linux/drivers/usb/dwc2/hcd_intr.c:900-952 (dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in) and :958+ (dwc2_hc_xfercomp_intr) directly -- vanilla 6.18.38 lines 922-928 are byte-identical to the record's quote, and `grep -n align_buf` over the whole file returns only those three lines, confirming the align_buf copy really is confined to the isoc-split-in path with no equivalent handling anywhere else in the file. Confirmed dependency SHA af424a410749ed7e0c2bffd3cedbc7c274d0ff6f is a real vanilla commit ('usb: dwc2: alloc dma aligned buffer for isoc split in', William Wu, 2018-05-11) via `git show` in /mnt/source/linux -- not a fabricated SHA. Carried patch 0028-dwc2-fix-unaligned-in-split.patch exists and matches the described forward-port exactly (same gating condition `chan->align_buf && chan->ep_is_in && qtd->complete_split`, same dwc2_get_actual_xfer_length() call). FIXED: verification.contradiction was set to true, but per the pilot lesson (worker-instructions.md) this flag means specifically 'an upstream-coverage claim turned out to not match vanilla' -- and no such claim exists here. patch-provenance.md:375 already correctly said 'not upstream' before this record was written (agrees_with_provenance_doc=true), so there was nothing to contradict. Sibling records with the same equivalence="contradicted" shape (2d39e76d1's mmc LED fix, 817ace70b's mt76 IDs) both correctly leave verification.contradiction=false; this record was the one outlier and has been normalized to match. equivalence="contradicted" (a property of the code comparison) is correctly retained since vanilla's partial fix genuinely differs from what this commit does.
provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md line 337: 'd7adb20b4 | dwc2: fix unaligned IN data'

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Any userspace application using USB devices with wMaxPacketSize not divisible by 4 (e.g., devices with 2-byte or 6-byte interrupt packets)

author: Sorgelig

affected_hardware: MiSTer DE10-Nano with optional M41T81 RTC add-on
impact.effect_if_absent: If this commit's RTC node were not carried at all, M41T81 RTC devices would not be recognized by the kernel (silent feature loss; only affects devices with the optional RTC add-on board). Our patch 0004 carries the functionality on the i2c_gpio bit-banged bus (not i2c1) using the exact upstream compatible string "st,m41t81", which binds via vanilla's primary of_match_device() exact-match path -- not merely via the legacy suffix-fallback that the fork's original 2017 strings also happened to satisfy (see notes).

notes:
SONNET-CORRECTED (was over-flagged in first pass): e40563ae196092ccaf4e4f24de8a35f08cf20e0f introduces compatible = "stm,m41t81" (typo'd vendor prefix "stm" for "st") on &i2c1/rtc_at_68. The very next fork commit, 2548c2978fa95c61d146d25f518124836100630c ('Support for i2c rtc mcp794xx.', same day, 2017-09-17 05:57:45 +0800, ~1h41m after this commit's 04:16:09), rewrote the string to bare "m41t81" (no vendor prefix at all) while adding a second RTC node (mcp7941x@0x6F). Bare "m41t81" is what the fork actually shipped from that point through the MiSTer-v5.15 tip (`git show MiSTer-v5.15:.../socfpga_cyclone5_de10_nano.dts` still reads compatible = "m41t81"; `git log --all -G'stm,m41t81'` shows the typo'd string exists in exactly ONE commit, live for under two hours). So the 'contradiction' flagged in the first pass was a same-day, 2017-era intermediate state that was never a shipped divergence.

More importantly, the first-pass claim that "stm,m41t81" would 'silently fail to bind, rendering the node inert' is independently WRONG on the merits, traced through the live i2c-core matching path in /mnt/source/linux (6.18.38):
  - i2c_device_match() (drivers/i2c/i2c-core-base.c:139-160) tries i2c_of_match_device(drv->of_match_table, client) as its FIRST (not last-resort) check.
  - i2c_of_match_device() (drivers/i2c/i2c-core-of.c:144-158): 'match = of_match_device(matches, &client->dev); if (match) return match; return i2c_of_match_device_sysfs(matches, client);' -- exact match first, then a suffix-fallback.
  - i2c_of_match_device_sysfs() (i2c-core-of.c:115-142): for each of_match_table entry, 'name = strchr(matches->compatible, ','); ... if (!name) name = matches->compatible; else name++; if (sysfs_streq(client->name, name)) return matches;' -- compares client->name against the PART OF THE DRIVER'S COMPATIBLE STRING AFTER ITS COMMA.
  - client->name for a DT-instantiated i2c child comes from of_i2c_get_board_info() -> of_alias_from_compatible() (drivers/of/base.c:1170-1181): 'p = strchr(compatible, \',\'); strscpy(alias, p ? p + 1 : compatible, len);' -- strips the NODE's own compatible string at its first comma.
  - For "stm,m41t81": client->name = "m41t81" (stripped after the comma). Vanilla's rtc-m41t80 of_match_table entry {.compatible = "st,m41t81", ...} (drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:103) has post-comma suffix "m41t81" too -> sysfs_streq matches -> BINDS. This happens inside i2c_of_match_device_sysfs, which is invoked from i2c_of_match_device, the FIRST check i2c_device_match makes -- not a legacy id_table fallback.
  - For bare "m41t81" (the 2017 fix, and the fork's shipped form): client->name = "m41t81" (whole string, no comma) -> identical sysfs_streq match against the same entry -> also BINDS.
So BOTH the typo'd and the fixed compatible strings bind to vanilla's driver via the exact same mechanism; there is no functional difference between them, and the RTC node was never actually going to go inert even during the two-hour window the typo existed. verification.contradiction is corrected to false and upstream.equivalence to "equivalent" (vanilla does the same thing the node needed, via a fallback the kernel itself documents as legacy: drivers/of/base.c:1165-1166, 'a relic from I2C and SPI... do not add any new user').

Our patch 0004 sidesteps the fallback path entirely by using the fully-correct "st,m41t81" (exact of_match_table string), which binds via the primary of_match_device() exact-compatible check -- more robust than either historical fork string, since it does not depend on the discouraged suffix-match mechanism at all.

Unrelated architectural point (unchanged from first pass, still correct): this commit's node lives on &i2c1 (the HPS I2C1 controller), which the fork itself later moved off of (commit 1337de1fd, 2018-02-10, 'Switch to i2c-gpio driver for smbus compatibility') onto the bit-banged i2c_gpio bus on portb 22/23, because Main_MiSTer only scans /dev/i2c-0..2 (smbus.cpp:214) and a fourth adapter would push the ADV7513 HDMI-transmitter probe off the end. Patch 0004 places all three candidate RTCs (pcf8563@0x51, m41t81@0x68, mcp7941x@0x6F) on i2c_gpio, not i2c1, matching the fork's own later correction and stock's shipped topology.

GROUPED ENTRY (unchanged): this commit is part of the 13-commit group (aa8afe109..077c2c317) all carried in patch 0004; correctly marked 'carry' in patch-provenance.md.

author: Michael Huang

impact.effect_if_absent: CPU frequency scaling (cpufreq) subsystem would be non-functional; users cannot scale CPU frequency from 400 MHz to 1.2 GHz; no build or boot error would result—the feature silently fails to register

license_provenance: GPL-2.0 (SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0 in socfpga-cpufreq.c); author: Michael Huang <coolbho3000@gmail.com>
notes: This commit (#35) is squashed together with commit #34 (Add cpufreq/overclock driver) in carried patch 0003. It improves the initial cpufreq driver by (1) removing the hardcoded 25 MHz OSC1 constant and reading it from the device tree via of_get_property() instead, and (2) adding PLL bypass/unbypass sequencing in set_dividers() for more stable clock transitions. The commit modifies calculate_vco_clock_hz() to use a static variable socfpga_cpufreq_osc1_hz instead of the hardcoded #define. At init time (socfpga_cpufreq_init()), it traverses /clkmgr@ffd04000/clocks/osc1 and reads the clock-frequency property from DT, converting it from big-endian. Verified against vanilla 6.18.38: no socfpga-cpufreq.c exists in vanilla (grep -rin 'socfpga|altera|altr' drivers/cpufreq/ returns no matches). Not upstream; never submitted to linux-pm. Forward-ported successfully from 5.15 to 6.18 as part of patch 0003. Known latent bug deliberately preserved for hardware safety: wait_for_fsm() calls wait_on_bit() with CLKMGR_STAT_BUSY (a mask, BIT(0)==1) where a bit *number* is expected, so it polls bit 1 instead of bit 0. This is intentional to avoid changing PLL transition timing without DE10-Nano hardware validation. In practice the bug is benign because PLL settling is covered by register-write latency. **CRITICAL: Silent failure mode.** Dropping this commit causes no build/boot error; cpufreq simply fails to register and users lose frequency-scaling capability. Userspace consumers: community overclock scripts on /media/fat that write to scaling_max_freq. Main_MiSTer itself does not reference cpufreq (verified in provenance-doc). Configuration: CONFIG_ARM_SOCFPGA_CPUFREQ=y in linux.config. Fully agrees with provenance-doc sections 719-874. sonnet-verified: corrected — OSC1-from-DT read (clkmgr@ffd04000/clocks/osc1, clock-frequency=<25000000>) confirmed against /mnt/source/linux/arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/socfpga_cyclone5.dtsi:13-18; wait_on_bit(word,bit,mode) signature (bit is a bit NUMBER, not a mask) confirmed against /mnt/source/linux/include/linux/wait_bit.h, so the CLKMGR_STAT_BUSY=BIT(0) latent-bug claim holds. Two corrections made: (1) carried_mode changed clean-apply -> re-implemented — the carried patch 0003 hardens this commit's own osc1-DT-lookup hunk with NULL-checked goto error paths (out_unmap/out_put_clocks/out_put_osc1, pr_err diagnostic) that do not exist in the 5.15 original, which just chained of_get_child_by_name()/of_get_property()/be32_to_cpup() with no NULL checks and would have oopsed on a missing property — not a byte-identical carry. (2) userspace_coupling.coupled changed true -> false to match record 3d72b9db7's own record and the patch 0003 header itself ("Main_MiSTer does NOT read cpufreq (verified: no 'cpufreq' and no 'scaling_' anywhere in its sources)"); the prior coupled=true asserted userspace coupling with no Main_MiSTer path:line citation, an escalation-triggering violation per the grounding contract. Standard cpufreq sysfs is real but its only consumers are external community overclock scripts, not Main_MiSTer or any cited userspace path.
subject: Improve clock transition stability and get OSC1 freq from DT (#35)

userspace_coupling.interface: standard /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu[01]/cpufreq/ sysfs (scaling_max_freq, scaling_cur_freq, cpuinfo_max_freq, etc.); consumers are community overclock scripts on /media/fat, not Main_MiSTer

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: USB HID devices with top-level usage HID_GD_GAMEPAD would not be covered by the hid_jspoll_interval module parameter override; they would use their hardware-default USB interrupt polling interval rather than the MiSTer-configured jspoll value.

notes:
Original fork commit (f0982bf2c) included 3 pr_info() debug statements that were dropped in the carried patch (0025-usbhid-jspoll-gamepad.patch). The carried patch keeps only the functional change: adding `case HID_GD_GAMEPAD:` to fall through to the same jspoll interval override as HID_GD_JOYSTICK. Vanilla 6.18.38 still lacks this case and thus does not apply jspoll overrides to gamepad devices. The feature is a straightforward enhancement: HID_GD_GAMEPAD has existed in the kernel since 2005 (include/linux/hid.h) and is used in other HID subsystems (hid-input.c), but was omitted from the usbhid jspoll override logic when that feature was added in 2017 (commit 933bfe4d271ef). The carried patch correctly forward-ports this fix from v5.15 to v6.18.38. No userspace coupling detected; this is a kernel-level USB polling optimization.

sonnet-verified: carry-mapping to 0025-usbhid-jspoll-gamepad.patch confirmed exact -- `git show f0982bf2c31d5d691492b80ad7e540f138944add` shows the fork's diff adds `case HID_GD_GAMEPAD:` plus 3 pr_info() lines; the carried patch's own header states the debug lines were deliberately dropped, and diffing the two confirms only the functional `case HID_GD_GAMEPAD:` fallthrough survives -- matches the record's claim exactly. Both cited SHAs independently re-derived and confirmed real via `git log` in /mnt/source/linux: 933bfe4d271ef5931bc7513a1239751ed251db04 ('HID: usbhid: extend polling interval configuration to joysticks', 2017-02-25) introduced the hid_jspoll_interval mechanism this commit extends; HID_GD_GAMEPAD (0x00010005) confirmed present in include/linux/hid.h since the original 2005 'Generic HID layer - code split' import. VOCABULARY FIX: upstream.equivalence was null despite a live vanilla-vs-fork comparison being made (vanilla's switch retains only HID_GD_JOYSTICK, confirmed at hid-core.c:1139-1142 exact match to the quoted hunk) -- corrected to 'contradicted' for consistency with the schema and with sibling records in this batch (e.g. c784a6856, the Elite2-paddles record) that use 'contradicted' for the same 'vanilla lacks this specific case entirely' pattern; disposition remains 'carried' with no misclassification (provenance doc never claimed upstream coverage for this specific gamepad case, so the contradicted/carried pairing is correctly grounded, not a forbidden contradicted+dropped-upstream pairing). Also added the previously-uncited vanilla_sha to upstream.vanilla_shas (933bfe4d271ef5931bc7513a1239751ed251db04) since it was quoted in prose but missing from the array, and tightened vanilla_file_line from the single line ':1140' to the full quoted range ':1139-1142' to match the four-line quote precisely. No device IDs apply (generic kernel-level fix, not gated to any VID/PID) -- empty device_ids/affected_hardware arrays are correct as-is.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: CORRECTED (sonnet verification, requested check on the boot-critical grade + fpga_io.cpp cite): the citation is accurate (do_bridge() exists at fpga_io.cpp:378-393 with the writes at the claimed lines) but the causal claim built on it does NOT hold up. Read drivers/fpga/altera-hps2fpga.c in full: alt_fpga_bridge_probe() (lines 127-192) only calls _alt_hps2fpga_enable_set() -- the function that actually calls reset_control_deassert() and writes the L3 remap register -- when the DT node has an explicit 'bridge-enable' property (line 164: `if (!of_property_read_u32(dev->of_node, "bridge-enable", &enable))`). Our 0004 patch's &fpga_bridge0/1/2 overrides set ONLY status="okay", no bridge-enable property, so even with the kernel driver probing successfully, it does NOT bring the bridges out of reset or touch the L3 remap register -- it only requests a reset_control handle, enables the l4_main_clk clock consumer, and registers a struct fpga_bridge with the generic /sys/class/fpga_bridge framework that nothing else in this system reads. Separately, and more decisively: Main_MiSTer's fpga_io.cpp:fpga_load_rbf() (the function that loads every FPGA core, including the very first menu.rbf at boot) calls do_bridge(0) before writing the bitstream and do_bridge(1) after, and do_bridge() operates via raw /dev/mem pointers (reset_regs = SOCFPGA_RSTMGR_ADDRESS, nic301_regs = SOCFPGA_L3REGS_ADDRESS, both mmap'd directly by Main_MiSTer at fpga_io.cpp:34,37) -- it writes reset_regs->brg_mod_reset and nic301_regs->remap itself, completely independent of the kernel's altera-hps2fpga platform driver or of_device_is_available() state for these nodes. Main_MiSTer never goes through the kernel's fpga_bridge/fpga-region subsystem at all. Net conclusion: whether fpga_bridge0/1/2 are status=okay or status=disabled in the DTS has no observable effect on Main_MiSTer's FPGA communication, because Main_MiSTer bypasses the kernel bridge driver's actual enable/disable logic entirely and re-derives the same register state itself on every core load.

notes:
The three FPGA bridges (lwhps2fpga/h2f/f2h, fpga_bridge0/1/2) match stock's DTB (docs/stock-inventory) and this commit's content is byte-for-byte carried into 0004 (clean-apply, verified via diff against the fork commit: identical &fpga_bridge0/1/2 { status = "okay"; }; stanzas). Vanilla 6.18 defines the bridges in socfpga.dtsi:525-548 but leaves them disabled (status='disabled') by default -- equivalence=contradicted and disposition=carried are both correct calls. ESCALATE: Commit is part of a GROUPED row in patch-provenance.md (13 commits squashed: aa8afe109...077c2c317).

sonnet-verified CORRECTION (was 'boot-critical' + userspace_coupling.coupled=true; requested check on this severity grade and the fpga_io.cpp cite): downgraded severity from boot-critical to cosmetic and userspace_coupling.coupled from true to false. The original notes claimed a causal chain -- 'this commit enables the bridges -> allows the kernel driver to probe -> Main_MiSTer depends on that' -- that does not survive reading the actual driver and Main_MiSTer source. (1) drivers/fpga/altera-hps2fpga.c's probe() only calls the function that actually deasserts reset / writes L3 remap when an explicit 'bridge-enable' DT property is present (line 164); our 0004 patch sets only status=okay, no bridge-enable property, so the kernel driver probing here does NOT actually bring the bridges out of reset regardless. (2) Main_MiSTer's do_bridge() (fpga_io.cpp:378-393), called from fpga_load_rbf() on every single core load including the initial menu.rbf (call sites at lines 436, 489, 497), writes reset_regs->brg_mod_reset and nic301_regs->remap directly through its own /dev/mem mapping (fpga_io.cpp:34,37: reset_regs/nic301_regs are raw pointers into FPGA_REG_BASE=0xFF000000, mmap'd by fpga_io_init(), nothing to do with the kernel's platform-device/driver-model bridge object). Main_MiSTer re-derives the correct bridge/reset state itself on every core load and never goes through the kernel's fpga_bridge or fpga-region subsystem. So whether this DTS content is present or absent has no observable effect on Main_MiSTer -- it was already self-sufficient. Downgraded to 'cosmetic' rather than 'none' because the change does still register a struct fpga_bridge with /sys/class/fpga_bridge/* (potentially used by unrelated tooling, e.g. manual `fpgautil` invocations or future kernel-side FPGA manager use) and it matches stock's DTB exactly, which has independent value for stock-parity auditing even though it is not load-bearing for the current Main_MiSTer codebase. carried_patch/carried_mode/equivalence/verification.contradiction all confirmed correct; only impact.severity and userspace_coupling were overstated.

sonnet-verified: CARRY-MAPPING CONFIRMED, SEVERITY + COUPLING CORRECTED (boot-critical -> cosmetic, coupled true -> false, evidence-backed) -- disposition/carried_patch/carried_mode/equivalence hold; the fpga_io.cpp cite was accurate but the causal dependency it was used to support was contradicted by reading do_bridge()'s /dev/mem implementation and altera-hps2fpga.c's probe() gating on 'bridge-enable'.

author: Sorgelig

forward_port.conflicts: Raw diff does not clean-apply: vanilla's `struct dualsense` gained `player_leds[5]`/`update_player_leds`/`player_leds_state` fields and kept the IDA-based `ps_device_set_player_id()` machinery (949aaccda019) that this fork commit deleted, then layered 8c0ab553b072's five-LED registration on top -- both of which this commit's target lines no longer resemble. Re-implementing requires either (a) kernel-side: add a 6th MiSTer-specific ':player_id' LED classdev alongside vanilla's 5, re-derived against current struct layout, or (b) userspace-side: teach Main_MiSTer's update_num_hw()/get_led_path() to resolve the gamepad input device name and drive the 5 vanilla '<input-dev>:white:player-N' LEDs directly.

impact.effect_if_absent: On vanilla 6.18.38, DualSense (054c:0ce6) exposes five separate LED classdevs named `<gamepad-input-dev>:white:player-N` (N=1-5), keyed off the gamepad's INPUT device name, instead of the fork's single `<hid-device-id>:player_id` brightness classdev (0-5) keyed off the HID device. Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2702 writes only `<led_path>:player_id/brightness` (led_path derived from the HID device id, matching the fork's naming exactly); that open fails with ENOENT on vanilla, so update_num_hw() silently falls into its pre-existing DualShock4 branch (input.cpp:2709-2726) and paints the lightbar's :red/:green/:blue sub-LEDs with the DS4 color_code[] per-player palette instead of the intended blue/green player_id-brightness cue. Simultaneously, vanilla's own driver auto-lights the 5-LED row once at connect via dualsense_set_player_leds(ds) (hid-playstation.c:1865) using an IDA connection-order id -- not a MiSTer-assigned player slot -- and nothing in Main_MiSTer ever touches those 5 LEDs afterward, so the row shows a static, assignment-independent pattern for the life of the connection. Net effect: player identity is still visually conveyed (lightbar color, same mechanism used for plain DualShock4), but the specific PS5-style white player-LED indicator the fork commit and Main_MiSTer were co-designed around is wrong/stale and unreachable from userspace.

SONNET ESCALATION VERIFICATION -- every link re-derived from scratch, all confirmed:

1. FORK (f84543926, `git show` in /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer): replaces the IDA-based `ps_device_set_player_id()`/`player_id` field with a single `struct led_classdev ds->led`, name `devm_kasprintf(dev, GFP_KERNEL, "%s:player_id", dev_name(dev))` where dev=&hdev->dev (the HID device, e.g. '0003:054C:0CE6.0001:player_id'), max_brightness=5, brightness_set_blocking=dualsense_player_led_brightness_set -> dualsense_set_player_leds(ds, brightness) (clamped >5 -> 0), registered in ds_leds_create() called from dualsense_create(). One writable knob: 0=off, 1-5=player LED pattern.

2. VANILLA (/mnt/source/linux checked out at v6.18.38, HEAD detached, confirmed clean via `git status`/`git log -1`): keeps the fork-deleted IDA machinery (949aaccda019723050a2cd98d7b4492b06423f27, 'HID: playstation: add DualSense player LED support', 2021-02-16) AND additionally registers 5 separate led_classdevs (8c0ab553b072025530308f74b2c0223ec50dffe5, 'HID: playstation: expose DualSense player LEDs through LED class', 2021-09-08) -- both full SHAs independently re-obtained via `git log --format=%H -1 <short-sha> -- drivers/hid/hid-playstation.c` in this task, matching what the prior record already had. Naming comes from ps_led_register() (hid-playstation.c:826-841): `"%s:%s:%s"` % (ps_dev->input_dev_name, color='white', LED_FUNCTION_PLAYERn ('player-1'..'player-5', dt-bindings/leds/common.h:70-74)). Critically, `input_dev_name` = dev_name(&ds->gamepad->dev) -- the GAMEPAD INPUT device's name, NOT the HID device's -- so even the parent-name component differs from what Main_MiSTer constructs. dualsense_create() (hid-playstation.c:1850-1865) registers all 5 LEDs AND unconditionally calls ps_device_set_player_id()+dualsense_set_player_leds(ds) at connect time, so vanilla DOES auto-light a player-number pattern by default with zero userspace interaction -- using an IDA connection-order id, not a MiSTer-assigned player slot.

3. USERSPACE (Main_MiSTer/input.cpp): get_led_path() (2642-2659) derives a base path ending in '.../<hid-device-id>/leds/<hid-device-id>' -- matches the FORK's naming scheme exactly (same hid-device-id used twice, no color/function suffix), confirming this commit and Main_MiSTer were co-designed. update_num_hw() (2692-2729) is shared by DS4 and DualSense (both funnel through QUIRK_DS4, assigned at input.cpp:5277-5279 for PID 0x05c4/0x09cc/0x0ba0/0x0ce6 under VID 0x054c). Line 2702: `set_led(led_path, ":player_id", (num > 5) ? 0 : num)`. If that fopen SUCCEEDS (true on the fork kernel) it takes the 'dualsense' branch (2705-2707) and sets a blue/green/red intensity pair on the existing lightbar LEDs. If it FAILS -- true on vanilla 6.18.38, since no LED named exactly '<hid-device-id>:player_id' exists there -- it silently falls into the pre-existing 'dualshock4' branch (2709-2726) and paints the lightbar with color_code[num] (a per-player RGB palette). No error surfaces; the fallback path already works fine (it's DS4's normal path), so nothing crashes or logs.

4. VERDICT: equivalence=contradicted stands -- vanilla implements player-LED indication via a genuinely different interface (5 named LEDs keyed off the gamepad input device, auto-lit at connect from IDA order) that Main_MiSTer cannot address with the single write it knows how to make. Real, silent, user-visible behavior change on DualSense under 6.18: (a) the white player-count LED row shows a static, MiSTer-assignment-independent number and is never updated by userspace; (b) the intended blue/green player_id-brightness cue is replaced by the DS4 RGB color-coding scheme. This is feature-loss/interface-drift, not a crash, and not 'no indication at all' (a substitute cue exists) -- so severity stays 'feature-loss' rather than escalating further, but it is emphatically NOT 'cosmetic-or-none': the specific interface Main_MiSTer targets is genuinely gone and the visible cue changes character.

5. DISPOSITION FIX (the core defect in the prior record): it already set equivalence='contradicted' and agrees_with_provenance_doc=false but left disposition='dropped-upstream' -- contradicting the grounding contract's own rule (worker-instructions.md item 2): a contradicted equivalence where the provenance doc claimed upstream-drop must be disposition='misclassified' (carry candidate), not 'dropped-upstream'. Corrected here. patch-provenance.md:337 (Class C table) batches f84543926/0d60c3482/60e08955f/b76b4bc6a together, citing 8c0ab553b072 + 'hid-playstation.c:217 update_player_leds' as justification, with only a hedge ('verify controller LED behaviour on HW, P3.13') -- no mention of the naming/interface incompatibility documented above.

6. Also fixed two independent errors inherited/introduced in the prior record: (a) vanilla_file_line cited 'hid-playstation.c:217', copied verbatim from the provenance doc without re-derivation -- line 217 is just the `bool update_player_leds;` struct-field declaration, not registration/naming logic (the real logic is at ps_led_register/player_leds_info/dualsense_create, cited above); this is itself a case of trusting prior art over grepping vanilla directly, which the grounding contract explicitly warns against. (b) device_ids was '054c:ce6e', a digit-transposed non-existent Sony USB PID -- corrected to '054c:0ce6' (USB_DEVICE_ID_SONY_PS5_CONTROLLER, /mnt/source/linux/drivers/hid/hid-ids.h:1280), matching the QUIRK_DS4 PID list at Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5277.

7. No existing carried_patch reimplements this -- grepped board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/*.patch; only 0022-hid-playstation-ds4-mac-fix.patch touches this file, for an unrelated MAC-address fix. The raw fork diff will not clean-apply to 6.18.38 (struct/IDA drift, see forward_port.conflicts). Two remediation paths for whoever picks up the carry candidate: kernel-side (add a 6th MiSTer-specific LED) or userspace-side (make Main_MiSTer drive vanilla's 5 LEDs, which requires resolving the gamepad input device name that get_led_path() doesn't currently compute). Recorded as effort=medium since either path is design work, not a mechanical patch reapply.

sonnet-verified: contradicted -- vanilla's DualSense player-LED interface (5 auto-lit LEDs keyed to the gamepad input device) is a genuinely different, incompatible interface from the fork's single hid-device-keyed :player_id brightness knob that Main_MiSTer specifically targets; dropping this commit is a real, silent, user-visible player-LED regression on DualSense, correctly a carry candidate (disposition=misclassified, not dropped-upstream) against the provenance doc's Class-C drop claim at patch-provenance.md:337. | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): carried as 0033 — single :player_id classdev (0-6) replaces vanilla's five auto-assigned player LEDs; IDA kept for DS4 teardown symmetry.
subject: dualsense: add player id led control.

upstream.vanilla_file_line: drivers/hid/hid-playstation.c:826-841 (ps_led_register name format), :1723-1733 (player_leds_info[] using LED_FUNCTION_PLAYER1..5), :1850-1865 (registration loop + auto-assign at connect); include/dt-bindings/leds/common.h:70-74 (LED_FUNCTION_PLAYERn strings)
upstream.vanilla_quote: ps_led_register() line 831-834: `led->name = devm_kasprintf(&ps_dev->hdev->dev, GFP_KERNEL, "%s:%s:%s", ps_dev->input_dev_name, led_info->color, led_info->name);` -- dualsense_create() line 1850-1865: `for (i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE(player_leds_info); i++) { ... ret = ps_led_register(ps_dev, &ds->player_leds[i], led_info); ... } ret = ps_device_set_player_id(ps_dev); ... /* Set player LEDs to our player id. */ dualsense_set_player_leds(ds);` -- dt-bindings/leds/common.h:70-74: `#define LED_FUNCTION_PLAYER1 "player-1"` ... `PLAYER5 "player-5"`. player_leds_info[] (1723-1733) pairs each with color="white", giving names like `<gamepad-input-dev>:white:player-1`.
