The fork's naming -- devm_kasprintf(dev, "%s:player_id", dev_name(dev)) with dev=&hdev->dev -- exactly matches how Main_MiSTer's get_led_path() constructs its base path (truncates the input device's sysfs path at '/input/', appends '/leds/<hid-device-id>'), i.e. this commit and Main_MiSTer were co-designed around the same naming convention.
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2642 get_led_path(), :2661 set_led(), :2692-2729 update_num_hw() -- line 2702 `set_led(led_path, ":player_id", (num > 5) ? 0 : num)`; on failure falls through to the :2709-2726 DualShock4 color_code[] fallback branch. QUIRK_DS4 (which routes DualSense through this code) is assigned at input.cpp:5277-5279 for VID 0x054c, PID in {0x05c4, 0x09cc, 0x0ba0, 0x0ce6}.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech K400r and K400 Plus keyboards default to Fn-locked mode, requiring users to hold Fn to access media keys (play, volume, etc). Main_MiSTer UI expects bare media keys for normal operation.

notes: GROUPED ENTRY: provenance.md lists fc8f3c2c6 and b745ce6d9 together in one row. This commit (fc8f3c2c) adds the Fn inversion logic (defines FEATURE_FN_INVERSION 0x40A0 and FEATURE_NEW_FN_INVERSION 0x40A2, hidpp_fn_set() helper, k400_enable_Fn() function). The related later commit b745ce6d9 (HGD73, 2021-12-24) adds the K400 Plus device ID 0x404D to the device table. The carried patch 0019-hidpp-k400-fn-inversion.patch combines both commits and has been forward-ported to 6.18.38 with modifications: (1) dedicated fn_feature_index field added to struct k400_private_data instead of reusing the shared feature_index; (2) hidpp_root_get_feature() signature changed from 4 args to 3 (dropped feature_type output); (3) k400_connect() signature changed from 2 args to 1 (dropped bool connected). Grep of vanilla 6.18.38: drivers/hid/hid-logitech-hidpp.c has no FEATURE_FN_INVERSION, no k400_enable_Fn(), confirms not upstream. Device IDs: 0x4024 (K400) is upstream (K400 support via tap-to-click feature was added by commit 90cdd986335eddb7cac6e456f7c48c1ce76fd095, verified: `git -C /mnt/source/linux show 90cdd986335e --stat` = 'HID: logitech-hidpp: add support to disable tap-to-click on the K400', Benjamin Tissoires, 2015-09-03); 0x404D (K400 Plus) is new from b745ce6d9. SILENT FAILURE MODE: dropping this commit silently leaves keyboards in default Fn-locked state, breaking media key access without user-visible error. sonnet-verify fix: original main_mister_ref only quoted the patch's own commit message ('Main_MiSTer's UI expects bare media keys'), which is circular, not a Main_MiSTer citation. Verified independently: Main_MiSTer/user_io.cpp:4201-4213 (user_io_kbd()) dispatches raw KEY_MUTE/KEY_VOLUMEDOWN/KEY_VOLUMEUP events straight to set_volume(), gated only on hasAPI1_5() — confirming the coupling claim is substantively true, just previously uncited.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.4, line: `fc8f3c2c6`, `b745ce6d9` | Logitech K400r / K400 Plus: disable Fn swap | ... | `0019-hidpp-k400-fn-inversion.patch`
sonnet-verified: carry-mapping into 0019 confirmed exact (both fc8f3c2c6 and b745ce6d9 hunks present, header documents both origin SHAs); main_mister_ref was a self-referential citation (quoted the patch header, not Main_MiSTer) — amended to user_io.cpp:4201-4213, verified accurate
subject: Logitech K400r: disable Fn swap.

userspace_coupling.interface: HID media key events (KEY_MUTE, KEY_VOLUMEDOWN, KEY_VOLUMEUP)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/user_io.cpp:4201-4213 (user_io_kbd(): dispatches KEY_MUTE/KEY_VOLUMEDOWN/KEY_VOLUMEUP directly to set_volume() when hasAPI1_5()) — the patch header's 'bare media keys' claim was not previously backed by an actual Main_MiSTer citation; verified during sonnet review

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: initramfs /init boot flow with loop= parameter parsing (PLAN §5)

forward_port.conflicts: init/do_mounts.c and drivers/block/loop.c both diverged substantially between 5.15 and 6.18 (sys_ioctl()/sys_close() removed from init code; no include/linux/loop.h or drivers/block/loop.h to declare a prototype against; mount_block_root() is now a parameter-taking helper, not the file-global-driven function the 5.15 patch edited).
forward_port.effort: high; compile-tested only (ARCH=arm vmlinux links warning-free with the board config, both CONFIG_BLK_DEV_LOOP=y and =m), not boot-tested. See the patch's own commit message for the point-by-point rationale.

impact.effect_if_absent: In 5.15 fork: the loop= kernel parameter would not be recognized, /dev/loop8 would not be created, and loop-mounted root images would not boot. In 6.18 forward port: functionality is provided by initramfs instead; effect is silent—users would not see an error if they passed loop= to a kernel without this patch.

notes:
Class B commit in patch-provenance.md. NOT applied to Buildroot_MiSTer's own image -- functionality is replaced there by an initramfs-based boot flow per PLAN §5 and patch-provenance.md §4 analysis (that half of this record is unchanged since the original 2026-07-15 triage). No userspace coupling confirmed via Main_MiSTer audit (no references to /root2, /dev/loop8, or LOOP_SET_FD ioctl). sonnet-verified: correct — diff re-confirmed against the fork commit exactly (loop_max_part() export in drivers/block/loop.c, loop= __setup + m_open/loop_setup + mount_root() rewrite in init/do_mounts.c); patch-provenance.md §4's 11-point line-by-line coverage table substantiates the '100% of the loop= patch's own logic' claim; grep for root2/loop8/LOOP_SET_FD in Main_MiSTer returns zero hits, confirming no userspace coupling.

UPDATED 2026-07-18: the exported Linux-Kernel_MiSTer tree (scripts/export-kernel-tree.sh, PR #75) is upstream's kernel for every stock MiSTer, not just the one this repo builds, and every stock MiSTer boots through the loop= mechanism this commit adds -- so the exported tree must keep carrying it even though our own image does not. Forward-ported to 6.18 and carried at board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches-upstream/0100-init-support-for-init-loop-device.patch: applied ONLY by scripts/export-kernel-tree.sh, never by Buildroot (BR2_LINUX_KERNEL_PATCH points at linux-patches/ only). Disposition changed from dropped-deliberate to carried-upstream-only to reflect this -- it was never accurate to call this commit's functionality 'dropped' once we started publishing it upstream; it is carried, just not into our image. See linux-patches-upstream/README.md for the directory's rules and docs/kernel-recon/reconciliation.md's disposition legend for the new value.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md:326 — Class B: loop= root patch, disposition 'delete — replaced by initramfs (§5)'
subject: Support for init loop device.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: fc97b4d6a1a6 (HID: playstation: expose DualSense lightbar through a multi-color LED, 2021-09-08, upstream in v5.16)

forward_port.conflicts: Do not carry this commit forward. Vanilla 6.18.38 already ships a complete, standard-API lightbar implementation (led_classdev_mc, drivers/hid/hid-playstation.c:200/862-899/1197-1211/1841-1848) that supersedes it. Our own board/mister/de10nano/linux.config already sets CONFIG_LEDS_CLASS_MULTICOLOR=y (line 387), so the kconfig prerequisite for vanilla's approach is already satisfied on our side — no config gap to close. (Note: stock's config, docs/stock-inventory/stock-linux.config:2969, has `# CONFIG_LEDS_CLASS_MULTICOLOR is not set`, i.e. stock's older kernel predates/omits this — irrelevant to us since we already carry it.)
forward_port.effort: none — take vanilla's file as-is; nothing to port, no interface shim needed since no consumer targets the fork's :red/:green/:blue paths

impact.effect_if_absent: No functional loss: dropping this fork commit and taking vanilla 6.18.38 as-is gives DualSense lightbar RGB control back, just through a different sysfs shape. Fork: three independent led_classdev nodes /sys/class/leds/<hid-devname>:red/, :green/, :blue/ (each plain 0-255 brightness, no color semantics). Vanilla: one led_classdev_mc node /sys/class/leds/<input-devname>:rgb:indicator/ using the standard multicolor LED ABI (multi_intensity for the R:G:B ratio, brightness for overall level). Userspace that wants to set a lightbar color must target a different set of sysfs paths, but the capability itself (arbitrary RGB lightbar color from userspace) is fully preserved and the default color on boot (blue, 0,0,128) is identical in both.

notes: PROVENANCE DOC IS WRONG for this row. Verbatim, patch-provenance.md:337: '| `f84543926`, `0d60c3482`, `60e08955f`, `b76b4bc6a` | DualSense player LEDs / lightbar / mute / player-6 | **`8c0ab553b072`** *HID: playstation: expose DualSense player LEDs through LED class* (2021-09-08); **`8e5198a12d64`** *…add initial DualSense lightbar support* (2021-02-16) | 6.18 `hid-playstation.c:217` `update_player_leds`, `:155` lightbar flag. MiSTer's were pre-upstream backports. ⚠ verify controller LED behaviour on HW (P3.13) |'. Two errors: (1) it GROUPS four textually-and-functionally distinct fork commits (player-id LED, lightbar RGB, mute button/LED, player-6 LED config) into one row cited against only two upstream SHAs — this is exactly the grouping failure mode this recon project exists to eliminate; each of the four needs its own record (0d60c3482 is this one). (2) For 0d60c3482 specifically, neither cited SHA is the actual match. `8e5198a12d64` (2021-02-16, confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`) only sets a hardcoded default lightbar color at probe time — it provides NO userspace-adjustable control, so it cannot be what 0d60c3482 (July 2021, adds a full 3-LED userspace-writable interface) 'backports'. The commit that actually gives userspace lightbar control is `fc97b4d6a1a6` (2021-09-08, also confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38), which lands upstream TWO MONTHS AFTER the fork commit and uses a materially different mechanism (one led_classdev_mc vs. three led_classdev). So 0d60c3482 is not a backport of anything — it is Sorgelig's own independent pre-upstream implementation of the same feature, which vanilla then solved differently and later. Given (a) vanilla provides the same user-facing capability (arbitrary RGB lightbar color from userspace, same blue default), (b) via a different but not inferior interface (led_classdev_mc is the standard kernel ABI for multicolor LEDs, arguably cleaner than three independent classdevs), and (c) confirmed zero Main_MiSTer coupling to the fork's :red/:green/:blue paths — this is graded `dropped-upstream`/`superseded-better`, not `misclassified`. Reserving `misclassified` for cases where the doc's error conceals a genuine regression (e.g. the DualSense mute→BTN_Z commit 60e08955f, part of this same grouped row, which IS a real uncarried regression per prior analysis) keeps that flag meaningful; using it here for a same-capability interface swap would dilute it. sonnet-verified: dropped-upstream/superseded-better, doc disagreement confirmed and re-derived independently — vanilla_shas corrected to fc97b4d6a1a6 only (8e5198a12d64 removed, it is not the matching commit), device_ids corrected to 054C:0CE6/054C:0DF2 (prior record had non-existent IDs 054C:CE6E/054C:DF11), fork LED registration and vanilla multicolor registration both quoted with file:line, Main_MiSTer grep re-run and confirmed zero coupling.
subject: dualsense: add lightbar color control.

upstream.vanilla_quote: L200: `struct led_classdev_mc lightbar;` — one multicolor LED classdev, not three separate ones. L862-899 `ps_lightbar_register()`: allocates a 3-entry `mc_subled` array (`LED_COLOR_ID_RED/GREEN/BLUE`), sets `lightbar_mc_dev->subled_info`/`num_colors = 3`, names the single node `led_cdev->name = devm_kasprintf(&hdev->dev, GFP_KERNEL, "%s:rgb:indicator", ps_dev->input_dev_name)`, and registers it with `devm_led_classdev_multicolor_register(&hdev->dev, lightbar_mc_dev)`. L1197-1211 `dualsense_lightbar_set_brightness()`: calls `led_mc_calc_color_components(mc_cdev, brightness)` then reads `mc_cdev->subled_info[0/1/2].brightness` for red/green/blue and forwards to `dualsense_set_lightbar()`. L1841-1848 in `dualsense_create()`: `ret = ps_lightbar_register(ps_dev, &ds->lightbar, dualsense_lightbar_set_brightness);` followed by `dualsense_set_lightbar(ds, 0, 0, 128); /* blue */` — vanilla's out-of-the-box default color is blue, identical to the fork's default.

userspace_coupling.interface: LED sysfs. Fork exposes three separate classdevs (…:red, …:green, …:blue); vanilla exposes one multicolor classdev (…:rgb:indicator). Confirmed by exhaustive grep of /mnt/source/Main_MiSTer for lightbar/RGB-LED/multicolor/dualsense sysfs usage: the ONLY /sys/class/leds/ reference in the entire tree is `input.cpp:4052: #define LED_MONITOR "/sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed"`, which is the unrelated HPS status LED (board LED, not a controller lightbar). Main_MiSTer never reads or writes any DualSense/dualshock/lightbar LED path in either the fork's or vanilla's naming scheme.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: 74cb485f68eb (upstream HID: playstation: sanity check DualShock4 calibration data)

affected_hardware: Clone DualShock4 devices with invalid calibration
impact.effect_if_absent: Kernel oops (division by zero) during DualShock4 motion sensor report parsing when device reports invalid (zero) calibration denominators. Affects clone/defective DualShock4 controllers that don't properly populate calibration data.

notes:
Fork's 5.15 fix was a runtime guard: `calib->sens_denom ? mult_frac(...) : 0` applied during dualshock4_parse_report() (confirmed via `git show 1412bd7075d24db01785f0749f8efe498f24181d` in the fork repo: hid-sony.c, +2/-2). Upstream 6.18 fix (74cb485f68eb3dff6802ee446a602607b03f4fc9, committed Jan 5 2023 by Roderick Colenbrander, confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38) is more comprehensive: checks ALL calibration data (gyro+accel) at initialization time (dualshock4_get_calibration_data()) and replaces zero denominators with safe defaults (S16_MAX), effectively disabling calibration for that axis. Upstream fix is superior because: (1) catches problem at init rather than per-report, (2) applies to both gyro and accelerometer, (3) substitutes safe value instead of just guarding division. Original commit targets hid-sony.c's dualshock4_parse_report(); DS4 support was moved to hid-playstation.c in upstream commit 4f1f391869eecb11d57e9e07ddec143ff3dc08ee ('HID: sony: remove DualShock4 support', 2023-01-18, confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38).

sonnet-verified correction: the original record's vanilla_file_line (hid-playstation.c:1831-1847, 1868-1884) was derived from the COMMIT DIFF's hunk line numbers rather than re-grepped against the live /mnt/source/linux checkout, and pointed at the WRONG function. Lines ~1083-1128 in the current checkout are inside `dualsense_get_calibration_data()` (the PS5 DualSense controller -- confirmed via `ds->gyro_calib_data`/`DS_GYRO_RANGE` naming, a near-identical sanity check added to that function by a LATER, separate upstream commit not among the cited SHAs). The actual DualShock4 fix from 74cb485f68eb lives at hid-playstation.c:2053-2085 inside `dualshock4_get_calibration_data()` (confirmed via `ds4->gyro_calib_data`/`DS4_GYRO_RANGE` naming and function-boundary grep). Quote text/content was accurate; only the cited line numbers/function attribution were wrong. Corrected.

sonnet-verified correction: `agrees_with_provenance_doc` was flipped from false to true. patch-provenance.md line 369 IS stale (reflects the pre-P1.9 5.15-fork-era triage, as the original record noted), but the doc's own later, dated 'New findings during the port' section (lines 1000-1034) and its patch-status table (line 1153) already contain the exact same conclusion this record reaches -- superseded upstream by init-time sens_denom guards, div-by-0 half dropped, not carried. Since the doc's current/authoritative content agrees with this record, agrees_with_provenance_doc should be true, with a note that the specific old row is superseded within the same document (not that the doc disagrees).

sonnet-verified: corrected
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md line 369 is a STALE pre-P1.9 triage row (`1412bd707`, `5c410e935` | ... | 0022-hid-sony-fixes.patch) predating the P1.9 forward-port. The doc's CURRENT/authoritative content -- lines 1025-1027 ('1412bd707 (hid-sony div-by-0, folded into old 0022) is superseded upstream ... a better one ... init-time guard with a safe fallback ... Dropped.') and line 1153 ('div-by-0 half dropped (already superseded upstream by init-time sens_denom guards)') -- already states exactly this record's conclusion within the SAME document, dated 2026-07-12. Read as a whole (not just the stale line-369 row), the doc agrees with this record.
subject: hid-sony: fix divide by 0 exception.

upstream.vanilla_quote: Sanity check gyro calibration data. This is needed to prevent crashes during report handling of virtual, clone or broken devices not implementing calibration data properly. for (i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE(ds4->gyro_calib_data); i++) { if (ds4->gyro_calib_data[i].sens_denom == 0) { hid_warn(...); ds4->gyro_calib_data[i].sens_denom = S16_MAX; }}

userspace_coupling.interface: Motion sensor axes (ABS_RX, ABS_RY, ABS_RZ) provided by kernel at report-parse time; userspace (Main_MiSTer) does not depend on specific calibration implementation
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: /mnt/source/Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2697 (DS4 quirk detection), but no direct coupling to motion calibration

author: Shig

superseded_by: 94f18bb19945 (HID: nintendo: add support for nso controllers, 2023-12-04, vanilla 6.18+)

impact.effect_if_absent: NSO N64 controller (057e:2019) over USB or Bluetooth would not be recognized and would fail to register as an input device, resulting in complete feature loss for this hardware

notes: This commit adds NSO N64 controller support to the fork ahead of vanilla (Sept 2 vs Dec 4, 2023). The fork's implementation uses JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_N64=0x0D; vanilla later uses 0x0C (confirmed by direct diff of both commits: fork's 2799f8b9 sets `JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_N64 = 0x0D`, vanilla's enum has `JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_GEN = 0x0D, JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_N64 = 0x0C`). The fork's later commit b00a72159 (Mega Drive controller, Sept 4) corrected the N64 type to 0x0C and renamed the fork's own 0x0D to MD, matching vanilla's eventual pattern -- confirmed via `git show b00a72159 -- drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c`. Vanilla's 94f18bb19945 is substantially more comprehensive (649 insertions/266 deletions per `git show --stat`, vs this commit's 112/28), introducing a cleaner 'struct joycon_ctlr_button_mapping' design and support for NES/SNES/Genesis controllers in a single refactor. Vanilla's implementation is the canonical upstream version present in 6.18.38. This fork commit is superseded and should be dropped; the N64 feature is fully covered by vanilla. Device ID (057e:2019, confirmed at hid-ids.h:1068) and core functionality (button mapping, dpad, analog stick support) are identical. No userspace coupling detected in Main_MiSTer (grepped input.cpp for N64CON/joycon/0x2019-adjacent references: only generic JOYCONL/JOYCONR pairing logic at input.cpp:4689-4774 and 5845, which predates/is unrelated to this commit). Failure mode is silent: the device simply would not appear as an input device if dropped (since vanilla 6.18.38 provides the support). CORRECTION to prior pass: the vanilla_file_line citation for hid-ids.h was wrong (claimed :989-991, which lands in an unrelated Microsoft-mouse ID block; N64CON is actually at :1068). hid-nintendo.c citation widened to :314-323 to cover the full enum the quote reproduces (prior :323-325 only captured the N64 line and two lines past the enum's closing brace).
sonnet-verified: corrected -- SHA/ancestry, N64=0x0D fork vs 0x0C vanilla claim, b00a72159 correction, 649-insertion count, and no-userspace-coupling all confirmed accurate; the hid-ids.h file:line citation was wrong (:989-991 lands in an unrelated Microsoft ID block) and has been fixed to :1068.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: None (functional) / cosmetic (metadata) — MODULE_FIRMWARE(_firmware) expands to MODULE_INFO(firmware, _firmware) (/mnt/source/linux/include/linux/module.h:305), which only embeds a modinfo tag readable via `modinfo`/depmod/initramfs-building tools (dracut, mkinitramfs); it is NOT consulted by the runtime firmware loader. The kernel's actual request_firmware() call (drivers/bluetooth/btrtl.c:883, `request_firmware(&fw, name, &hdev->dev)`) resolves the RTL8761B firmware by the `.fw_name`/`.cfg_name` strings hardcoded in the `ic_id_table[]` id_table entry, which the fork already had BEFORE this commit (`.fw_name = "rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin"`, present since the v5.13.12 base-import commit e12ed6c19, confirmed via `git log -S` in Linux-Kernel_MiSTer). So RTL8761B firmware loading works identically with or without these two MODULE_FIRMWARE lines. MiSTer's target build has no initramfs (BR2_TARGET_ROOTFS_EXT2 direct ext4 rootfs, not cpio-based) and Buildroot's linux-firmware package does not derive its file list from kernel-module modinfo — firmware inclusion is driven entirely by explicit BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_* options in the defconfig. Confirmed our built image already ships rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin, rtl_bt/rtl8761b_config.bin (and the rtl8761bu variants) directly at output/target/usr/lib/firmware/rtl_bt/, via BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_RTL_87XX_BT=y (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:651), independent of any MODULE_FIRMWARE declaration. Net effect of dropping this commit: `modinfo btrtl` would omit two lines; zero effect on device function.

notes: MiSTer v5.13.12 backport (Aug 2021) of functionality later added to vanilla upstream (May 2023, bb23f07cb639). Not carried forward to MiSTer-v5.15 branch. Upstream patch is more comprehensive (also adds rtl8761bu, rtl8723d, rtl8723cs_vf, rtl8821c, rtl8821cs, rtl8822cs, rtl8822cu, rtl8851bu variants), but the rtl8761b_fw.bin and rtl8761b_config.bin declaration lines this fork commit added are byte-identical to bb23f07cb639's hunk (verified: `git show bb23f07cb63975968bbabe314486e2b087234fc5 -- drivers/bluetooth/btrtl.c` in /mnt/source/linux; SHA confirmed to exist and is an ancestor of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`). Line numbers re-verified against current HEAD checkout: both fork and vanilla now sit at btrtl.c:1523-1524. Commit existed only on old v5.13 branch, not on stock v5.15 branch, so was not carried forward during version bump. CORRECTION (sonnet re-verification): the original record's impact claim ("devices won't function without the declarations") was WRONG. MODULE_FIRMWARE() is a MODULE_INFO() modinfo-only macro (include/linux/module.h:305) with no effect on the request_firmware() runtime path — the actual firmware filename used at runtime comes from the `.fw_name`/`.cfg_name` fields in btrtl.c's id_table, which predate this commit. Verified our build ships the rtl8761b firmware files directly (output/target/usr/lib/firmware/rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin, rtl8761b_config.bin exist, dated Oct 10 2025, via BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_RTL_87XX_BT). Severity downgraded feature-loss -> cosmetic and effect_if_absent rewritten to reflect that this is a metadata-only gap (modinfo output), not a functional regression. Equivalence upgraded partial -> equivalent since the specific 2-line delta this commit introduces is byte-identical upstream (upstream's commit is broader in scope, covering unrelated chips, but that doesn't diminish equivalence of what THIS commit changed). Disposition (dropped-upstream) and vanilla_shas/vanilla_quote/vanilla_file_line were already correct and unchanged. sonnet-verified: corrected — impact/severity was overstated (MODULE_FIRMWARE is modinfo metadata, not required for request_firmware() at runtime; our .fw_name id_table entry and shipped firmware files predate/are independent of this commit), downgraded feature-loss -> cosmetic; equivalence sharpened partial -> equivalent; SHA/quote/line-number grounding re-confirmed against live /mnt/source/linux checkout.
subject: btrtl: add RTL8761B ROMs to list.

+MODULE_FIRMWARE("rtl_bt/rtl8761b_config.bin");

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: f7cbce60a38a (Bluetooth: hci_sync: Fix UAF on create_le_conn_complete)
881559af5f5c (Bluetooth: hci_sync: Attempt to dequeue connection attempt)

impact.effect_if_absent: NULL pointer dereference in hci_connect_le_scan_cleanup() when a LE connection attempt fails and races with connection cleanup. Kernel crash / NULL deref in older kernels (pre-6.8).

notes:
Sorgelig's patch adds a simple NULL check: `if(conn) hci_connect_le_scan_cleanup(conn);` to guard against NULL returned by hci_lookup_le_connect() (confirmed via `git show 552f9f197121f0c73b3e2af3b091043684dce5f7` in fork repo: net/bluetooth/hci_conn.c, +1/-1, old function signature `create_le_conn_complete(struct hci_dev *hdev, u8 status, u16 opcode)`). Vanilla 6.18.38 fixes this through: (1) architectural refactoring moving create_le_conn_complete() from hci_conn.c to hci_sync.c with different signature `(struct hci_dev *hdev, void *data, int err)`; (2) conn now passed as parameter via data, never looked up; (3) hci_conn_valid() check added at hci_sync.c:6953 after hci_dev_lock() acquisition (commit f7cbce60a38a). This upstream fix is SUPERIOR to Sorgelig's NULL guard because hci_conn_valid() validates the connection object is still alive in the hash, not just non-NULL — protecting against use-after-free, not just NULL deref. The vulnerable code path is structurally absent from 6.18.38 (`grep create_le_conn_complete net/bluetooth/hci_conn.c` returns nothing). Provenance doc (line 346) already correctly categorizes as 'patch site gone; NULL path structurally absent', exact match including the hci_sync.c:6942 line citation for the function definition.

sonnet-verified: independently re-ran every grounding step given this record's dropped-upstream disposition did not self-escalate per the worker's own escalation rule (any dropped-upstream disposition should set ESCALATE:yes) -- treated with extra suspicion accordingly. Re-confirmed both vanilla_shas (f7cbce60a38a, 881559af5f5c) exist and are ancestors of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`. Re-derived hci_sync.c:6942 (`static void create_le_conn_complete(struct hci_dev *hdev, void *data, int err)`) and hci_sync.c:6953 (`if (!hci_conn_valid(hdev, conn)) goto done;`) by direct line count against the live checkout -- both exact. Read f7cbce60a38a's own diff: it adds precisely the `hci_conn_valid` check at this location and its commit message tags `Fixes: 881559af5f5c ("Bluetooth: hci_sync: Attempt to dequeue connection attempt")`, substantiating both cited SHAs and the refactor narrative. Confirmed `create_le_conn_complete` is absent from hci_conn.c in vanilla (structural absence, not just a rewritten guard). No factual errors found in the refactor claim; content is sound despite the missing self-escalation. The missing ESCALATE:yes is a process-compliance gap in the original worker's final message, not a defect in the record's content.

sonnet-verified: confirmed

author: Sorgelig

forward_port.conflicts: Not a conflict: vanilla 6.18.38's plausibility check (50503e360eeb, 'HID: nintendo: check analog user calibration for plausibility', Sep 21 2022) is mathematically a strict superset of the fork's check. Fork triggers fallback only when max==min on an axis; vanilla triggers whenever NOT(min<center<max). If max==min=v, then min<center<max reduces to v<center<v, which is impossible for any center, so vanilla's negated condition always fires too -- every case the fork's check caught, vanilla also catches, plus additional out-of-order cases (e.g. min>=center with max!=min) the fork's narrower check missed. Confirmed for the specific all-zero clone case (min=center=max=0): 0>=0 trips vanilla's first clause immediately. Per-stick reset granularity also matches: both trees reset both X and Y axes of a stick together whenever either axis is flagged bad (fork: drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c:846-858 in /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer; vanilla: joycon_use_default_calibration() called once per stick from the same ret check). Only the fallback default magnitudes differ (see impact.effect_if_absent).

impact.effect_if_absent: None for the crash/misbehavior case the fork commit targeted: vanilla 6.18.38's ordering check (min<center<max) is a strict superset of the fork's max==min check (proof in notes) and DOES catch the all-zero/empty-calibration clone case, plus a wider class of implausible calibration data the fork's narrower check missed. The only residual difference is the numeric fallback defaults used once a stick is deemed invalid: MiSTer's fork uses center=2048/max=3595 (min=500, unchanged); vanilla uses the original center=2000/max=3500/min=500 (grep-confirmed unrevised since introduction). Because joycon_map_stick_val() normalizes raw ADC readings into a fixed +-32767 logical range using these constants only on the fallback path, a device whose true rest position sits near the 12-bit ADC midpoint (2048) would read roughly a 3% off-center offset under vanilla's defaults versus ~0% under MiSTer's — but this only ever applies to devices that already have failed/implausible calibration (broken clones, or SPI-flash read failures), never to genuine Nintendo hardware with valid factory/user calibration.

notes:
CRITICAL ESCALATION ISSUE (from prior pass, still valid as context): This commit is listed in patch-provenance.md line 334 as part of a GROUPED ROW (4 commits: c4ec5cb40, 9bdab534b, 60821059c, 45283785a) labeled 'Switch Pro/Joy-Con backport + fixes' citing upstream commit 2af16c1f846b ('HID: nintendo: add nintendo switch controller driver', 2021-09-11, v5.16) -- that citation only covers the BASE driver addition, not this specific empty-calibration fix; the doc's specific claim for this sub-commit is imprecise. Re-derived independently: this fork commit predates the upstream driver's initial mainline addition (2021-08-04 fork vs 2021-09-11 mainline), confirming it is a fix to the fork's own 5.15 backport, not a fix to the upstream driver. The correct upstream analogue is 50503e360eeb (Sep 21 2022), found via `git log -- drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c` in /mnt/source/linux and confirmed by its commit message, which explicitly describes the identical bug class this fork commit fixes: 'Cheap clone controllers may (falsely) report as having a user calibration... The reported user calibration however is min = center = max = 0' -- word-for-word the same failure mode MiSTer's commit message targets, and the vanilla commit additionally cites a real-world divide-by-zero kernel crash (GuliKit KingKong 2 controller) that this exact bug class caused.

MATHEMATICAL PROOF OF SUPERSET (resolving the prior 'contradicted' grading): fork's trigger condition per stick is `x.max==x.min || y.max==y.min`. Vanilla's trigger condition per stick is `NOT(x.min<x.center<x.max) || NOT(y.min<y.center<y.max)`, i.e. `(x.min>=x.center || x.center>=x.max) || (y.min>=y.center || y.center>=y.max)`. Claim: fork-triggers implies vanilla-triggers. Proof: suppose x.max==x.min==v. For vanilla to NOT trigger on x, we'd need x.min<x.center AND x.center<x.max, i.e. v<x.center AND x.center<v -- these two inequalities are mutually exclusive for any real x.center, so vanilla's x-clause must trigger whenever x.max==x.min. Symmetric argument for y. Therefore fork's trigger set is a strict subset of vanilla's trigger set: every case the fork caught (including the all-zero clone case: 0>=0 trivially trips vanilla's min>=center clause), vanilla also catches, PLUS additional implausible-ordering cases (e.g. min>=center with max!=min, or reversed center/max) that the fork's narrower max==min-only check would miss. There is no gap where the fork protected users and vanilla does not -- disposition=dropped-upstream is correct to stand, and equivalence should never have been 'contradicted' (a forbidden pairing with dropped-upstream per worker-instructions.md pilot lessons in the first place).

DEFAULTS DELTA, HONESTLY ASSESSED: fork changed DFLT_STICK_CAL_CEN 2000->2048 and DFLT_STICK_CAL_MAX 3500->3595 (MIN stayed 500 in both). Vanilla 6.18.38 (drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c:1097-1099) still reads CEN=2000/MAX=3500/MIN=500 -- confirmed via `git log -p --follow -- drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c` in /mnt/source/linux that no commit ever revised these constants after their introduction in 83d640c4f8f8. joycon_map_stick_val() (byte-identical in both trees: fork hid-nintendo.c:1038-1054, vanilla :1321-1337) only uses these constants on the fallback path, normalizing raw ADC value into +-32767 via (val-center)*32767/(max-center) or the min-side equivalent. Is the sensitivity delta user-noticeable? Only for the narrow population of devices that hit the fallback path (broken/clone calibration or SPI-read failure) -- real Nintendo hardware with valid calibration never touches these constants regardless of kernel version. For a hypothetical fallback device whose true physical rest ADC value sits near the 12-bit midpoint (2048), vanilla's center=2000 default would read approximately (48/1500)*32767 ~= 3.2% off-center at rest versus ~0% under MiSTer's center=2048 default -- a real but small delta, and arguably moot in practice since a clone controller that fails calibration plausibility checks has no verified 'true center' to compare against in the first place. Not verified on physical hardware in this environment; flagged for hardware confirmation but assessed LOW/cosmetic severity given it never touches genuine hardware and the baseline (broken clone with garbage calibration) has no reliable ground truth to begin with. Severity downgraded from feature-loss to cosmetic on this basis (see impact.severity).

SUPERSEDED-BETTER EVIDENCE (userspace non-coupling to the fork's specific interface, per pilot lessons): grepped Main_MiSTer/input.cpp for literal 2048, 3595, 2000, 3500 -- zero hits. process_joycon() (input.cpp:4740-4831) only performs axis-sign inversion for JOYCON combo mode and DPAD simulation/combo-split bookkeeping on already-normalized +-32767 EV_ABS values; it does not know or care which default calibration constants produced them. This confirms zero userspace coupling to the fork's specific default magnitudes, satisfying the evidentiary bar for 'superseded-better'.

CORRECTION FROM FIRST PASS: prior record paired disposition='dropped-upstream' with equivalence='contradicted' and verification.contradiction=true -- a forbidden combination per worker-instructions.md pilot lessons (contradicted implies carry-candidate/misclassified, which directly contradicts a dropped-upstream disposition standing unchanged). The contradiction flag is reserved for an upstream-coverage claim that turns out not to match vanilla; here vanilla's check is proven (above) to be a strict superset of the fork's, so the upstream-coverage claim fully holds and is in fact stronger than the fork's own fix. Corrected: equivalence -> 'superseded-better', verification.contradiction -> false, impact.severity feature-loss -> cosmetic (the actual protection is present and better; only a minor fallback-default magnitude differs), vanilla_file_line off-by-one corrected from 1090-1092 to 1089-1092 (the comment line the quote begins with is line 1089). disposition, carried_patch=null, failure_mode=silent, dependencies.superseded_by=50503e360eeb, and agrees_with_provenance_doc=false were already correct and remain unchanged. sonnet-verified: corrected (equivalence was wrongly graded 'contradicted', a forbidden pairing with dropped-upstream; proved by construction that vanilla's min<center<max ordering check is a strict superset of the fork's max==min check -- it catches the same all-zero clone case plus more -- so regraded to superseded-better with contradiction cleared; severity downgraded feature-loss->cosmetic since real protection is present; the 2000/3500 vs 2048/3595 default-constant delta is real but honestly assessed as low-impact, fallback-path-only, and unverifiable on hardware in this environment).

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:4740-4831 process_joycon() — consumes EV_ABS events from Joy-Cons (axis-sign inversion for combo mode, DPAD simulation, combo/split bookkeeping); grepped input.cpp for the literal constants 2048, 3595, 2000, 3500 and found none — process_joycon() never inspects or depends on which default calibration constants produced the value it receives, so there is zero coupling to the specific fork-vs-vanilla default magnitudes (only to the generic normalized-abs-value interface, which is identical in both trees).

notes: This commit represents a wholesale resync of the fork's xpad.c driver with a version that incorporates upstream changes from 2022–2024. Vanilla 6.18.38 already includes all the substantive features this commit brings: (1) Event code constants: BTN_GRIPL/GRIPR/GRIPL2/GRIPR2 (0x224–0x227, verified at input-event-codes.h:605-608, exact match) for Xbox Elite paddle buttons; (2) ABS_PROFILE (0x21, input-event-codes.h:893) for profile selector (Xbox Adaptive Controller); (3) MAP_SHARE_BUTTON/MAP_PADDLES/MAP_PROFILE_BUTTON (xpad.c:83-85, exact match) and packet type detection (PKT_XBE1/XBE2_FW_OLD/FW_5_EARLY at xpad.c:103-105) for GIP protocol decoding; (4) Paddle button reporting with firmware-version-specific byte offsets (BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2 dispatch at xpad.c:1096-1099 and again at :1209-1212 for a second firmware branch, both inside PKT_XBE2_FW_5_11-related handling starting ~xpad.c:1087); (5) All GIP protocol command defines; (6) device table reorganization. CORRECTION to prior pass: equivalence was mislabeled 'byte-identical' -- it is not. `git show af27afc4c:drivers/input/joystick/xpad.c | wc -l` = 2383 lines vs vanilla's 2352 (`wc -l drivers/input/joystick/xpad.c`); the files differ structurally (different sort order, fork carries MiSTer-only deltas from other commits layered into the same file, vanilla evolved independently from a different lineage). The *functional* features this specific commit's diff introduces are present and working the same way upstream, which is 'equivalent', not a byte-for-byte match -- reserve 'byte-identical' for cases actually verified via diff with zero output (see e.g. record 9521b003c, Stadia FF). ESCALATION FINDING CONFIRMED: `head -80 board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch` contains, verbatim in its Provenance section: 'NOT ported: af27afc4c2662d9b4673687c0279994914060d2a "Update xpad driver (#63)", zakk4223, 2025-09-29 -- a wholesale resync of xpad.c to a much newer upstream version. Its content is superseded by 6.18's own xpad.c, which already carries MAP_PADDLES/MAP_SHARE_BUTTON/MAP_PROFILE_BUTTON, the paddle report path (BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2), Flydigi Apex 5, and XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2c22) is the only piece of it this patch still needs to add itself (see delta 2).' This directly contradicts docs/patch-provenance.md:365, which lists `af27afc4c, f3c75eb02, a2242dd85, c035c21c0` together under 'xpad deltas' → `0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch` with no indication that af27afc4c specifically was excluded -- a genuine doc/patch-header disagreement, not a re-derivation error on this record's part. The only MiSTer-specific features (cpoll, GIP exclusion for xone driver, Flydigi Vader support, Qanba explicit entry) are not in vanilla 6.18.38 and are correctly carried via separate deltas in the 0017 patch (deltas 1–4 of that patch correspond to fork commits f3c75eb02, a2242dd85, c035c21c0, but NOT af27afc4c). No silent-regression flag needed: dropping this commit causes no functional loss on our image because 6.18.38 already carries the generalized upstream equivalents.

author: Shig

superseded_by: 94f18bb19945 (vanilla, 2023-12-04, 'HID: nintendo: add support for nso controllers')

affected_hardware: Nintendo Switch Online Mega Drive/Genesis Controller
impact.effect_if_absent: NSO Mega Drive/Genesis controller would still work via vanilla 6.18's gencon support (joycon_type_is_gencon), but over Bluetooth would report incorrect product ID (0x2017/SNESCON instead of 0x201E/MDCON) to userspace input layer.

notes: ESCALATION REQUIRED — GROUPED ROW in provenance.md: This commit is grouped with `e155f6a2f` (NSO NES/SNES) and `2799f8b94` (NSO N64), but must be analyzed independently (instructions §3). **Disposition analysis**: Fork commit (2023-09-04) precedes upstream 94f18bb19945 (2023-12-04). Vanilla 6.18.38 already includes NSO Genesis/Mega Drive support via 94f18bb19945, using `JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_GEN (0x0D)` and `USB_DEVICE_ID_NINTENDO_GENCON (0x201e)` -- confirmed at hid-nintendo.c:322 and hid-ids.h:1067. **Key difference (equivalence=partial)**: Fork adds a product-ID forcing workaround (`git show b00a72159`: `if (hdev->product == USB_DEVICE_ID_NINTENDO_SNESCON && ctlr->ctlr_type == JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_MD) hdev->product = USB_DEVICE_ID_NINTENDO_MDCON;` in joycon_input_create()) for a hardware quirk: the NSO MD controller reports PID 0x201e over USB but 0x2017 (SNESCON's PID) over Bluetooth. Vanilla is *aware* of the identical quirk -- its own comment at hid-nintendo.c:2446-2452 reads verbatim: 'Unfortantly the hdev->product can't always be used due to a ?bug? with the NSO Genesis controller. Over USB, it will report the PID as 0x201E, but over bluetooth it will report the PID as 0x2017 which is the same as the NSO SNES controller' -- but vanilla's fix is architectural, not cosmetic: it never dispatches button/type behavior off hdev->product at all (all type dispatch uses `ctlr->ctlr_type` read from a device-info subcommand, e.g. joycon_type_is_snescon() at hid-nintendo.c:701-703), so buttons are correct either way. Crucially, vanilla makes NO attempt to correct hdev->product itself -- confirmed by grepping hid-nintendo.c for `hdev->product =` (only 2 hits, both unrelated IMU/IMU-input id.product propagation at :2070/:2134, never a corrective assignment) -- so `input_dev->id.product` (set at hid-nintendo.c:2134: `ctlr->input->id.product = hdev->product;`) is left at 0x2017 for a Bluetooth-connected MD pad in vanilla 6.18.38, exactly as this record originally suspected. **RESOLVED (was open in prior pass): the workaround DOES matter for a real consumer.** Main_MiSTer's gamecontroller_db.cpp:544 (gcdb_map_for_controller) builds its DB lookup key as a raw GUID string directly from (bustype, vid, pid, version) -- see gamecontroller_db.cpp:554 -- and looks it up by exact match in an external SDL-format gamecontrollerdb.txt (community-maintained, not shipped in this repo, loaded from /media/fat/linux/gamecontrollerdb/). This is the same GUID scheme SDL2's upstream SDL_GameControllerDB uses, which is known to carry separate, differently-shaped entries for Nintendo SNES vs Genesis/Mega Drive NSO controllers (distinct physical button layouts -- SNES has 4 face buttons + 2 shoulders, Genesis has 3 face buttons + Mode/Start/Z). Main_MiSTer's pid field is populated straight from the kernel's reported id.product (input.cpp:5197), with no MD/SNES-specific correction anywhere in Main_MiSTer C++ (grepped input.cpp for 0x201e/0x2017/Genesis/MDCON: no hits). So on vanilla 6.18.38 with no forward-port of this workaround, a real Bluetooth-connected NSO Genesis controller would have its physical (correct, kernel-level) button events silently re-mapped by gcdb using the SNES controller's DB entry -- a real, concrete, non-hypothetical mis-mapping for anyone using that pad over Bluetooth with a gamecontrollerdb-driven core/frontend. Buttons work correctly at the raw evdev level (kernel dispatch uses ctlr_type, not product), but the SDL-DB remap layer that many RetroArch-style cores/frontends rely on keys strictly off (vid,pid) and would get it wrong. **Carry recommendation**: this is a 4-line hunk (the hdev->product-forcing `if` block) with zero risk of regressing anything else; recommend carrying it as a small supplemental patch (or filing it upstream, since vanilla's own comment shows the maintainers are already aware of the quirk and simply chose not to normalize the user-visible PID). **Failure mode=silent**: no build/boot error, no dmesg output -- only a subtly wrong controller mapping that would be hard for a user to diagnose. | KNOWN LIMITATION (present-day, minor): over Bluetooth the NSO Mega Drive pad reports the SNES PID (057e:2017 instead of 2019) because the fork's product-ID normalization is not upstream and was not carried; buttons map correctly via controller type, but SDL-gamecontrollerdb lookups keyed on raw VID:PID may pick the SNES entry (gamecontroller_db.cpp:544/554). USB connection unaffected.
sonnet-verified: corrected -- SHA/ancestry, line citations (hid-nintendo.c:322, hid-ids.h:1067), the doc-disagreement/grouped-row flag, and the core partial-equivalence disposition all confirmed accurate; the open question the prior pass left unresolved ('does the product-ID workaround matter for a real consumer') is now answered definitively YES with a path:line citation (gamecontroller_db.cpp:544/554, input.cpp:3009/5197) and a concrete carry recommendation added to notes and userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref.
subject: Add support for NSO Mega Drive Controller (#50)

userspace_coupling.interface: hdev->product (input device id.product field, propagated to input_dev->id.product at vanilla hid-nintendo.c:2134); consumed by Main_MiSTer's GUID-keyed SDL gamecontrollerdb lookup
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/gamecontroller_db.cpp:544 gcdb_map_for_controller(bustype, vid, pid, version, ...) builds a GUID string directly from the raw (bustype,vid,pid,version) tuple (line 554: sprintf(guid_str, "%04x0000%04x0000%04x0000%04x0000", ...)) and looks it up in /media/fat/linux/gamecontrollerdb/gamecontrollerdb.txt (an external SDL-format community DB, not shipped in this repo); called from Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:3009 with input[sub_dev].pid, which is set at input.cpp:5197 from the kernel-reported id.product. A misreported PID therefore looks up a different GUID row than the physical device.

author: Sorgelig

294a828759d0 (v5.16, charging grip)

impact.effect_if_absent: Nintendo Switch Joy-Con and Pro Controllers will not be recognized or will be mishandled by the HID layer. Charging Grip and NSO controllers (NES, SNES, N64, Genesis) will also not function.

license_provenance.attribution: Copyright (c) 2019-2021 Daniel J. Ogorchock; acknowledges multiple reverse-engineering projects (dekuNukem, Peter Rankin, etc.)
license_provenance.author: Daniel J. Ogorchock (djogorchock@gmail.com)
license_provenance.origin: Linux kernel upstream; commit authored by Ogorchock, submitted to linux-hid for v5.16 (kernel.org)

notes: DISPOSITION RATIONALE: The MiSTer commit (2021-08-20) predates the upstream equivalent (2021-09-11, v5.16) by 3 weeks. The MiSTer version appears to be an enhanced backport: it includes charging grip in the device binding table (which vanilla's 2af16c1f846b did not), but lacks support for NSO controllers (NES, SNES, N64, Genesis) which were added later in upstream (94f18bb19945, v6.3). Vanilla 6.18.38 has the complete driver (2853 lines) with all features integrated, making this commit superseded and safely dropped. EQUIVALENCE IS PARTIAL: Vanilla 6.18.38 has more complete NSO controller support (via enum expansion and type-specific button mappings) than the MiSTer commit, which only adds device IDs without corresponding controller type support. CONFIG_HID_NINTENDO=y and CONFIG_NINTENDO_FF=y are both enabled in our linux.config; Kconfig dependencies (NEW_LEDS, LEDS_CLASS, POWER_SUPPLY) are satisfied. joydev.c changes correctly blacklist Nintendo controllers (PROCON, CHRGGRIP, JOYCONL, JOYCONR) to prevent them appearing as joystick devices -- verified byte-identical against vanilla 6.18's joydev_blacklist[] (drivers/input/joydev.c:797-800), which lists exactly these four and no others (SNESCON/GENCON/N64CON are never in the accelerometer blacklist, upstream or fork, since only the JCL/JCR/PROCON/CHRGGRIP family carries an IMU that would confuse the legacy joydev ABI). UNUSED SNESCON ID (verified, correcting prior-pass note): this commit defines USB_DEVICE_ID_NINTENDO_SNESCON (0x2017) in hid-ids.h but does NOT wire it into nintendo_hid_devices id_table -- confirmed by `git show c4ec5cb40 -- drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c`, whose id_table has only PROCON/CHRGGRIP/JOYCONL/JOYCONR. The define is dead code as of this commit; a SNESCON device would not even bind to this driver yet (hid-generic would claim it instead) -- it is NOT the 'probes successfully with no buttons' failure mode. Full SNES support, including the SNESCON id_table entries and the JOYCON_CTLR_TYPE_SNES dispatch case, was added complete-in-one-shot by the later fork commit e155f6a2f5fe088527f7c01e637e7c51b18a5695 ('hid-nintendo: support for Switch NES and SNES controllers', 2021-09-04) -- SNESCON was never left in a half-wired, silently-dead state in the fork. CORRECTION to prior pass: the earlier version of this note cited '0015-hid-nintendo-nso-famicom.patch' as evidence that unrecognized SNESCON devices 'probe successfully but create an input device with no buttons/axes.' That citation is wrong -- 0015 documents fork commit 484f681720ca5037042a8d3b89158e6d3af09563 (NSO Famicom controllers, type bytes 0x07/0x08), a completely different, much later commit and device family, not SNESCON (type 0x0B) and not this commit. The Famicom pads hit that silent-dead-gamepad failure mode because they report themselves over Bluetooth under the *existing* JOYCONR product ID (0x2007, already in this commit's id_table) with an unrecognized ctlr_type the driver had no case for -- a structurally different bug than an unbound PID. No misclassification with provenance doc (docs/patch-provenance.md line 334 correctly identifies this as upstream-equivalent, class C drop).
provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md table row: 'c4ec5cb40 + 9bdab534b + 60821059c + 45283785a | Switch Pro/Joy-Con backport + fixes | 2af16c1f846b'
sonnet-verified: corrected -- disposition/equivalence/SHAs/joydev-blacklist all confirmed accurate, but the notes' SNESCON failure-mode citation was wrong (borrowed the Famicom-controller 'silent dead gamepad' evidence from an unrelated later commit/patch); rewrote to state the real, verified fact: SNESCON id is defined-but-unused by this commit and was fully wired up (not left half-broken) by the later fork commit e155f6a2f.

Vanilla 6.18.38 has complete integration of all features.

userspace_coupling.interface: HID input events via /dev/input/eventX and /dev/hidraw*; controller type detection via device info subcommands; calibration via SPI flash read/write subcommands; force feedback via input_ff_memless
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer controller input handling relies on HID input event codes from hid-nintendo driver for all Nintendo Switch-based controllers. The driver provides button mappings, stick axes, IMU data, and rumble/force-feedback functionality.

author: Sorgelig

device_ids: 057e:2017 (SNESCON — dedicated PID, own HID_USB_DEVICE + HID_BLUETOOTH_DEVICE id-table entries)

057e:2007 (NESL/NESR — NOT a distinct PID; shared with the ordinary Joy-Con R id-table entry, disambiguated purely by an internal ctlr_type handshake byte read at runtime, report->subcmd_reply.data[2] == 0x09/0x0A; no new hid_device_id entry is added for NES at all, in either the fork or vanilla)
impact.effect_if_absent: Without either the fork's or vanilla's support, Nintendo Switch Online SNES controller (dedicated PID 0x2017) fails to enumerate entirely — no input device created, completely non-functional. NES controllers (which reuse the standard Joy-Con R PID 0x2007, see device_ids) would be misidentified as a plain Joy-Con R: they would create an input device but the type-9/10 (0x09/0x0A) handshake byte would be unhandled, likely leaving buttons unmapped or misbehaving rather than cleanly failing. With vanilla 6.18.38's support present instead of the fork's: BOTH controller families work and are recognized, but for the plain NES controller specifically, the A and B buttons are SWAPPED relative to what the fork commit reports (see upstream.equivalence=partial) — a real, silent, button-mapping regression for anyone whose game-core default mappings assume the fork's A/B assignment.

license_provenance: GPL-2.0+; driver source is Daniel J. Ogorchock's hid-nintendo (originally out-of-tree, vendored by fork starting 2021-08-20, integrated upstream 2021-09-11 to v5.16, enhanced with NSO support 2023-12-04).
notes: GROUPING ERROR IN PROVENANCE DOC (line 335, confirmed still present): this commit is grouped with 2799f8b94 (N64 controllers, +2 years) and b00a72159 (Mega Drive, +2 years). They are separate additions at different times (e155f6a2f: 2021-09-04, 2799f8b94: 2023-09-02, b00a72159: 2023-09-04). Only e155f6a2f adds NES/SNES support. DISPOSITION: vanilla 6.18.38 via commit 94f18bb19945915fcdfd1903841020ef1b6af44a ('HID: nintendo: add support for nso controllers', Ryan McClelland, 2023-12-04) provides the same DEVICE coverage (same type enum values 0x09/0x0A/0x0B; same SNESCON id-table entries) with identical D-pad handling, identical rumble/IMU exclusion for NES/SNES (joycon_has_rumble()/joycon_has_imu() at hid-nintendo.c:746-767 exclude both types by construction, matching the fork's explicit skip), and IDENTICAL SNES button mapping (A→BTN_EAST, B→BTN_SOUTH, X→BTN_NORTH, Y→BTN_WEST, L→BTN_TL, R→BTN_TR, ZL→BTN_TL2, ZR→BTN_TR2, MINUS→BTN_SELECT, PLUS→BTN_START — verified byte-for-byte against snescon_button_mappings[] at hid-nintendo.c:446-458). BUT the standalone NES controller mapping is NOT identical: nescon_button_mappings[] at hid-nintendo.c:436-444 maps JC_BTN_A→BTN_SOUTH and JC_BTN_B→BTN_EAST, which is the REVERSE of the fork's shared A/B block (`input_report_key(dev, BTN_EAST, btns & JC_BTN_A); input_report_key(dev, BTN_SOUTH, btns & JC_BTN_B);` — verified via `git show` diff, applies unconditionally to BOTH nescon and snescon in the fork). This is a REAL, confirmed A/B swap for the plain NES controller only (not SNES) — SONNET-VERIFY caught the original record's 'byte-identical' grade as WRONG; per worker-instructions ('independent implementations are rarely byte-identical; check button-for-button') this is exactly the failure mode flagged. Independently corroborated: docs/patch-provenance.md §9.2 (lines 1343-1352, written for a DIFFERENT record, the 0015-hid-nintendo-nso-famicom.patch carry) already documents this exact swap: "The original reports 'A' as BTN_EAST and 'B' as BTN_SOUTH for all NSO controllers. Upstream 6.18 maps NES-family pads positionally instead — 'A' → BTN_SOUTH, 'B' → BTN_EAST ... Consequence: A and B are swapped relative to stock MiSTer" — this is a pre-existing, already-known consequence of taking upstream's hid-nintendo, just never linked back to THIS record before. Re-graded equivalence byte-identical → partial: SNES is functionally byte-identical, NES has a confirmed behavioral divergence in the A/B assignment (everything else — D-pad, L/R, Select/Start, rumble/IMU exclusion, device probing — matches). Also corrected: device_ids expanded (SNES has a real dedicated PID 0x2017; NES has NO dedicated PID at all, it rides on the pre-existing Joy-Con R entry 0x2007, disambiguated only by a runtime handshake byte — re-derived from hid-ids.h and the nintendo_hid_devices[] id table, which the original record's device_ids field did not capture); main_mister_ref citation corrected from a non-existent ~1795 to the actual LED logic at input.cpp:2752-2765, plus a newly-found QUIRK_JOYCON/combo-pairing coupling at input.cpp:5331-5334 that the original coupling analysis missed. Not carried (no 00xx patch file exists for this specific commit; the LATER, SEPARATE 0015-hid-nintendo-nso-famicom.patch carries Famicom support and inherits this same A/B convention deliberately, per patch-provenance.md §9.2). FAILURE MODE IS SILENT either way — no probe error, just missing/altered buttons. sonnet-verified: confirmed vanilla-SHA ancestry (`git merge-base --is-ancestor 94f18bb19945915fcdfd1903841020ef1b6af44a v6.18.38` holds), disposition, and grouping-error finding; corrected equivalence (byte-identical→partial), device_ids (added 057e:2007 nuance for NES), and main_mister_ref (wrong line ~1795 → actual 2752-2765, plus added the QUIRK_JOYCON coupling at 5331-5334). | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): disposition stays dropped-upstream (controllers supported upstream); the A/B assignment divergence is restored to stock via 0034 (also aligns 0015's Famicom tables).
provenance_doc_ref: line 335, but grouped incorrectly with commits 2799f8b94 (N64, 2023-09-02) and b00a72159 (Mega Drive, 2023-09-04). This commit (2021-09-04) is a separate addition of NES/SNES support only.
subject: hid-nintendo: support for Switch NES and SNES controllers.

userspace_coupling.interface: HID device enumeration / input event reporting via /dev/input/event*; Main_MiSTer player-number LED control via sysfs (joycon player LEDs); Main_MiSTer QUIRK_JOYCON combo-pairing logic (unintended secondary coupling — see main_mister_ref)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:2752-2765 (NOT ~1795 as originally cited — that line range is unrelated `get_map_*()` accessor code) — `else if (input[dev].vid == 0x057e && ((input[dev].pid & 0xFF00) == 0x2000)) { // nintendo switch controllers ... set_led(led_path, ":player1"/":player2"/... ) }`; this LED path matches SNESCON (0x2017) since 0x2017 & 0xFF00 == 0x2000, but does NOT match NES controllers (0x2007 & 0xFF00 == 0x2000 also matches — so NES DOES get this LED path too). ADDITIONAL coupling not in the original record: input.cpp:5331-5334 unconditionally sets `input[n].quirk = QUIRK_JOYCON` and `misc_flags = 1<<29` (JOYCON_RIGHT) for ANY device with vid=0x057e/pid=0x2007 — since NES controllers share this exact PID with the standard Joy-Con R, Main_MiSTer will attempt to treat a standalone NES controller as a right Joy-Con eligible for the JOYCON_COMBO left/right-pairing dance (input.cpp:4689 check_joycon(), :4740 process_joycon()). This is a real behavioral quirk of sharing the JOYCONR PID, present for BOTH the fork's and vanilla's implementation equally (not something this commit's disposition changes), but worth flagging since it was absent from the original coupling analysis.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: a3dc32c635bae0ae569f489e00de0e8f015bfc25 (vanilla usb-storage unusual_devs.h blacklist, equivalent mechanism)

impact.effect_if_absent: Realtek WiFi adapter (0x0bda:0x1a2b) would enumerate as a fake CD-ROM (mass storage device) and usb_modeswitch would fail to switch it to WiFi mode, leaving it non-functional.

notes:
MiSTer's 5.15 fork approach: adds 0x0bda:0x1a2b to the ignore_ids[] table in usual-tables.c (struct ignore_entry only captures vid/pid/bcdmin/bcdmax -- ANY match causes usb_usual_ignore_device() to return -ENXIO from storage_probe(), unconditional of any flags field) with narrow bcdDevice range (0x0000-0x9999). Vanilla 6.18.38 approach: adds an entry to unusual_devs.h (the MAIN usb_storage_usb_ids match table, a different table from ignore_ids) with the US_FL_IGNORE_DEVICE flag and wider bcdDevice range (0x0000-0xffff), AND also adds a companion entry for 0x0bda:0xa192 (Mercury MW310UH, confirmed present at unusual_devs.h:1520). This flag is checked separately in usb.c:689 (`if (us->fflags & US_FL_IGNORE_DEVICE) return -ENODEV;`), a genuinely different code path/table from the fork's ignore_ids mechanism (usb.c:1212) though both live in drivers/usb/storage/usb.c's storage_probe() and produce the same practical outcome: usb-storage refuses to bind the device. Equivalence='equivalent' (not byte-identical) is the right grade given the differing mechanism/table and the wider range plus extra device vanilla covers.

sonnet-verified correction (significant): the original record claimed 'kernel-level suppression is redundant given usb_modeswitch coverage' and cited userspace_coupling.coupled=true backed by files under work/imgroot/. Independent verification found this is WRONG for our actual build: BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH and BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH_DATA are both explicitly unset in the current output/.config, and never appear in configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig's git history. The work/imgroot files cited are stale artifacts (2022/2025 timestamps) from the gitignored work/ build-output tree, not the current shipped rootfs (output/target has zero usb_modeswitch files). Conclusion: there is NO userspace-level fallback for this device in Buildroot_MiSTer today. The kernel-level blacklist (currently obtained for free by building against vanilla 6.18.38, since a3dc32c635ba is upstream) is the SOLE protection mechanism -- not redundant, but load-bearing. This does not change the disposition (still dropped-upstream/equivalent, correctly not carried as a 00xx patch since vanilla covers it) but it does invalidate the 'redundant' framing and raises the practical importance of keeping the vanilla kernel source (not a fork patch) as the source of this protection going forward. Disposition re-verified against patch-provenance.md:345, which matches exactly (byte-for-byte) and does not itself make the redundancy claim -- that claim originated solely in this record's own (unverified) notes.

sonnet-verified: corrected

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Kernel panic (divide-by-zero exception) if Nintendo Switch Joy-Con or Pro Controller sends calibration data with zero scale or offset values

notes: This commit is byte-identical in function to vanilla commit 6eb04ca8c52e (2023-12-05) which fixes a real divide-by-zero crash reported in SteamOS (github.com/ValveSoftware/SteamOS/issues/1070). The MiSTer fork applied this fix 10 months later (2024-10-08), presumably after the upstream kernel was advanced to 6.18+. Since the MiSTer kernel will be rebased to vanilla 6.18.38 or later, this commit is now redundant and should be dropped. The fix involves two parts: (1) guarding IMU calibration divisor calculations in joycon_calc_imu_cal_divisors() to replace zero divisors with 1 and log a warning; (2) moving the divide-by-zero check in joycon_parse_imu_report() to execute after samples are reset, preventing race conditions. Verified in vanilla 6.18.38:drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c lines 930-950 (divisor guards) and 1186-1196 (avg_delta check). Already documented in patch-provenance.md:336 as dropped-upstream. No userspace coupling: this is a kernel-internal crash fix. sonnet-verified: Re-derived from scratch — `git show 6eb04ca8c52e3f8c8ea7102ade81d642eee87f4a` in /mnt/source/linux confirms the full 40-char SHA resolves and `git merge-base --is-ancestor` confirms it is reachable from v6.18.38; both fork hunks (accel+gyro divisor guards in joycon_calc_imu_cal_divisors, and the imu_avg_delta_ms==0 check relocated out of the periodic-recalc block in joycon_parse_imu_report) are present in vanilla 6.18.38 with logic that diffs byte-for-byte identical modulo whitespace/blank-line placement (confirmed via direct `diff` of extracted function bodies), so equivalence corrected from byte-identical to equivalent (not partial — vanilla covers both sites the fork does, no under-coverage); vanilla_file_line in the original record was wrong for the current checkout (930-950/1186-1196 mixed historical @@ line numbers with the wrong current location — 1186 is actually where joycon_calc_imu_cal_divisors now starts, not the second hunk) and has been corrected to 1186-1209 and 1360/1441-1444, matching patch-provenance.md:336's citation of 1196,1201 for the divisor guards; agrees_with_provenance_doc=true confirmed independently.
subject: drivers: hid-nintendo: fix possible division by 0.

upstream.vanilla_file_line: drivers/hid/hid-nintendo.c:1186-1209 (joycon_calc_imu_cal_divisors guards, warn at 1207-1208), :1360,1441-1444 (joycon_parse_imu_report imu_avg_delta_ms guard moved outside the periodic-recalc block)
upstream.vanilla_quote: static void joycon_calc_imu_cal_divisors(struct joycon_ctlr *ctlr) { int i, divz = 0; ... if (ctlr->imu_cal_accel_divisor[i] == 0) { ctlr->imu_cal_accel_divisor[i] = 1; divz++; } if (ctlr->imu_cal_gyro_divisor[i] == 0) { ctlr->imu_cal_gyro_divisor[i] = 1; divz++; } } if (divz) hid_warn(ctlr->hdev, "inaccurate IMU divisors (%d)\n", divz); } ... [in joycon_parse_imu_report, after the JC_IMU_SAMPLES_PER_DELTA_AVG block] /* don't ever want divide by zero shenanigans */ if (ctlr->imu_avg_delta_ms == 0) { ctlr->imu_avg_delta_ms = 1; hid_warn(ctlr->hdev, "calculated avg imu delta of 0\n"); }

author: fjmartinez2k

superseded_by: board/mister/de10nano/linux.config (CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF=y)

impact.effect_if_absent: Pantherlord and GreenAsia game controllers would not have force feedback (rumble/vibration) support

notes: Grouped defconfig entry in patch-provenance.md (11 commits total). Commit enabled CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF in fork's arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig, changing it from disabled (# CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF is not set) to enabled (CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF=y). The option exists in vanilla 6.18 (drivers/hid/Kconfig:896) and is already enabled in our 6.18 config (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config). Stock 5.15 image (IKCONFIG snapshot) also has CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF=y. Not carried as a patch; the configuration change has been adopted into our linux.config. Userspace coupling confirmed: Main_MiSTer uses FF_RUMBLE ioctl for rumble effect support across multiple device types including Pantherlord controllers. No conflicts or regressions detected. | sonnet-verified: DEVICE-ID CORRECTION. Re-derived drivers/hid/hid-pl.c's pl_devices[] table directly (this is the actual CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF-gated driver, compiled as hid-pl.o per drivers/hid/Makefile:100 'obj-$(CONFIG_HID_PANTHERLORD) += hid-pl.o'): it matches {USB_VENDOR_ID_GAMERON(0x0810), USB_DEVICE_ID_GAMERON_DUAL_PSX_ADAPTOR(0x0001)}, {GAMERON, DUAL_PCS_ADAPTOR(0x0002)}, {USB_VENDOR_ID_GREENASIA(0x0e8f), 0x0003}, {USB_VENDOR_ID_JESS2(0x0f30), JESS2_COLOR_RUMBLE_PAD(0x0111)} (drivers/hid/hid-ids.h:537-539,581-583,749-750). Note: GAMERON and PANTHERLORD are two separate #define names in hid-ids.h that happen to share the literal vendor ID 0x0810 (PANTHERLORD_TWIN_USB_JOYSTICK is also 0x0001, hid-quirks.c:160) — same rebranded hardware, so labeling 0810:0001/0002 'Pantherlord' is defensible. REMOVED '1209:FACA / ControllaBLE Bluetooth gamepad' — exhaustive grep of drivers/hid/*.c and hid-ids.h for '0x1209', '0xFACA', and 'ControllaBLE' found zero matches; this device ID does not appear anywhere in the pl_devices[] table or the kernel tree and was hallucinated in the original pass. userspace_coupling citations independently re-verified byte-exact: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:4881 is literally 'fef->type = FF_RUMBLE;' and :5228 is literally 'if (test_bit(FF_RUMBLE, ff_features)) {' — both confirmed correct, no change needed. Disposition dropped-deliberate was already correct (config enable IS present in our linux.config, matches the adopted/superseded_by pattern) — no disposition change, device_ids/affected_hardware arrays corrected only.

notes: CORRECTED (Sonnet escalation, orchestrator-flagged internal contradiction). The prior version of this record was self-contradictory: its notes asserted 'ZERO in-kernel coverage ... adapter transparently unavailable ... regression vs stock' while its own dependencies.superseded_by simultaneously named package/rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng and package/rtl8188fu as the coverage. Both halves were wrong. Re-derived from the live checkouts: (1) configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:568-569 explicitly disables BOTH packages ('# BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8188EU_AIRCRACK_NG is not set' / '# BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8188FU is not set'), each with an inline comment '-> mainline rtl8xxxu (8188eu)' / '-> mainline rtl8xxxu (8188fu)', and a block comment at defconfig:550-557 stating they are left OFF specifically to avoid bind-fighting the in-kernel driver on the same USB IDs -- so the packages were never the active coverage mechanism; the prior record's own superseded_by list was mistaken. (2) board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:212-213 has CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m and CONFIG_RTL8XXXU_UNTESTED=y. (3) /mnt/source/linux drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtl8xxxu/core.c dev_table[] unconditionally claims every VID:PID this record lists, bound to rtl8188eu_fops/rtl8188fu_fops (see dependencies.superseded_by for exact line numbers), none gated behind the CONFIG_RTL8XXXU_UNTESTED #ifdef (core.c:8130, after these entries). (4) Only rtl8xxxu.ko is actually built into our image for these IDs -- the two BR2 packages are not built at all (not set), so there is no real bind race, only a documented hypothetical one the defconfig comment preempts. Conclusion: this is full, active, verified in-kernel coverage via the standard upstream mac80211 rtl8xxxu driver -- not a regression, not silent, not a coverage gap. Corrections applied: impact.severity feature-loss -> none; impact.failure_mode silent -> null (nothing is absent, so nothing fails); dependencies.superseded_by rewritten to name CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m as the primary/active supersession and demote the two BR2 packages to inert documented fallback, not coverage; upstream.* populated with real vanilla SHAs/quote (previously null) since the coverage genuinely traces to vanilla mainline, strengthening rather than reclassifying the dropped-deliberate disposition (kept as instructed -- we deliberately chose not to carry/vendor the original driver, in favor of already-upstream mainline). Secondary finding for the auditor: patch-provenance.md:389 (Class E table) is itself stale on this point -- it still states the plan as 'package/rtl8188eu, rtl8188fu ... Do not vendor', predating the later, defconfig-only-documented decision to prefer mainline rtl8xxxu for these two chips specifically (and to rename the aircrack-ng fork package to avoid a Buildroot-upstream Kconfig/Make namespace collision). That is a provenance-doc staleness issue, tracked separately from this record's now-corrected coverage claim. sonnet-verified: corrected -- replaced a self-contradictory 'silent regression' narrative with confirmed, fully-cited mainline rtl8xxxu in-kernel coverage (severity none, disposition dropped-deliberate with an accurate superseded_by).

impact.effect_if_absent: No user-visible impact. Removed symbols are build-system-auto-detected (def_bool), not user-selectable options. Removing them from defconfig is a cleanup that corrects config practice without affecting functionality.

notes: Grouped defconfig entry in patch-provenance.md (11 commits total, row labeled 'defconfig'). This commit removes 4 auto-detected config symbols and fixes 1 trailing-whitespace issue in PANTHERLORD_FF line. Removed symbols: (1) CONFIG_CC_HAS_ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS=y — def_bool based on $(cc-option,-fzero-call-used-regs=used-gpr) in security/Kconfig.hardening:190, auto-detected; (2) CONFIG_ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS — bool option that depends on CC_HAS_ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS, disabled in defconfig; (3) CONFIG_PAHOLE_HAS_SPLIT_BTF=y — def_bool based on PAHOLE_VERSION>=119 in lib/Kconfig.debug, auto-detected; (4) CONFIG_HAVE_KCSAN_COMPILER=y — def_bool based on compiler capabilities in lib/Kconfig.kcsan, auto-detected. All four symbols exist in vanilla 6.18.38 (verified via grep in respective Kconfig files). CRITICAL: These are NOT user-selectable options — they are build-time capability detection symbols. Having them hardcoded in defconfig is incorrect kernel config practice. They should NOT appear in defconfig files; the build system automatically detects them during make. Our 6.18 linux.config correctly omits these symbols. Disposition: DROPPED-DELIBERATE because (a) the cleanup applies only to fork's 5.15 defconfig artifact, (b) auto-detected symbols must not be in defconfig, (c) our 6.18 linux.config is the successor artifact and correctly excludes them, (d) no functionality loss. ESCALATION FLAG: Commit is part of GROUPED defconfig row (11 commits), per worker instructions §4.7 requires escalation. | sonnet-verified: all 4 auto-derived-symbol claims independently re-derived and CONFIRMED exact, including line numbers: security/Kconfig.hardening:190-191 'config CC_HAS_ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS / def_bool $(cc-option,-fzero-call-used-regs=used-gpr)' (record's cited line 190 is exact); lib/Kconfig.debug:392-393 'config PAHOLE_HAS_SPLIT_BTF / def_bool PAHOLE_VERSION >= 119'; lib/Kconfig.kcsan:6-7 'config HAVE_KCSAN_COMPILER / def_bool (CC_IS_CLANG && ...) || (CC_IS_GCC && ...)'. ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS itself correctly described as a plain bool (not def_bool) depending on CC_HAS_ZERO_CALL_USED_REGS — record does not overclaim its type. Cross-checked against sibling commit 316288a3d (one day later, same defconfig): it briefly RE-ADDS 'CONFIG_PAHOLE_HAS_SPLIT_BTF=y' before commit 97a398176 (2026-04-17) removes it again — a minor flip-flop across fork history, harmless (auto-detected symbols surviving in a defconfig are inert, the build system recomputes them), noted here for completeness but does not change this record's analysis or disposition. Disposition dropped-deliberate confirmed correct as-is (this is a pure cleanup/removal, not a config-enable-adoption case, so the macvlan/dropped-deliberate distinction does not apply here). No corrections required.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch

impact.effect_if_absent: MiSTer RTC add-on boards using mcp7941x or m41t81 chips on i2c1 bus would not be detected or functional

notes:
PROVENANCE DOC DISAGREEMENT: This commit is listed in patch-provenance.md as part of a GROUPED row with 12 other commits (aa8afe109..077c2c317), violating the grounding contract's requirement to analyze commits independently. This analysis treats it as a single commit.

FUNCTIONAL ASSESSMENT: This commit adds two RTC devices (&i2c1 rtc_at_68 and rtc_at_6F) using bare compatible strings ('m41t81' and 'mcp7941x', without vendor prefixes). Vanilla 6.18.38 supports these chips via rtc-m41t80.c and rtc-ds1307.c drivers. The bare compatible strings work via a fallback mechanism in of_i2c_register_devices() → of_i2c_get_board_info() → of_alias_from_compatible(), which extracts the device name and matches against i2c_device_id tables: both 'm41t81' and 'mcp7941x' exist in those tables (/mnt/source/linux/drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:m41t80_id and rtc-ds1307.c:ds1307_id). Equivalent functionality exists in vanilla 6.18.38: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:41 has { 'm41t81', ... } and drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c has { 'mcp7941x', mcp794xx }.

CARRIER PATCH CORRECTION: The carrier patch 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (/mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch:180-182) supersedes this commit with vendor-prefixed compatible strings ('st,m41t81' and 'microchip,mcp7941x'), which are the proper device tree bindings that match the of_device_id tables in vanilla. The patch provenance document §3.1 line 62 correctly lists commit 2548c2978 as one of the de10-nano DTS follow-ups that were consolidated into patch 0004 during forward-port P1.7.

DISPOSITION RATIONALE: This commit is NOT carried as a separate patch; its functionality is ABSORBED into 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch. The commit's changes are superseded by the carrier patch's corrected compatible strings. Dropping this commit is deliberate—the feature it adds (RTC support) is preserved via the carrier patch but with improved/corrected device tree bindings.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR IF ABSENT: If this commit were reverted in the fork, the RTCs would not be added to &i2c1. However, since the fork later switched from &i2c1 to i2c-gpio (commit 1337de1fd), this functionality is moot. In our 6.18.38 carry, RTCs are added via the i2c-gpio bus (0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch lines 170-183), which is more robust than the raw &i2c1 approach and uses the correct vendor-prefixed compatible strings.

sonnet-verified: carry-mapping CONFIRMED. Re-read 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch directly: rtc@6f { compatible = "microchip,mcp7941x"; reg = <0x6f>; } is at patch lines 180-182 exactly as cited, and rtc@68 { compatible = "st,m41t81"; ... } at lines 175-178. Cross-checked both vendor-prefixed compatible strings against the live vanilla driver ID tables: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:103 has of_device_id.compatible="st,m41t81", drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c:1142 has compatible="microchip,mcp7941x". Also confirmed the bare-string fallback claim: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:77 i2c_device_id has "m41t81", drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c:1079 i2c_device_id has "mcp7941x" -- both match this commit's original (pre-0004) bare compatible strings. Re-read the original fork commit diff (`git show 2548c2978`) and confirmed it matches the record's description exactly (stm,m41t81 -> bare m41t81, plus new rtc_at_6F/mcp7941x node). No corrections needed.

However, most have implicit defaults or are auto-selected in vanilla 6.18: PNFS layouts default to NFS_V4's value; IMPLEMENTATION_ID_DOMAIN defaults to 'kernel.org'; NFS_USE_KERNEL_DNS defaults to y when NFS_V4 && !LEGACY_DNS; NFS_V4_2_SSC_HELPER defaults to y if NFS_V4_2; SUNRPC_BACKCHANNEL is selected by NFS_V4_1; PAHOLE_HAS_SPLIT_BTF is auto-detected (def_bool PAHOLE_VERSION >= 119). Stock inventory (20250402, 15mo old) has all commit's symbols enabled. Failure mode is silent: NFS4 feature silently unavailable if core support is disabled. No userspace coupling detected in Main_MiSTer.
