notes: CORRECTED RECORD (Sonnet escalation re-derivation). The first-pass record mischaracterized this commit as 'DEAD CODE' / 'an abandoned attempt' on the sole basis that the CONFIG_RTL8188EU/RTL8188FU/RTL8821AU/RTL88X2BU/RTL8821CU symbols are unset in OUR 6.18 linux.config. That conflates two different kernels and misses the actual question, which is what this commit did in the STOCK 5.15 fork and how our build replaces that functionality. Re-derivation: (1) stock-linux.config DOES build these driver families =m -- 'CONFIG_RTL8188EU=m / CONFIG_RTL8188FU=m / CONFIG_RTL8812AU=m / CONFIG_RTL8821AU=m / CONFIG_RTL8822BU=m / CONFIG_RTL8821CU=m' (stock-linux.config:1530-1535), confirming these are live, shipped drivers in stock, not dead code there. Note stock's config has RTL8812AU and RTL8821AU as SEPARATE symbols, which only exist in the fork AFTER 3740d5b88 split them (morrownr backport) -- so stock built a later revision of this driver-tree lineage, not commit 33ff5146a's tree byte-for-byte; the disposition and coverage conclusion are the same either way since this commit is the root of that lineage. (2) Per-chipset coverage table for OUR 6.18 build, each verified: RTL8188EU/RTL8188FU -> mainline CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m (rtl8xxxu.h/core.c, USB IDs 0x8179/0x0179 for 8188E and 0xf179 for 8188F confirmed at /mnt/source/linux/drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtl8xxxu/core.c:8061-8072); RTL8812AU -> out-of-tree BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:565, morrownr's aircrack-ng fork, commit-pinned); RTL8821AU (RTL8811AU/RTL8821AU) -> out-of-tree BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR=y (defconfig:567); RTL88X2BU (RTL8812B/RTL8822B, Kconfig symbol RTL8822BU) -> mainline CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m (linux.config:474-481, deliberately chosen over the out-of-tree 88x2bu because the vendored driver FAILED WPA3-only association on real hardware -- status_code=1 -- documented in linux.config comment and docs/wifi-parity.md; confirmed present in mainline at rtw88/rtw8822bu.c:12); RTL8821CU -> mainline CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m (linux.config:483-488, confirmed at rtw88/rtw8821cu.c:16). ALL SIX chipset families have live, working coverage in our build -- none is a silent regression. Interesting adjacent finding (not a defect in this record's disposition, but worth flagging for kconfig hygiene): vanilla 6.18 also carries mainline rtw88 drivers for RTL8812A and RTL8821A (rtw8812au.c/rtw8821au.c, landed upstream in 6.13 via 8f82bb2cfaf7) that our linux.config comment ('mainline still cannot drive: RTL8812AU, RTL8811AU/RTL8821AU and RTL8814AU', defconfig:545) does not mention and does not enable -- we still rely on the out-of-tree morrownr packages for those two chips. This is not a coverage gap (the morrownr packages work), just a possibly-stale comment/an unexplored option to drop two more out-of-tree packages in favor of mainline; out of scope for this record. Our own docs/patch-provenance.md §3.6 (line 389) and docs/wifi-parity.md already carry the correct 're-source, do not vendor' disposition and correct Kconfig-symbol mapping for this commit family -- the first-pass record's 'dead code/abandoned' framing directly contradicted prior art that was sitting in the same repo. License provenance: all five driver trees are Realtek GPL-2.0 vendor code (copyright headers quoted above), vendored wholesale from Realtek's SDK releases circa 2012-2019; not upstream-mergeable as-is (out of mainline coding-style/API compliance), which is exactly why the project's strategy is 're-source from actively-maintained forks' (morrownr, aircrack-ng) rather than 'carry this vendor tree forward' or 'submit upstream'. sonnet-verified: CORRECTED — disposition changed from an incoherent 'dead code, safe to drop' framing to 'dropped-deliberate, superseded per-chipset by either an out-of-tree BR2 package or a mainline in-kernel driver', with equivalence=partial (mainline covers 4 of 6 chipsets; the other 2 -- 8812A/8821A -- have no mainline driver enabled in our config and are covered by out-of-tree BR2 packages instead) and contradiction=true against the first-pass record's severity/notes fields specifically.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: exFAT filesystem volume dirty flag synchronization is lazy instead of eager; mitigates potential data-loss scenarios in power-fail during file operations on SD card

notes:
CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC is a Samsung exfat driver feature that does not exist in vanilla 6.18 Kconfig. This commit (on MiSTer-v5.14 branch only) toggles it from disabled to enabled in the fork's v5.15 defconfig. Vanilla 6.18's exfat driver lacks this option entirely. Our 6.18 linux.config does not include CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC even though stock-linux.config has it enabled=y; this is expected since our mainline exfat lacks the feature. The Samsung exfat symlinks are carried via patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, but that patch does not include the DELAYED_SYNC codepaths (which were part of the fork's Samsung driver implementation). No userspace code references this option. Disposition: dropped-deliberate — the fork feature was not forward-ported to 6.18's mainline exfat.

sonnet-verified: PASS, no amendment. `grep -rn EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC /mnt/source/linux` returns zero hits anywhere in the vanilla 6.18.38 tree (fs/exfat/Kconfig only defines EXFAT_FS and EXFAT_DEFAULT_IOCHARSET) -- confirmed absent as claimed. Re-derived the commit's own diff via git show: it flips `# CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC is not set` to `CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC=y` in the fork's MiSTer_defconfig, and `git branch -a --contains` confirms this commit exists only on origin/MiSTer-v5.14, matching the record's source_branch field. Also confirmed stock-linux.config:3511 has CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC=y (stock did carry it) while both board/mister/de10nano/linux.config and the resolved output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config lack the symbol entirely (grep returns nothing in either) -- consistent with 'not forward-ported' rather than 'forward-ported but disabled'. 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch confirmed to contain no reference to delayed sync.

This commit is the morrownr-sourced replacement of the earlier vendored drivers (33ff5146a); it IMPROVES the driver quality by moving from old static versions to actively-maintained fork sources, but the fork itself was intentionally not carried forward in-tree for our kernel builds (replaced with BR2 packages that tie to specific commit SHAs and hashes for verification).

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: e23c69e3324892f7420686b3aaa0403df6cf152c — Input: xpad - add support for XBOX One Elite paddles (2022-08-18) — supersedes this fork commit's xpad.c contribution specifically (adds MAP_PADDLES + full paddle-packet decoding that the fork's plain ID-only addition lacked); NOT what our build actually uses

package/xone (BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y) — the driver that ACTUALLY binds 0x045e:0x0b00 in our 6.18 build; confirmed via output/build/xone-f2aa9fe.../driver/gamepad.c:17 `#define GIP_PRODUCT_ELITE_SERIES_2 0x0b00` and paddle-button reporting at :370-373 using the same BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5-8 codes xpad/vanilla use, so Main_MiSTer's paddle-shift coupling (see userspace_coupling) is preserved despite xpad's entry being disabled

forward_port.conflicts: 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch:134-135 comments out `{ 0x045e, 0x0b00, "Microsoft X-Box One Elite 2 pad", MAP_PADDLES, XTYPE_XBOXONE },` (verified present verbatim in the patch file) so that xpad never claims this device, deliberately ceding it to package/xone's GIP driver, which detects it via bInterfaceSubClass=0x47/bInterfaceProtocol=0xd0 (or the GIP dongle/wired transport) rather than xpad's USB-ID match.

impact.effect_if_absent: Dropping this FORK commit itself has NO effect on our 6.18 build: whether xpad.c's 0x045e:0x0b00 entry comes from this fork commit (mapping=0, no paddles) or from vanilla e23c69e3324892f7420686b3aaa0403df6cf152c (MAP_PADDLES, full paddle support), our 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch comments the entry out of xpad.c entirely (line 134-135) so xpad never claims the device either way. Real-world effect if the DEVICE were unrecognized by ANY driver (xpad nor xone): Xbox One Elite 2 pad (0x045e:0x0b00) would not generate input events, AND Main_MiSTer's paddle-triggered chatpad-shift quirk (QUIRK_SHIFT, input.cpp:5523/5810) would never arm, since it depends on paddle button events (BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5-8) that only a GIP/xpad-paddle-aware driver produces. In our actual build this is a non-issue because package/xone binds 0x045e:0x0b00 via the GIP transport and independently implements paddle reporting (output/build/xone-*/driver/gamepad.c:36-39,224-254,370-373 — BTN_GRIPR/R2/L/L2 aliased to BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5/6/7/8), which is what Main_MiSTer's QUIRK_SHIFT logic actually observes.

notes:
CORRECTED ON SONNET-VERIFY (three substantive changes, not a rubber stamp): (1) disposition dropped-upstream → dropped-deliberate. 'dropped-upstream' implies our build relies on vanilla's xpad.c coverage of this ID, but it does not — 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch comments out the SAME line regardless of whether the underlying xpad.c is the 5.15 fork's or 6.18 vanilla's, specifically so package/xone (a BR2 kmod package implementing the proper GIP protocol) binds the device instead. Per worker-instructions pilot lessons, coverage via a BR2 package is dropped-deliberate/dropped-obsolete with superseded_by naming the package, never plain vanilla coverage. (2) equivalence partial → superseded-better, but scoped to the FORK-vs-VANILLA xpad.c comparison only (not our build's actual routing): fork's commit added the ID with mapping=0 (zero paddle support — verified via `git show` diff, single line added, no MAP_PADDLES); vanilla's e23c69e33248 added the SAME id with MAP_PADDLES plus ~190 lines of paddle-packet decoding across three firmware generations (PKT_XBE1/XBE2_FW_OLD/XBE2_FW_5_EARLY/XBE2_FW_5_11) — this is unambiguously superseded-better, not partial, per the schema definition (same device, strictly more capability, zero fork-interface userspace coupling from xpad specifically since xpad never runs against this PID in our build). (3) userspace_coupling.coupled false → true: grep of Main_MiSTer for the raw device ID 0x045e/0x0b00 (not just symbol names) turned up input.cpp:5523 and :5810 — a paddle-buttons-as-chatpad-shift quirk (QUIRK_SHIFT / cfg.xbe2_shift) keyed on this exact VID:PID. The original record's 'No userspace coupling found' claim came from searching for xpad-specific symbols/PIDs that were never grepped directly; searching the literal 0x0b00 constant surfaces it immediately. This coupling is satisfied in our build via xone's independent paddle implementation (BTN_GRIPR/R2/L/L2 == BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5/6/7/8, output/build/xone-*/driver/gamepad.c:36-39), so severity is 'none' for us, but this is a load-bearing fact for anyone considering disabling/removing the xone package without restoring xpad's paddle-capable entry — doing so would silently break the chatpad-shift feature. Device class: input/joystick (xpad) / USB HID composite (xone). Vendor: Microsoft.

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5523 — `if (input[n].vid == 0x045e && input[n].pid == 0x0b00) input[n].quirk = QUIRK_SHIFT;` with source comment 'XBox Elite 2 controller, use paddles as a special shift for chatpad accessory. Works only through cable or USB dongle.'; consumed at input.cpp:5810 — `if (input[dev].quirk == QUIRK_SHIFT && cfg.xbe2_shift && ev.code == cfg.xbe2_shift) xbe2_shift = ev.value;`. This is a REAL, previously-unflagged coupling: it fires on the raw evdev VID:PID reported by whichever driver binds the device (evdev EVIOCGID reflects the physical USB descriptor, not the matching driver's ID-table entry), so it is satisfied by xone in our build even though xpad's entry for this PID is disabled.

author: zakk4223

superseded_by: hid-logitech-hidpp (for G923 Xbox, 046d:c26e) — /mnt/source/linux/drivers/hid/hid-logitech-hidpp.c:4621: `HID_USB_DEVICE(USB_VENDOR_ID_LOGITECH, USB_DEVICE_ID_LOGITECH_G923_XBOX_WHEEL)`; device ID itself defined /mnt/source/linux/drivers/hid/hid-ids.h:905 `#define USB_DEVICE_ID_LOGITECH_G923_XBOX_WHEEL 0xc26e`. CONFIG_HID_LOGITECH_DJ=y confirmed set in board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:313 (selects CONFIG_HID_LOGITECH_HIDPP by Kconfig default, correctly absent from the savedefconfig-style linux.config as a selected/default symbol).

forward_port.conflicts: The fork's new-lg4ff rewrite (introduced in commit 8a100f2ed) replaces hid-lg4ff.c wholesale with berarma/new-lg4ff, an out-of-tree hrtimer-driven force-feedback scheduler. This rewrite is not carried forward to 6.18; we use vanilla hid-lg4ff which has a different architecture (input_ff_memless). The commit is an update to that out-of-tree driver and does not apply to vanilla 6.18.

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech G923 racing wheel variants (PlayStation mode: 046d:c267, native mode: 046d:c266) will not bind to lg4ff driver. G923 PlayStation users will get basic joystick functionality via hid-generic (steering, pedals, buttons work) but no force feedback, range control, or mode switching. G923 Xbox variant (046d:c26e) is unaffected—already fully supported upstream by hid-logitech-hidpp.

notes:
DELIBERATE OMISSION. Detailed reasoning in patch-provenance.md §9.3:

1. **Architectural mismatch**: Commit 8a100f2ed introduced berarma/new-lg4ff, an out-of-tree hrtimer-driven rewrite. We carry vanilla hid-lg4ff instead. This commit updates that vendored fork driver, which is not in our 6.18 build.

2. **The 32-bit rumble/FF fix is not applicable to 6.18**: The diff changes `parameters[i].k1 = div_s64(...)` — gain-scaling code that exists only in the out-of-tree rewrite. Vanilla 6.18 hid-lg4ff has no parameters, k1/k2, gain scaling, div_s64, or hrtimer. FF scaling is done by input_ff_memless in the kernel input core. There is no bug to fix in vanilla 6.18.

3. **G923 device support requires untestable code**: Adding G923 device IDs (0xc266, 0xc267) to hid-lg4ff would require ~120 lines of multimode/mode-switch code in the vanilla driver to be useful. This code:
   - Handles PS-mode boot (c267) and switches to native mode (c266) via vendor command (report ID 0x30, payload f8 09 07 01 01 00 00)
   - Requires detection via bcdDevice mask (0xff00 & 0x3800) — a magic number with no public documentation
   - Has never run on any wheel (proof-of-concept only)
   - Has a hard-fail hazard: if c267 is added to lg_devices[] but the mode switch fails or device does not match bcdDevice, the wheel binds to lg_devices but lg4ff_init() fails, leaving it with NO driver at all (hid-generic is blocked by lg_devices[] match). This is worse than the current state.

4. **User impact is acceptable**: G923 PlayStation users currently get hid-generic (joystick, no FF). Carrying this code might LOSE that graceful fallback if the hard-fail triggers. The bet we are not making.

5. **G923 Xbox is already fully supported upstream**: CONFIG_HID_LOGITECH_HIDPP=y (selected by CONFIG_HID_LOGITECH_DJ=y) binds 046d:c26e in mainline and provides full FF support. No regression for Xbox users.

6. **Upstream status**: Mainline 6.18 has no G923-PS support. This code is original to the fork's rewrite and never landed in mainline. Carrying a 120-line untestable hard-fail hazard in a forward-port is outside the project's risk tolerance. Full recipe for a future contributor with hardware is documented in patch-provenance.md §9.3.

sonnet-verified: (1) G923-Xbox-via-hidpp claim now carries a file:line citation (dependencies.superseded_by, added — was previously bare prose with no citation). Re-derived against the LIVE checkout per grounding-contract pilot lessons rather than copied from patch-provenance.md: the doc's own §9.3 cites `hid-logitech-hidpp.c:4669`, but `grep -n G923_XBOX_WHEEL /mnt/source/linux/drivers/hid/hid-logitech-hidpp.c` shows the actual binding at line 4621 in this checkout — used the live-derived line rather than the doc's (source-drift between the doc's checkout and this one, not an error in either). (2) §9.3 hard-fail rationale cross-checked against patch-provenance.md:1358-1442 and found accurately represented: the 'hid-generic blocked once c267 matches lg_devices[], then lg4ff_init() hard-fails leaving no driver bound at all' mechanism, the bcdDevice magic-number gate (mask 0xff00, result 0x3800), and the 'G923 Xbox already fully supported, G923 PS gets hid-generic joystick-only, nothing to fix in vanilla for the 32-bit rumble diff' conclusions all match the source doc's substance. Disposition=dropped-deliberate confirmed correct and well-justified; this is a case where equivalence=contradicted legitimately does NOT imply carry-candidate because the record's own extensive reasoning already treats it as a carry candidate and documents why carrying is actively worse than not (the pilot-lesson heuristic's intent is satisfied, not violated). | KNOWN LIMITATION (present-day): the Logitech G923 PlayStation variant (046d:c266/c267) has no force feedback on this build — steering/pedals/buttons work via hid-generic; the G923 Xbox variant and all G29/G27/G25-family wheels are fully supported by mainline hid-lg4ff/hidpp. Deliberate: carrying the new-lg4ff rewrite carries an untestable hard-fail probe hazard (patch-provenance.md §9.3).

notes: Commit fixes Makefiles for out-of-tree Realtek wireless drivers (rtl8812au, rtl8821au, rtl8821cu, rtl88x2bu) that are vendored in the MiSTer kernel fork. The changes replace $(src) with $(srctree)/$(src) include paths, which is a standard fix for proper out-of-tree module path resolution. However, all four driver symbols (CONFIG_RTL8812AU, CONFIG_RTL8821AU, CONFIG_RTL8821CU, CONFIG_RTL88X2BU) are intentionally disabled in board/mister/de10nano/linux.config — the kernel drivers are not built. These devices are covered via: (1) BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU and BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR for 11ac chips without mainline USB drivers; (2) mainline rtw88_8821cu/rtw88_8822bu for newer chips (commit 4cf2fc7 'WiFi: mainline-first drivers', ADR 0016, disabled rtl88x2bu due to WPA3 failures with status_code=1 in out-of-tree version). As the kernel-tree drivers are not enabled in the config, this makefile fix produces no user-visible effect if removed. patch-provenance.md correctly classifies this commit as part of the 're-source' group — these drivers should have their own BR2 packages or mainline alternatives rather than being carried in the kernel tree. sonnet-verified: `grep -n "^CONFIG_RTL8812AU\|^CONFIG_RTL8821AU\|^CONFIG_RTL8821CU\|^CONFIG_RTL88X2BU"` against board/mister/de10nano/linux.config returns zero matches (not even '# ... is not set' lines) — precision note: these symbols are absent rather than merely disabled, because the vendored driver directories that define their Kconfig entries are not part of our 6.18 kernel source tree at all (never forward-ported in-tree; superseded entirely by the BR2 packages/mainline drivers named in superseded_by). Net effect (severity=none) is unchanged by this distinction. Package/mainline supersession list confirmed precise against configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:481/488 (CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m, CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m).

The Buildroot package approach is superior for 6.18 reconciliation: (1) avoids vendoring out-of-tree code in the kernel; (2) enables independent driver updates without full kernel rebuilds; (3) upstream fork (dlundqvist) already includes all MiSTer's local enhancements (rumble support, Elite-2 paddle support with PADDLE_ELITE/PADDLE_ELITE2_4X/PADDLE_ELITE2_510 firmware-version cases, per-PID firmware naming, sysfs software pairing attribute). Disposition: dropped-deliberate in favor of re-sourcing via package/xone. sonnet-verified: correct — vendored commit's own SPDX/copyright headers (GPL-2.0-or-later, Severin von Wnuck, medusalix/xone) confirmed against the fork's git history at 4ddd8ec3d; the 7-module Makefile list (xone-wired/xone-dongle/xone-gip-bus/xone-gip-common/xone-gip-gamepad/xone-gip-headset/xone-gip-chatpad) confirmed exactly; JOYSTICK_XONE absent from vanilla 6.18.38 confirmed. impact.effect_if_absent: Fork's defconfig lacks NFS/SUNRPC/LOCKD configuration; no effect on our 6.18 build as Buildroot uses custom linux.config

notes: GROUPED COMMIT: This commit is part of a 11-commit defconfig group in patch-provenance.md (5391b8171 + 10 others). Patch-provenance marks it 'config — feeds P1.3' with no associated patch file. All CONFIG_* symbols this commit added exist in the fork's 5.15 base and, where still-named, in vanilla 6.18.38 Kconfig. Fork's defconfig is not carried in Buildroot patches; Buildroot uses custom linux.config at board/mister/de10nano/linux.config. | sonnet-verified: DISPOSITION CORRECTED not-evaluated -> dropped-deliberate; the original 'not setting NFS_V3/SUNRPC/LOCKD' framing was MISLEADING and is now annotated per instruction. fs/nfs/Kconfig (vanilla 6.18) shows 'config NFS_FS ... select CRC32 / select LOCKD / select SUNRPC / select NFS_COMMON' — so NFS_FS=y (which IS explicit in our linux.config) force-selects LOCKD, SUNRPC, and NFS_COMMON regardless of whether they appear as separate lines. fs/Kconfig shows 'config LOCKD ... select GRACE_PERIOD' (so GRACE_PERIOD also auto-follows) and 'config NFS_V3 ... default y' (NFS_V3 needs no explicit line at all — it defaults on whenever NFS_FS is enabled and not overridden). net/sunrpc/Kconfig shows 'config RPCSEC_GSS_KRB5 ... depends on SUNRPC && CRYPTO ... default y ... select SUNRPC_GSS' — so the Kerberos GSS stack also auto-enables once SUNRPC+CRYPTO are present. PROOF, not just static reading: the actual post-olddefconfig resolved kernel .config at /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config (lines 3941-3971) shows NFS_FS=y, NFS_V2=y, NFS_V3=y, LOCKD=y, LOCKD_V4=y, GRACE_PERIOD=y, NFS_COMMON=y, SUNRPC=y, SUNRPC_GSS=y, RPCSEC_GSS_KRB5=y, RPCSEC_GSS_KRB5_ENCTYPES_AES_SHA1=y — ALL present and =y in the actual build despite most having NO line in our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config fragment (only NFS_FS/NFS_V2/NFS_V4/NFS_V4_1/NFS_V4_2/NFS_V4_2_READ_PLUS are explicit there, grep-confirmed). CONCLUSION: there is no real feature-loss gap — NFSv2 and NFSv3 client support (the two versions this specific commit's diff is chiefly about) are both fully present in our actual build via the select/default chain rooted at NFS_FS=y. SEPARATE CORRECTION: CONFIG_SUNRPC_DISABLE_INSECURE_ENCTYPES is NOT a 'fork-only custom option' as previously claimed — it genuinely existed upstream from Feb 2019 (commit fe9a270519c7 'SUNRPC: Add build option to disable support for insecure enctypes', present in the 5.15 base the fork forked from) until it was replaced upstream by commit dfe9a123451a 'SUNRPC: Enable rpcsec_gss_krb5.ko to be built without CRYPTO_DES' (2023-01-15, predates this fork commit's 2023-04-23 date) with the per-enctype RPCSEC_GSS_KRB5_ENCTYPES_AES_SHA1/CAMELLIA/AES_SHA2 opt-in model (AES_SHA1 default y). It is a removed/superseded-upstream symbol, not a fork invention; the stale '# ... is not set' line in the fork's defconfig is simply inert cruft that any Kconfig tool silently ignores for an unknown symbol. Grep confirms it is genuinely absent from /mnt/source/linux's net/sunrpc/Kconfig at the checked-out v6.18.38 HEAD. This correction does not change the disposition. agrees_with_provenance_doc set true (the doc's 'config — feeds P1.3' framing is accurate; P1.3 did in fact resolve this).

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: CFG80211 and MAC80211 frameworks would be compiled as built-in kernel components instead of loadable modules. No functional change to wireless capability, but increases kernel size and affects boot-time dependency loading. Wireless drivers that depend on these frameworks would still function identically.

notes:
This defconfig change from the MiSTer 5.15 fork aligns the fork's wireless configuration with stock kernel behavior by converting CFG80211 and MAC80211 from built-in (=y) to modular (=m). Both options remain tristate in vanilla 6.18.38 Kconfig, supporting either configuration. Not carried as a patch; instead, the same configuration goal is maintained through board/mister/de10nano/linux.config (currently CONFIG_CFG80211=m, CONFIG_MAC80211=m), which was derived from stock kernel behavior. Stock 5.15.1 multi_v7_defconfig already had these as modules, confirming this commit brought the fork into alignment with upstream defaults. The modular approach reduces kernel footprint and allows loading wireless frameworks only when needed.

DELIBERATELY DROPPED FROM 6.18 KERNEL because: (1) xone is now packaged as a Buildroot out-of-tree kernel-module package (package/xone) rather than vendored in the kernel source; (2) the dlundqvist/xone fork (f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280) pinned in package/xone/xone.mk already contains an identical or more sophisticated paddle implementation — verified: device IDs (0x045e:0x0b00, 0x045e:0x02e3), firmware thresholds (0x04FF, 0x050A), and PaddleCapability enum all match the kernel commit byte-for-byte; (3) no separate carry patch is needed. CONFIRMED with source-grep of packaged tarball (grep -n PADDLE_ in dlundqvist gamepad.c yields lines 119–254, matching kernel commit's paddle enums, query functions, and input reporting). This commit is explicitly documented in patch-provenance.md §3.5 as one of 4 MiSTer-local deltas (rumble fix, Elite-2 paddles, per-PID firmware, sysfs pairing) that turned out unnecessary as separate patches because the dlundqvist fork independently grew equivalent functionality. Zero compat patches needed for 6.18. xone GIP driver does not exist in vanilla mainline 6.18 (grep -rl gip_driver drivers/ in vanilla → nothing), so 'upstream' equivalence is inapplicable. CORRECTION (sonnet audit): the original userspace_coupling claim (coupled=true, citing Main_MiSTer/joymapping.cpp:220 map_paddle_btn() and BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPR2) was a false citation and has been removed. map_paddle_btn() is unrelated to Xbox Elite rear paddles: it is part of Main_MiSTer's core-defined button-naming system (joymapping.cpp:1 'lookup information on known controllers'), gated behind QUIRK_PDSP/QUIRK_MSSP (input.cpp:1441-1445), which only activates for MiSTer's own 'MiSTer-S1'/'MiSTer-A1' analog paddle/spinner peripherals (input.cpp:5463-5471) — an unrelated device class that happens to share the English word 'paddle'. grep for BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPR across all of /mnt/source/Main_MiSTer returns zero hits. Elite paddle buttons surface as ordinary extra EV_KEY buttons (BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5-8 fallback on kernels <6.17, BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPR2 on newer, per driver/gamepad.c's own #ifndef fallback block) that Main_MiSTer's generic per-core button-mapping UI picks up like any other joystick button, with no xone-specific code path. author: atrac17

superseded_by: vanilla 6.18.38 drivers/hid/hid-lg4ff.c (in-tree, compiled into hid-logitech.ko) — identical device-table membership to this commit for every ID it touches (WINGMAN_FG/FFG, WHEEL/DF-EX, MOMO_WHEEL, MOMO_WHEEL2, DFP, G25, DFGT, G27, G29, WII_WHEEL); FF_CONSTANT+FF_AUTOCENTER retained, which is the entirety of what Main_MiSTer uses

forward_port.conflicts: The commit is a ~2000-line wholesale replacement of hid-lg4ff.c with the out-of-tree berarma/new-lg4ff hrtimer-driven rewrite; vanilla 6.18.38 is still the pre-2019 architecture (no hrtimer, no LG4FF_VERSION, no per-slot effect scheduler). A forward-port would mean carrying an entire third-party driver rewrite in-tree, for a capability set (FF_SPRING/DAMPER/FRICTION condition effects, FF_PERIODIC waveforms) that Main_MiSTer does not currently consume for any of this commit's wheel IDs. forward_port.effort: high, and currently low-value: no consumer exists for the added capability

affected_hardware: 046d:c24f (G29_WHEEL — MISSING from first-pass device_ids despite being named in its prose)
impact.effect_if_absent: CORRECTED (first-pass claim was wrong). Vanilla 6.18.38's in-tree hid-lg4ff.c (compiled into hid-logitech.ko via hid-lg.c) continues to bind ALL of this commit's wheel IDs — WINGMAN_FFG, WHEEL/DF-EX, MOMO_WHEEL, MOMO_WHEEL2, DFP, G25, DFGT, G27, G29, WII_WHEEL — with the LG_FF4 quirk (hid-lg.c:859-923); none of them fall back to hid-generic. Vanilla supplies FF_CONSTANT + FF_AUTOCENTER, the same two effect types the pre-rewrite driver had. Main_MiSTer's ENTIRE Logitech-wheel force-feedback implementation (input.cpp:4958-5003) writes exactly one FF primitive for these wheels — an EV_FF/FF_AUTOCENTER event (input.cpp:4996-4999) — which vanilla's lg4ff_set_autocenter_default()/_ffex() fully services via input_ff_create_memless(). So for G29/G27/G25/DFP/DFGT/WHEEL(DF-EX), dropping this commit changes NOTHING observable on a MiSTer: steering, pedals, buttons, autocentering force, and range control (set_wheel_range via the driver's real_id/range sysfs attrs, also present in vanilla) all continue to work identically. What IS genuinely lost is the extended effect set this commit's new-lg4ff rewrite adds — FF_SPRING/FF_DAMPER/FF_FRICTION condition effects and FF_PERIODIC/FF_SINE/FF_SQUARE/FF_TRIANGLE/FF_SAW_UP/FF_SAW_DOWN/FF_RAMP waveforms, driven by an hrtimer multi-slot scheduler (fork blob hid-lg4ff.c:161-175, :918-921) — none of which Main_MiSTer or any MiSTer core currently invokes (grep of input.cpp for FF_SPRING/FF_DAMPER/FF_FRICTION/FF_PERIODIC finds exactly one hit for FF_SPRING, and it is NOT in the Logitech branch — see userspace_coupling below). This capability loss is real but currently inert: it would only matter to a hand-written evdev client driving per-frame condition/periodic effects, which does not exist on MiSTer today. Momo (0xc295) and WII_WHEEL (0xc29c) additionally have ZERO Main_MiSTer-side wheel-quirk wiring at all (Momo's case is explicitly commented out in input.cpp's switch, WII_WHEEL isn't referenced at all), so there is no FF coupling for those two PIDs in either direction, with or without this commit. NOTE ON SCOPE: this commit (8a100f2ed) by itself adds NO new device IDs — its device table (fork blob lines 221-232) has the identical membership to vanilla's. The G923 PlayStation/PC variant (046d:c267/native c266) that patch-provenance.md §9.3 identifies as the one real regression is added by a DIFFERENT, later commit (43c52e9ef, the '32-bit rumble fix' commit provenance.md groups with this one under the '0021' patch slot) and is out of scope for this single-commit record per the grouping-is-the-failure-mode rule; Main_MiSTer has zero references to 0xc266/0xc267/0xc26e/0xc26d anywhere (grepped input.cpp and the whole Main_MiSTer tree), so there is no userspace coupling to that variant either way.

license_provenance: Copyright (c) 2019 Bernat Arlandis <berarma@hotmail.com> added to file header; original copyright (c) 2010 Simon Wood retained. Vendored copy of the out-of-tree berarma/new-lg4ff project (hrtimer-driven memless-FF multi-slot scheduler). License: GPL-2.0-or-later (matches kernel SPDX header).
notes: SONNET ESCALATION VERIFICATION — the first-pass record's headline claim ('force feedback stops working completely... revert to hid-generic driver... critical regression') is FACTUALLY WRONG and is corrected here. Root cause of the error: (1) input.cpp:5029 ('fef.type = FF_SPRING') was misread as the Logitech-wheel code path; it is actually the Fanatec-wheel branch (vid==0x0eb7, input.cpp:5006-5051), an entirely separate driver family. Main_MiSTer's real Logitech-wheel FF code (input.cpp:4959-5003) uses only FF_AUTOCENTER, which vanilla hid-lg4ff.c fully supports via the standard set_autocenter/input_ff_create_memless mechanism (hid-lg4ff.c:1346-1369). (2) The claim that these wheels 'revert to hid-generic' is contradicted by hid-lg.c:859-923: every wheel ID this commit touches (WINGMAN_FFG, WHEEL/DF-EX, MOMO_WHEEL, MOMO_WHEEL2, DFP, G25, DFGT, G27, G29, WII_WHEEL) is registered in lg_devices[] with the LG_FF4 quirk, and hid-lg.c:836 unconditionally calls lg4ff_init() for LG_FF4 devices on probe — hid_generic_match() never gets a chance at these PIDs. (3) The record's own provenance_doc_ref pointed at patch-provenance.md §9.3, but the record's conclusions directly CONTRADICT that section's own table (patch-provenance.md ~1420-1424), which already states: 'G29, G27, G25, DFP, DFGT, Momo, DF-EX, Wingman | ... | mainline hid-lg4ff — FF, range and mode-switching, all of it, from the maintained in-tree driver ... Arguably a reliability gain.' The first-pass record set agrees_with_provenance_doc=true while asserting the opposite of what that section says; this correction makes the record's conclusion actually match the provenance doc's (already-correct) finding. (4) Device-ID list bug: 046d:c24f (G29_WHEEL) was named in the first-pass prose ('G29/G27/G25/...') but OMITTED from the device_ids/affected_hardware arrays; added here. (5) SCOPE DISCIPLINE: this commit (8a100f2ed) alone adds no new device IDs — G923 support (the one wheel variant that patch-provenance.md §9.3 correctly identifies as genuinely losing FF, falling back to hid-generic) comes from a separate commit, 43c52e9ef, which patch-provenance.md groups with this one under the '0021-hid-lg4ff-g923.patch' slot name but which is a distinct SHA not covered by this record. Per the worker instructions' 'never group commits' rule, that regression belongs to 43c52e9ef's own record, not this one. HONEST REGRESSION RESTATED: for this commit in isolation, retained = full basic FF (autocentering) + steering/pedals/buttons/range-control for every wheel ID it touches, because vanilla's older in-tree driver already does exactly what Main_MiSTer needs; lost = extended condition/periodic FF effect types and hrtimer-driven slot scheduling, which is real but has zero current consumer in Main_MiSTer or any MiSTer core. Severity corrected from 'feature-loss (critical)' to 'none' for MiSTer's actual current behavior; disposition (dropped-deliberate) is left unchanged since the underlying provenance-doc reasoning for not carrying the third-party rewrite wholesale is sound, just for different reasons than the first-pass record gave. sonnet-verified: contradicted — first-pass severity/failure-mode/userspace-coupling claims were disproven by direct source citation (hid-lg.c LG_FF4 table, hid-lg4ff.c autocenter wiring, and input.cpp's actual branch structure), and the corrected conclusion aligns with patch-provenance.md §9.3's own (previously ignored) table.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch

impact.effect_if_absent: The deletion of vanilla 5.15 exfat driver files has no effect if not carried, because vanilla 6.18 retains the exfat driver (same 14 files: fs/exfat/*.c|h with similar structure). Symlink support is provided separately via carried patch 0031 (exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch), which implements Samsung-compatible symlinks on vanilla exfat. FAT12/16/32 support is provided via vanilla vfat (CONFIG_VFAT_FS), not the Samsung driver.

notes: This commit was part of the fork's strategy to replace vanilla 5.15 exfat (14 files, no symlink support) with the out-of-tree Samsung/exfat-nofuse driver (20+ files, with symlinks for exFAT and FAT12/16/32 support). Patch-provenance.md §N1 and §3.8 document this replacement. The commit is NOT carried in our 6.18 build. Our approach (ADR 0010 + ADR 0019): (1) vanilla 6.18 exfat persists unchanged (same files as deleted here); (2) carried patch 0031 adds Samsung-compatible symlinks to vanilla exfat only; (3) vanilla vfat (CONFIG_VFAT_FS) handles FAT12/16/32 encoding via CONFIG_FAT_DEFAULT_UTF8=y. This trades the Samsung driver's multi-filesystem unified symlink support for maintainability via two vanilla upstream drivers. No exFAT functionality is lost (symlinks via patch 0031, encoding via mount options). FAT12/16/32 symlinks remain unsupported (design decision in ADR 0019). The fork's intent was to REMOVE vanilla exfat to enable Samsung driver integration; our 6.18 build deliberately KEEPS vanilla exfat and adds targeted symlink support instead via a separate carried patch — that is the reason for dropped-deliberate. upstream.equivalence corrected from 'contradicted' to null: this commit is a pure deletion (added=0, no vanilla_file_line/vanilla_quote grounding), so the equivalence enum (which grades behavioral equivalence of functionality a commit ADDS) does not apply — there is no added behavior for vanilla to be equivalent to or contradict, and 'contradicted' would incorrectly flag this dropped-deliberate deletion as a carry candidate per the pilot-lesson heuristic, which is wrong here. sonnet-verified: equivalence-flag semantics fixed per grounding contract (no upstream claim without a quote); disposition=dropped-deliberate and supersession by 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch confirmed correct.

notes: Four config changes: (1) CONFIG_JOYSTICK_XPAD y→m: exists in vanilla 6.18 (drivers/input/joystick/Kconfig:294), but OUR linux.config still has =y (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:238) — a genuine, unresolved config-parity divergence. (3) CONFIG_HID_GOOGLE_STADIA_FF reordered (line 2642→2646): exists in vanilla 6.18, no functional change, both configs have =y — trivially matched. (4) CONFIG_PAHOLE_HAS_SPLIT_BTF=y removed: correct decision — in vanilla it is def_bool (auto-derived from PAHOLE_VERSION>=119, lib/Kconfig.debug:392), should never be manually set in defconfig; confirmed absent from both our linux.config fragment and the resolved build (this build's pahole reports <119). | sonnet-verified: MAJOR CORRECTIONS. (a) userspace_coupling citation was conflating two distinct kernel mechanisms — see corrected userspace_coupling block; input.cpp:5867's BTN_Z remap belongs to the wired/RF XInput path (dependency c035c21c0/JOYSTICK_XPAD), not this record's Bluetooth CONFIG_HID_VADER4 driver (dependency b1b168eb6), which self-remaps at the kernel level and has no Main_MiSTer-specific citable dependency (grep-confirmed zero Vader/BTN_GRIPL/0xd7d7 references in Main_MiSTer). (b) JOYSTICK_XPAD drift-origin traced: `git blame board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:238` shows CONFIG_JOYSTICK_XPAD=y was set by commit 3dfeaebb ('P1.3: derive the 6.18.38 kernel config...', Michael C. Ferguson, 2026-07-12) — this POSTDATES the fork's 2026-04-17 y->m change (this very commit, 97a398176), yet the commit message for 3dfeaebb describes deriving the baseline from the stock config snapshot and makes no mention of XPAD. docs/stock-inventory/stock-linux.config:1643 (the aged, ~15-month-old snapshot referenced in provenance Q10) ALSO has CONFIG_JOYSTICK_XPAD=y — the same aged-baseline-inheritance root cause already flagged for CONFIG_MACVLAN in the pilot record (f0fb626ac). No evidence was found of a deliberate 'keep it built-in' engineering decision; this reads as inherited drift, not intent. One plausible-but-UNCONFIRMED technical consideration for a future decision: 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch's GIP-controller exclusion logic (so the out-of-tree xone kmod package claims those devices) runs inside xpad_probe(), and a built-in (=y) driver binds during kernel boot before any userspace module loading, whereas a module (=m) only binds once loaded — this could matter for boot-time device-claiming order relative to xone, but the exclusion check itself is correct regardless of module/built-in status, and no direct evidence ties our =y choice to this reasoning. Reporting both the evidence (inherited drift) and the open technical question honestly, without asserting either as settled. CORRECTED: this remap is gated on vid==0x45E (Microsoft), which is the WIRED/RF-dongle XInput path fed by fork commit c035c21c0 (xpad MAP_VADER4, carried in 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch) — NOT this record's CONFIG_HID_VADER4=m driver, which is a Bluetooth-only D-Input path (drivers/hid/hid-vader4.c, id_table HID_BLUETOOTH_DEVICE(0xd7d7,0x0041)) that self-remaps KEY_ENTER->BTN_Z etc INSIDE THE KERNEL before events ever reach userspace. Exhaustive grep of Main_MiSTer for 'Vader', '0xd7d7', 'BTN_GRIPL', 'BTN_GRIPR', 'BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY3', 'BTN_C' found ZERO matches — there is no Vader-specific or Bluetooth-D-Input-specific code in Main_MiSTer; those codes are consumed only via Main_MiSTer's generic joystick/button pipeline once the kernel driver emits them as standard evdev codes.

package/rtl88x2bu (morrownr/88x2bu-20210702 @ fecac340fb117eb979f4bb6d28e29730384c382b, merge titled 'fix-kernel-6.18-compatibility') -- pin verified but INACTIVE: # BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU is not set (mister_de10nano_defconfig:580). RTL8812BU/RTL8822BU is actually covered by the MAINLINE in-tree driver: CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m (drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8822bu.c; confirmed =m at output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config:1705 and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:484). Switched deliberately per mister_de10nano_defconfig:571-579: mainline's mac80211 path gets WPA3/SAE/PMF right where out-of-tree 88x2bu advertised SAE+CMAC but failed WPA3-only association (status_code=1, hardware-verified per that comment).

impact.effect_if_absent: No impact to 6.18 build. In-kernel Realtek drivers are not used; they are disabled in CONFIG. Coverage is split: RTL8812AU and RTL8811AU/RTL8821AU are replaced by external BR2 packages sourced from morrownr (package/rtl8812au, package/rtl8821au-morrownr); RTL8811CU/RTL8821CU and RTL8812BU/RTL8822BU are instead replaced by MAINLINE in-kernel drivers (CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m, CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m) under the defconfig's 'mainline-first' policy (mister_de10nano_defconfig P3.1/v9 comment block) -- the morrownr rtl8821cu-morrownr/rtl88x2bu packages this commit's diff touches are sourced (Config.in present) but explicitly disabled (# ... is not set) to avoid a USB-ID bind conflict with the mainline drivers.

notes:
In-kernel Realtek drivers in MiSTer's 5.15 Linux fork (rtl8812au, rtl8821au, rtl8821cu, rtl88x2bu) are NOT used in our 6.18 build — they are completely superseded, but NOT uniformly by external BR2 packages (see CORRECTION below). The in-kernel versions are not enabled in our kernel CONFIG (no CONFIG_RTL8812AU/8821AU/8821CU/8822BU entries in linux.config), and the Kconfig options for these drivers do not exist in vanilla 6.18. Commit 993b82e (Mar 2023) updated these drivers in the stock MiSTer 5.15 fork with two types of changes: (1) debug output reductions (CONFIG_RTW_DEBUG=y→n, CONFIG_PROC_DEBUG=y→n in Makefiles) and (2) cosmetic cleanups (whitespace, trailing lines). Userspace coupling via cfg80211/nl80211 is preserved across the transition regardless of which of the two replacement paths (out-of-tree package vs. mainline rtw88) ends up binding a given chip.

CORRECTION (sonnet-verify): The original 'superseded_by' list named all four morrownr packages (rtl8812au, rtl8821au-morrownr, rtl8821cu-morrownr, rtl88x2bu) as THE replacement, and impact.effect_if_absent claimed uniform BR2-package coverage. Checking the live mister_de10nano_defconfig (not just the package/ directory, which only proves the packages are *sourced*, not *enabled*) shows this is half true: BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y and BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR=y are active, but BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821CU_MORROWNR and BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU are both explicitly `# ... is not set` (defconfig:570,580). Those two chip families (RTL8811CU/8821CU and RTL8812BU/8822BU) are instead covered by MAINLINE in-kernel drivers under a documented 'mainline-first' policy (defconfig:543-580, P3.1/v9): CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m and CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m, both confirmed set in output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config. The defconfig's own comment explains WHY for the 8822BU case specifically: the out-of-tree 88x2bu advertised WPA3/SAE support but failed WPA3-only association on real hardware (status_code=1), while mainline rtw88's mac80211 path works correctly. This same staleness exists in docs/patch-provenance.md line 389, which also lists all six re-sourced package names (including rtl8821cu, rtl88x2bu) without the mainline-first caveat -- both that doc and this record predate the P3.1/v9 defconfig policy shift. Device_ids also amended: the original list (0x8812/0x881a/0x881b/0x881c/0x0811/0x0821/0x0823) covers only the RTL8812/RTL8821 'Default ID' entries shared across the fork's rtl8821cu and rtl88x2bu usb_intf.c ID tables; none of those IDs are actually RTL8822B-specific. Added 0xb812/0xb82c/0xb81a (RTL8822B 'Default ID' entries, confirmed in the fork's rtl88x2bu/os_dep/linux/usb_intf.c under #ifdef CONFIG_RTL8822B) so affected_hardware's claimed RTL8822BU coverage has a matching device ID.

Net effect on disposition: unchanged (dropped-deliberate is still correct -- this commit's diff does not survive to our tree either way) and severity/failure_mode unchanged (none/silent -- the chips are covered either way, just by a different subsystem than originally recorded). The pin verification requested by this audit task passed cleanly: all four package .mk files were checked directly (VERSION hash, SITE morrownr repo/tag, and the commit-message rationale strings) and match verbatim what's quoted in the original superseded_by list.

sonnet-verified: PASS with substantive correction (superseded_by/impact/device_ids amended; two of the four claimed morrownr packages are actually inactive, real coverage for those two chip families is mainline in-kernel rtw88, not BR2 out-of-tree packages).

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch

impact.effect_if_absent: Symlinks on /media/fat would not be preserved when copied through Windows or other OS; would revert to 0x0040 encoding incompatible with future stock kernels

notes: This commit is part of the MiSTer fork's integrated Samsung out-of-tree exfat driver (v5.15 branch), not a carried patch. It changed the exfat symlink on-disk encoding from 0x0040 to 0x0004 (ATTR_SYSTEM), enabling Windows-compatible preservation of symlinks during file copies. For our 6.18 build: (1) The fork's Samsung driver is not carried (dropped per ADR 0010), replaced by vanilla exfat. (2) Symlink functionality is re-implemented via carried patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, which uses the SAME on-disk encoding (EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK = EXFAT_ATTR_SYSTEM = 0x0004). (3) On-disk format compatibility with stock-written symlinks is VERIFIED: both stock (v5.15 with this commit) and our patch 0031 (6.18) read/write 0x0004 as the primary symlink marker, with backward compatibility for 0x0040 (EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK_OLD). (4) Main_MiSTer actively resolves symlinks on /media/fat (file_io.cpp:1593, added Jan 2019), so this feature is user-visible and relied upon. (5) GROUPED ROW: patch-provenance.md §3.8 Class G line 405 groups this commit with 5 others (8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27, 858322ce6, 5220d6686, 7f7148c1f) under one table row, violating the grounding contract that each commit is analyzed independently. sonnet-verified: on-disk-format claim re-derived and CONFIRMED against live checkouts — vanilla /mnt/source/linux/fs/exfat/exfat_raw.h:69 `#define EXFAT_ATTR_SYSTEM 0x0004`; carried patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch defines `#define EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK EXFAT_ATTR_SYSTEM` (i.e. 0x0004) and `#define EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK_OLD 0x0040`, exactly matching this commit's 0x0004/0x0040 split. Main_MiSTer/file_io.cpp:1593 `else if (de->d_type == DT_LNK || de->d_type == DT_REG)` confirmed present (record cites :1593, matches exactly). Disposition, severity=feature-loss, and userspace_coupling all confirmed correct.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §N1 line 180, §3.8 Class G line 405 (GROUPED row)
subject: exfat: use ATTR_SYSTEM as symlink flag to preserve links while copying on Windows or other OS.

author: Fabio DL

impact.effect_if_absent: Force feedback (rumble) support for SmartJoy PLUS PS2/USB adapter would be unavailable; userspace input layer would not detect FF_RUMBLE capability

notes: VOCABULARY CORRECTION (per instructions' kconfig rule, grounding-contract item 5): this was previously marked disposition='dropped-upstream', which implies vanilla-source development superseded the fork's change -- wrong frame for a pure kconfig toggle. CONFIG_SMARTJOYPLUS_FF is not something that 'arrived upstream' after the fork's 5.15 base; it's a static Kconfig bool that has existed unchanged for years (confirmed present, unchanged wording, at vanilla Kconfig:1188-1193) and was never absent from either tree's *source*. The fork commit itself is nothing more than flipping one line in the fork's own arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig (`# CONFIG_SMARTJOYPLUS_FF is not set` -> `CONFIG_SMARTJOYPLUS_FF=y`, confirmed via `git show 9f59d13d5`) -- a file we do not carry or track commit-by-commit at all; we maintain our own board/mister/de10nano/linux.config directly. Re-derived per the kconfig rule: does the symbol still exist in 6.18? Yes (Kconfig:1188). Is it set in OUR linux.config? Yes -- confirmed `CONFIG_SMARTJOYPLUS_FF=y` at board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:343 (immediately following CONFIG_HID_SMARTJOYPLUS=y at :342). Stock also ships it `=y` (stock-linux.config:2705). So the desired end-state this commit wanted is already present in our build, achieved independently by our own maintained config rather than by carrying this specific commit -- correct disposition is 'dropped-deliberate' (we deliberately don't carry per-commit defconfig deltas; our linux.config is hand-maintained against stock+fork intent) with equivalence 'equivalent' (same effective setting, not a byte-identical artifact since there's no vanilla commit to diff against -- vanilla_shas is correctly empty, this was never an upstream source change). No linux-patches/*.patch touches SMARTJOYPLUS (grepped all 25 patch files, no hits) -- confirms carried_patch=null is correct; the setting lives purely in our maintained linux.config, not in any patch artifact. Userspace coupling confirmed: Main_MiSTer's input.cpp uses FF_RUMBLE (input.cpp:4881) and FF_SPRING (input.cpp:5029) effects and probes for FF_RUMBLE capability via EVIOCGBIT (input.cpp:5226-5228) -- generic, not SmartJoy-specific. If CONFIG_SMARTJOYPLUS_FF were ever unset, rumble would silently not work but would not cause build/boot failure. NOTE: patch-provenance.md:312 groups this commit with 10 others (including ae9313e22, NFS_FS) under one 'defconfig (11 commits)' row -- a grouped row that should itself be escalated per the instructions (never analyze/report on grouped commits as one unit), even though this record analyzes 9f59d13d5 alone as required.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: GDB debugging helper scripts not available; no effect on kernel binary or runtime behavior. These are optional development/debugging tools.

notes: MiSTer fork deliberately deleted 15 GDB helper scripts (−2,156 lines). These files exist in vanilla 6.18.38 and are generated as part of the kernel build process (always-y targets in scripts/gdb/linux/Makefile). However, they have zero functional impact on the built kernel binary or userspace. They are purely optional debugging aids for developers using GDB to debug the kernel. Our 6.18 build keeps these files (from vanilla 6.18.38) since they do not conflict with anything and provide value as optional debug helpers. No carries exist for these files. Disposition aligns with provenance doc: 'dismiss' and 'do not carry'. CORRECTION (sonnet audit): upstream.equivalence was 'byte-identical'; verified false by diffing the fork's pre-deletion file content (git show a547c18d0~1:scripts/gdb/linux/<file>) against current /mnt/source/linux/scripts/gdb/linux/<file> for all 14 non-__init__.py files. Only lists.py and the trivial 1-line __init__.py are byte-identical; the other 12 (clk.py, config.py, cpus.py, device.py, dmesg.py, genpd.py, modules.py, proc.py, rbtree.py, symbols.py, tasks.py, timerlist.py, utils.py) all differ — expected, since they picked up ~3 years of normal upstream GDB-script development between the fork's 5.15 base and 6.18.38 (e.g. clk.py gained a null-registration-list error check). Downgraded equivalence to 'equivalent' (same purpose, functionally interchangeable, not byte-for-byte) — this does not change the disposition or impact assessment, since severity is 'none' either way (debug-only tooling with zero effect on the built kernel). sonnet-verified: corrected — file list and zero-impact assessment solid; equivalence downgraded from byte-identical to equivalent per direct diff.
subject: remove unused files.

Sample diff, fork's deleted clk.py (pre-deletion, ~5.15-era content) vs /mnt/source/linux/scripts/gdb/linux/clk.py (6.18.38): vanilla gained `if utils.gdb_eval_or_none("clk_root_list") is None: raise gdb.GdbError("No clocks registered")` (76 -> 78 lines) — a normal ~3-years-of-upstream-development delta, not a byte-identical file.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Vanilla MAX_NR_CONSOLES=63 keeps a handful of MAX_NR_CONSOLES-sized arrays at their full compile-time size instead of the fork's 9: vt.c's static `vc_cons[MAX_NR_CONSOLES]` (struct vc = 1 ptr + 1 work_struct, ~40B/entry, ~2.2KB delta), fbcon's static `fb_display[MAX_NR_CONSOLES]` (struct fbcon_display, ~120B/entry, ~6.5KB delta, relevant here since our linux.config sets CONFIG_FRAMEBUFFER_CONSOLE=y) plus two 1-byte con2fb_map[] arrays (negligible), and — the dominant term — tty_io.c's __tty_alloc_driver(), which for the console driver (registered with TTY_DRIVER_REAL_RAW|TTY_DRIVER_RESET_TERMIOS, no TTY_DRIVER_DYNAMIC_ALLOC) unconditionally kcalloc()s a struct tty_port[MAX_NR_CONSOLES] array at boot (con_init(), drivers/tty/vt/vt.c:3893-3894); struct tty_port is a few hundred bytes (tty_bufhead, 2 wait_queue_head_t, 2 mutex, a kfifo, etc.), so 63 vs 9 entries is roughly a 15-20KB delta. Notably that ports[] array is dead weight for vt regardless of MAX_NR_CONSOLES: con_install() (drivers/tty/vt/vt.c) calls tty_port_install(&vc->port, ...) which sets tty->port before tty_init_dev()'s `if (!tty->port) tty->port = driver->ports[idx];` fallback (drivers/tty/tty_io.c:1411-1412) ever runs, so driver->ports[idx] is allocated but never read for consoles. Total eager/static delta across all these arrays is on the order of 25-30KB of permanently-resident kernel RAM from boot, regardless of how many VTs are ever opened — i.e. this is NOT purely a lazy-allocation non-issue. The genuinely lazy part is the big per-console payload: vc_allocate() in drivers/tty/vt/vt.c kzalloc()s struct vc_data (screen buffer, unicode tables, etc.) only for a VT index the first time it's actually opened, so raising the ceiling from 9 to 63 costs nothing there unless something actually opens tty10+. Net: dropping this commit (keeping vanilla's 63) has a real but tiny, boot-time, non-catastrophic memory cost (tens of KB on a board with ~1GB RAM), not zero and not lazy-therefore-free — but still far below any threshold that would change the severity rating.

notes: Commit reduces MAX_NR_CONSOLES from 63 to 9 in the uapi header (include/uapi/linux/vt.h:13, confirmed via `git show` on the fork). Vanilla 6.18.38 retains 63 (include/uapi/linux/vt.h:13, quoted above, confirmed via grep in /mnt/source/linux) — vanilla was never patched to 9 and there is no vanilla_sha to cite; this is purely a fork-local embedded-system trade-off, not a claim of upstream equivalence. Not carried: `grep -rl 'MAX_NR_CONSOLES\|vt\.h'` over board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/*.patch returns nothing — no 00xx patch touches vt.h. Provenance doc explicitly rules DROPPED at patch-provenance.md:397 (§3.7, row F-3: "a UAPI header edit for a few KB, only 3 consoles used... Not carried; no 00xx file") and again in the summary at line 292; TASKS.md P1.9 is the cited authority. Independently re-derived and AGREE with the drop: Main_MiSTer only ever activates VT 1 and VT 2 (video_chvt() call sites above) and never references MAX_NR_CONSOLES, so 9 vs 63 makes no functional difference to MiSTer; keeping vanilla's 63 is the deliberately conservative, zero-risk choice (matches stock, smaller header diff surface) at the cost of a genuine-but-tiny ~25-30KB of eager/static kernel RAM (see impact.effect_if_absent for the breakdown of vc_cons[]/fb_display[]/tty_port[] — the vc_data screen-buffer payload itself IS lazily allocated per open VT via vc_allocate(), so the ceiling alone doesn't scale with real usage the way the record originally implied it might, but it isn't fully cost-free either). failure_mode=silent is correct: no build break, no boot error, no userspace-visible change either way (verified no `#if MAX_NR_CONSOLES > 63` style build guard is tripped by either value in vc_screen.c). CORRECTION FROM FIRST PASS: the prior version of this record set verification.contradiction=true, but that flag (per worker-instructions.md §1/§2) is reserved for a dropped-upstream claim where vanilla turns out to do something different than asserted — it does not apply here, because disposition is dropped-deliberate (not dropped-upstream) and the provenance doc never claimed this was already upstream (its own table marks the "in vanilla?" column "no"). Vanilla keeping MAX_NR_CONSOLES=63 is the expected, intended baseline, not a contradiction of any upstream claim this record makes. Fixed to contradiction=false; disposition, severity, and failure_mode were already correct and are unchanged; effect_if_absent and userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref were expanded with the lazy-vs-eager allocation analysis and precise Main_MiSTer citations. sonnet-verified: corrected (contradiction flag was misapplied to a deliberate-drop record; disposition/severity/failure_mode were already right, impact analysis sharpened with eager-vs-lazy allocation detail).
subject: vt: reduce from 63 to 9 ttys.

upstream.vanilla_quote: #define MAX_NR_CONSOLES	63	/* serial lines start at 64 */

userspace_coupling.interface: Virtual terminal device nodes and VT ioctls, not the MAX_NR_CONSOLES constant itself
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: video.cpp:8,4251 (#include <linux/vt.h>; opens /dev/tty0 as the VT_ACTIVATE/VT_WAITACTIVE control device only — tty0 is the "current VT" alias, not itself a console index); menu.cpp:1353 video_chvt(1), menu.cpp:3372/7011 video_chvt(2) (only VT indices 1 and 2 are ever activated); menu.cpp:3402,7022 (agetty -L tty2). audio.cpp:7 also #includes <linux/vt.h>. No file in Main_MiSTer references the MAX_NR_CONSOLES symbol or opens any VT index >2, so both 9 and 63 satisfy actual usage with wide headroom; the coupling is to the VT ioctl ABI (stable across both values), not to the ceiling.

author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech RumblePad, RumblePad 2, WingMan, and Formula Vibration Feedback Wheel devices lose force-feedback/rumble capability; devices continue to work as input devices without haptic feedback.

notes:
CRITICAL: This commit is ONLY on MiSTer-v5.14 branch, NOT on MiSTer-v5.15 (stock). However, the same kconfig options (CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF=y) ARE enabled in v5.15's defconfig via commit 215e6e662 'Add defconfig' (2021-11-08). This represents a deliberate re-implementation: Sorgelig chose not to cherry-pick this v5.14 commit to v5.15, but instead replicated the functionality when creating the v5.15 defconfig. The commit is NOT listed in patch-provenance.md (which lists 11 defconfig commits at line 312, but not bbeff2c30). Both CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF are standard vanilla kconfig options in 6.18 (drivers/hid/Kconfig). They are enabled in stock-v5.15's linux.config and in our current linux.config. Main_MiSTer has userspace force-feedback support code (input.cpp) that depends on these drivers being present and enabled. Dropping these options would cause silent feature loss (rumble stops working, but devices remain functional as input devices). No upstream carries this specific commit because the options are standard vanilla features available in all versions since before 5.15.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: a95855a70 (socfpga-4.19 dwc2 port — v5.14 only, abandoned at v5.15)

affected_hardware: All systems using dwc2 USB host controller (DE10-Nano, SoCs with Synopsys DesignWare USB2)
impact.effect_if_absent: Entire vanilla dwc2 controller driver removed from v5.14 kernel; no functional effect on this commit alone (immediately replaced by socfpga-4.19 port in a95855a70)

notes:
**CRITICAL STRATEGIC FINDING**: This commit's purpose (removal + replacement by socfpga-4.19 port) was **entirely abandoned at v5.15**. The v5.15.1 baseline (pristine, hash-verified per patch-provenance.md §1.2) kept vanilla dwc2 unchanged from stock v5.15. This indicates the socfpga-4.19 port was deliberately dropped when the fork upgraded to v5.15, reverting to vanilla dwc2 for all subsequent versions (v5.15 → 6.18). **Reasons for abandonment**: (1) Maintainability — a 4.19-based port becomes increasingly divergent and harder to carry forward as the kernel advances (5.15 → 5.18 → 6.x); (2) Upstream maturity — vanilla dwc2 between 4.19 and 5.15 received numerous bug fixes and enhancements (ISOC handling, DDMA flow improvements, hibernation support, etc.) making the custom port obsolete; (3) **No critical socfpga-specific features remain missing** — the fact that we successfully boot and operate with vanilla dwc2 in 6.18 proves the socfpga port was not carrying essential hardware-specific logic. **The ONLY dwc2 patch we carry in 6.18 is 0028 (unaligned-IN split transfer fix, d7adb20b4)**, which is a genuine host-controller bug affecting any split-IN endpoint with wMaxPacketSize not a multiple of 4. This fix is NOT in vanilla 6.18 (hcd_intr.c:922-928 still has the old unconditional align_buf copy confined to isoc-specific path dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in). Recommendation: patch 0028 should be submitted upstream — it benefits all dwc2 users and removes a carried-patch obligation. **No escalation warranted** — the wholesale swap was deliberately strategic, not a missed carry or misclassification.

sonnet-verified: correct, all claims independently re-derived. Branch containment: `git branch --contains c3c1a0ec9... -a` returns ONLY remotes/origin/MiSTer-v5.14 (not v5.13.12, not v5.15) — confirms source_branch and the 'v5.14-only, abandoned before v5.15' story. Diff stat: `git show --stat` gives exactly 18 files changed, 25683 deletions(-), matching removed=25683. The 'abandoned swap' claim verified directly: diffed MiSTer-v5.15's drivers/usb/dwc2/hcd_intr.c against vanilla linux-5.15.1's hcd_intr.c — the only delta is the unaligned-IN-split fix (patch 0028, d7adb20b4), i.e. v5.15 = vanilla 5.15.1 dwc2 + patch 0028, NOT the socfpga-v4.19 port from this pair of commits. patch-provenance.md §1.1 does discuss version-bump commits (e.g. aba1ef4c1 at v5.15.1) replacing the tree wholesale, confirming the provenance_doc_ref. hcd_intr.c:922-928 in /mnt/source/linux (vanilla 6.18) confirmed to still gate the align_buf copy inside dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in() only (same bug patch 0028 fixes) — cross-checked against a95855a703fa7ae9714390487965a0a7e84406dd's identical claim, see that record. No errors found in this record.
provenance_doc_ref: Not mentioned in patch-provenance.md (correctly omitted: commit was abandoned on v5.14, never carried to v5.15+). See patch-provenance.md §1.1 note on version-bump commits replacing tree wholesale. No contradiction found.

notes: V5.14-ONLY COMMIT — not present on v5.15 (stock's release kernel version). Deliberately replaced by a more comprehensive version (33ff5146a) that re-commits these three driver trees with UPDATES (fixes for non-existent kernel includes like <net/ipx.h>, parameter adjustments) plus adds two additional drivers (rtl8188eu, rtl8188fu) and rtl8812au. The v5.14 vs v5.15 split point represents Sorgelig's iteration on the driver integration strategy: v5.14 separated the rtl8188eu/rtl8188fu addition (0d8641a2b, Aug 30) from the rtl8821au/rtl88x2bu/rtl8821cu group (c70a3fc27, Aug 31), then v5.15 unified all five drivers into 33ff5146a (Nov 8) with fixes and additions. Stock's v5.15.1 config reflects the later state: CONFIG_RTL8188EU=m / RTL8188FU=m / RTL8812AU=m / RTL8821AU=m / RTL8822BU=m / RTL8821CU=m (stock's config files show RTL8812AU and RTL8821AU as SEPARATE symbols, which only exist in the fork post-3740d5b88, confirming stock built a later revision, not 33ff5146a byte-for-byte either — the lineage continued to evolve). Our 6.18 build splits coverage per-chipset: RTL8821A→BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR, RTL8812A→BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU, RTL88X2BU→mainline rtw88, RTL8821CU→mainline rtw88 (never built the vendored tree in our config, via deliberate Kconfig choice, see linux.config:545 comment 'mainline still cannot drive' + hardware decision on WPA3 support at line 478-481). Zero silent regression: all four driver families have live, working coverage. Severity=none because stock v5.15 users already moved past this commit to 33ff5146a (and further to morrownr in 3740d5b88), and we have working drivers via either package or mainline in 6.18. The provenance.md document (dated 2026-07-08, covering v5.15 HEAD state only) does not reference this v5.14-only commit; no prior-art contradiction to flag. CORRECTION (Sonnet audit, batch A7): the prior version of this record mislabeled one of the two chip families this commit's combined rtl8821au tree covers as 'RTL8811A' (affected_hardware + device_id 0bda:8811) — re-derivation of the vendored os_dep/linux/usb_intf.c shows this tree actually contains TWO separate USB-ID blocks, one for RTL8812A (0x8812/0x881a/0x881b/0x881c, driver_info=RTL8812) and one for RTL8821A (0x0811/0x0820/0x0821/0x0823/0x8822, driver_info=RTL8821) — matching its own Kconfig help text 'Realtek 8812A/8821A USB WiFi'. There is no RTL8811A device ID anywhere in this commit's payload; 0bda:8811 (as opposed to the correct 0x0811) is actually an RTL8821C ID in mainline's rtw8821cu.c, unrelated to this tree. Corrected affected_hardware/device_ids to RTL8812A + the real RTL8821A IDs, added the previously-missing BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y to superseded_by (RTL8812A coverage was omitted even though BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR was cited for RTL8821A), and replaced the upstream.vanilla_file_line/vanilla_quote's stray citation of rtl8xxxu/core.c's RTL8188EU/FU device table (irrelevant — this commit never touches rtl8188eu/rtl8188fu, that's bdedb82d/0d8641a2b's payload) with the actually-matching rtw8812au.c:11-19 / rtw8821au.c:11-23 citations, which now correctly back the vanilla_shas already listed (8f82bb2cfaf7, 1ee6ff9ae3c1). Disposition (dropped-deliberate) and severity (none) are unchanged — this was a citation/labeling accuracy fix, not a disposition change. sonnet-verified: corrected — RTL8811A/RTL8812A mislabel and missing RTL8812AU package fixed; per-chipset coverage claim now fully grounded.

notes: MiSTer's 5.15 fork vendored the original medusalix/xone driver and patched in per-PID firmware support (this commit). However, the actively-maintained dlundqvist/xone fork — which we now package as BR2_PACKAGE_XONE — already natively includes identical per-PID firmware logic (sprintf(fwname, "xone_dongle_%04x.bin", fw_product) in transport/dongle.c:1117). This commit is therefore NOT a carry: it's functionality already present in our packaged source tree. Documentation of both PIDs' firmware is handled by package/xow-firmware/, which ships xone_dongle_02fe.bin (new adapter, also aliased to xow_dongle.bin for stock parity) and xone_dongle_02e6.bin (old adapter, a distinct 70008-byte blob from a different Microsoft .cab). Both external-USB PIDs are covered; PIDs 0x02f9 and 0x091e are soldered-in laptop modules and deliberately omitted (docs/decisions/0003-xone-firmware.md). Verified independently: dlundqvist source f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280 (package/xone/xone.mk XONE_VERSION) contains transport/dongle.c:1110-1118 (fw_product logic) and :1307-1308 (id assignment), matching the MiSTer commit's intent byte-for-byte. CORRECTION (sonnet audit): forward_port.applies_to_6_18 changed from true/effort:none to false/effort:null for consistency — 'applies_to_6_18' describes whether this literal kernel-tree patch would forward-port onto the 6.18 source (compare a genuinely carried record, e.g. 2d39e76d1, which correctly uses true+trivial for a patch that DOES still apply to the tree). Since xone left the kernel tree entirely for BR2_PACKAGE_XONE, there is no patch to apply to 6.18 at all — 'true' was conflating 'the functionality exists in 6.18's build' (correct) with 'the patch applies to 6.18' (not applicable), and was inconsistent with sibling xone records (4ddd8ec3d, 8270e78f4, e2eb39e6f, d5beb5aa6), which all correctly use false. sonnet-verified: corrected — sprintf/fw_product logic and firmware package split confirmed byte-for-byte at packaged dongle.c:1108-1118, xow-firmware.mk 02fe/02e6 split matches ADR 0003 scope claims exactly; forward_port vocabulary normalized.

The 'default LOGITECH_FF' claim is confirmed at drivers/hid/Kconfig (vanilla 6.18): 'config LOGIWHEELS_FF ... default LOGITECH_FF'; LOGIG940_FF has no default line (defaults n) but IS explicit =y in our fragment. Crucially this was verified against the STRONGEST possible evidence, not just static Kconfig reading: the actual post-olddefconfig resolved kernel .config at /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config shows CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y, CONFIG_LOGIG940_FF=y, AND CONFIG_LOGIWHEELS_FF=y (lines 2949, 2951, 2952) even though LOGIWHEELS_FF has no line at all in our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config fragment (grep confirms absent) — proving the default-resolution chain actually fires in our real build, not just in theory. Per the macvlan-pilot precedent (not-evaluated is reserved for cases where OUR build genuinely lacks the resulting symbol, e.g. CONFIG_MACVLAN which has no line anywhere and resolves to unset), this record's prior 'not-evaluated' was a misapplication: the commit's intent (both wheel/flight-stick FF options enabled) IS achieved in our actual build. author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: BR2_PACKAGE_XONE (dlundqvist/xone@f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280)

impact.effect_if_absent: GIP authentication subsystem (auth/ and crypto/) would be missing from xone driver; newer firmware dongles and Xbox Series X|S controllers requiring authentication would fail to authenticate

license_provenance: SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later per auth.c, crypto.c (Copyright (C) 2023 Severin von Wnuck-Lipinski <severinvonw@outlook.de>). Verified in package source via xone.mk license-header audit (all source files including auth.c, crypto.c checked at pin time 2026-07-13).
notes: CRITICAL: This is NOT a silent regression. The commit adds GIP auth v1 and v2 protocols to the xone driver via auth/auth.c (665 lines) and auth/crypto.c (290 lines), plus three new device drivers (madcatz_glam, madcatz_strat, pdp_jaguar). The kernel-tree copy of xone has been superseded by BR2_PACKAGE_XONE (dlundqvist/xone fork), which is pinned to commit f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280. This pinned commit includes the auth subsystem — verified by xone.mk explicitly checking auth.c and crypto.c for GPL-2.0-or-later headers at package pin time (2026-07-13). The actively-maintained dlundqvist fork independently developed equivalent auth support to what Sorgelig added to the kernel tree on 2026-04-17; dlundqvist's fork is explicitly the successor per the original medusalix/xone README ('in maintenance mode'). Architectural change (P3.2, re-source class D): no kernel patch carried; feature provided entirely by BR2_PACKAGE_XONE. No compat patches needed for 6.18 either (see xone.mk notes). Zero device IDs are *added* in this commit (new drivers use kernel input event codes, not new hardcoded IDs); verify new hardware support via the added driver bindings if needed. CORRECTIONS (sonnet audit): (1) device_ids previously listed '045e:02ea', which does not appear anywhere in this commit's diff or in the packaged dlundqvist source (grep for '02ea' in both returns zero hits) — removed as fabricated/ungrounded. The wired transport (transport/wired.c:544 XONE_WIRED_VENDOR macro, id table at line 545 `{ XONE_WIRED_VENDOR(0x045e) }`) matches Xbox Series X|S and other wired controllers by USB vendor ID + interface class/subclass/protocol only, not by product ID — the specific controller model is negotiated at the GIP protocol level, not via USB PID. Mad Catz (vendor 0x0738) and PDP (vendor 0x0e6f) — the vendors behind the three new drivers added in this commit — are likewise matched by vendor ID only in the same table, confirming no per-model PID exists to cite for any of the three new drivers. Retained 045e:0b00 (Elite Series 2) as the one grounded, still-relevant ID (its GIP client code in bus/protocol.c and driver/gamepad.c is rewritten by this commit). (2) license_provenance date range '2023-2024' corrected to '2023' — both auth.c and crypto.c headers (in this commit's own diff, and in the packaged tarball) read 'Copyright (C) 2023', no 2024 date appears in either file. sonnet-verified: corrected — auth.c/crypto.c line counts and new driver files (madcatz_glam.c, madcatz_strat.c, pdp_jaguar.c) confirmed present in package/xone extract; device_ids and license date range fixed as above.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.5 — re-sourced class D; all MiSTer xone commits (4ddd8ec3d, 5a7965488, c708f2222, e2eb39e6f, 8270e78f4, d776ddb4e, d5beb5aa6) migrated from kernel tree to BR2_PACKAGE_XONE

author: Nigel Shearman

impact.effect_if_absent: No MACVLAN virtual network interface support; container networking tools like Docker cannot create multiple virtual MACs on a single interface; network namespace isolation via macvlan is unavailable.

notes: CONFIG_MACVLAN has been upstream since Linux 2.6.23 (2007, commit b863ceb7ddce [NET]: Add macvlan driver). The fork deliberately enables it in MiSTer_defconfig to support Docker container networking and multi-MAC scenarios. However, CRITICAL CAVEAT: Buildroot (the actual build system) does not use the fork's arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig. Instead, Buildroot is configured (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config) to build 6.18.38 vanilla and uses a separate custom linux.config file (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config in Buildroot) — which DOES NOT include CONFIG_MACVLAN. Therefore, this fork-repository change has NO effect on the actual MiSTer images built by Buildroot. The MiSTer_defconfig appears to be maintained as a reference or for standalone kernel builds from the fork, but not for Buildroot. If MACVLAN support is desired in Buildroot-built images, it must be added to board/mister/de10nano/linux.config, not relying on MiSTer_defconfig. This is a config-level forward-port opportunity (add to linux.config if Docker support is desired) but requires a separate decision outside this commit's scope. Failure mode is silent: if the feature is wanted and not enabled, container networking simply fails without a loud error. Author co-authored with Claude Opus 4.6. | orchestrator-verified (Fable, 2026-07-15): confirmed by direct grep — fork HEAD MiSTer_defconfig:1302 has CONFIG_MACVLAN=y (commit dated 2026-07-08); our stock inventory docs/stock-inventory/stock-linux.config:1302 still has '# CONFIG_MACVLAN is not set' (snapshot PREDATES this stock commit); our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config has NO MACVLAN line (disabled). Disposition corrected carried->not-evaluated: the functionality is NOT in our build and no deliberate drop decision exists. ACTION REQUIRED: config-parity decision — add CONFIG_MACVLAN (=y or =m) to linux.config to match stock-next, or document a deliberate drop. Also a process finding: stock defconfig commits newer than our stock-inventory snapshot are a live drift channel; Phase 2 config reconciliation must diff against fork HEAD defconfig, not only the aged stock-linux.config snapshot. | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): CONFIG_MACVLAN=y added to board/mister/de10nano/linux.config — stock-next parity restored; config drift closed.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md: defconfig (11 commits) group, classified as 'config — feeds P1.3'; P1.3 note flags stock-linux.config as 15 months old and lacking CONFIG_MACVLAN=y
