upstream.vanilla_quote:
config MACVLAN
	tristate "MAC-VLAN support"
	help
	  This allows one to create virtual interfaces that map packets to
	  or from specific MAC addresses to a particular interface.

userspace_coupling.interface: socket/ioctl (SIOCADDRT, netlink RTM_* messages via libnl); Docker and container runtimes call ip link add type macvlan or use netlink directly

author: Sorgelig

affected_hardware: EDUP EP-AC1661 (named in the fork commit message as the motivating device with a known-bad EFUSE batch)
impact.effect_if_absent: EFUSE-corrupted rtl8821c-family adapters (map byte at offset 0xCA reads 0xFF/unprogrammed) get efuse->rfe_option = 0xFF & 0x1f = 0x1F in mainline rtw88, which matches none of the explicit switch cases in rtw8821c_read_efuse() (0x2/0x4/0x7/0xa/0xc/0xf) so hal->rfe_btg stays false, get_cck_rx_pwr() falls into the 'else' LNA gain table branch, and rtw8821c_coex_cfg_rfe_type()'s switch on rfe_module_type=0x1F hits whatever its default case is (not individually enumerated here) instead of the correct antenna/RFE-module case. Net effect: silently wrong RX LNA gain table selection and wrong BT-coexistence antenna/RFE-module configuration — degraded RX sensitivity and/or wrong BT-coex antenna switching, NOT a bind/probe failure and NOT boot-critical. The device still binds (its USB ID is in the mainline table) and should still associate to WiFi; the regression is silent RF/coex misconfiguration, not non-functionality.

notes:
sonnet-verified (ESCALATION review, redone from scratch):

(1) FORK COMMIT — confirmed via `git -C /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer show fc09a292a97b7cb2c4e1f60d55615bc8df419061`. Exact added hunk in hal/rtl8821c/rtl8821c_ops.c Hal_ReadRFEType():
```
 hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C];
 if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type)
     goto exit;
+
+// fix for bad EFUSE in EDUP EP-AC1661
+hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C-1];
+if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type)
+    goto exit;
```
EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C is `#define EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C 0xCA` (include/hal_pg.h:518 in the fork). Confirms the record's claimed offset 0xCA→0xC9 fallback exactly. The Makefile hunk is unrelated debug-logging config (CONFIG_RTW_DEBUG/CONFIG_RTW_LOG_LEVEL) and does not affect this analysis. IMPORTANT: this code is NOT gated on any USB VID/PID — it is a blanket change to Hal_ReadRFEType() affecting every rtl8821c-family device that hits mapvalid && map[0xCA]==0xFF, not an EDUP-specific quirk. EDUP EP-AC1661 is only the motivating case named in the commit message; any 8821c unit with the same bad-efuse symptom would hit the same fallback.

(2) VANILLA rtw8821c.c — confirmed NO equivalent fallback. rtw8821c_read_efuse() does a single unconditional read `efuse->rfe_option = map->rfe_option & 0x1f;` with no 0xFF sentinel check and no secondary-offset fallback anywhere in the function or file (grep of 'rfe_option' across rtw8821c.c shows only the 6 use sites quoted above — none add a bad-value guard). CRITICAL NUANCE not captured in the first-pass record: I pulled the actual `struct rtw8821c_efuse` layout from rtw8821c.h:74-98 and counted offsets — the byte immediately preceding `rfe_option` (0xCA) is `rf_antenna_option` (0xC9), a DIFFERENT, semantically distinct EFUSE field (antenna option, not a redundant/backup copy of RFE type). So the fork's 'fallback' is not reading a legitimate secondary RFE-type storage location — it is reinterpreting an adjacent, unrelated antenna-option byte as an RFE type when the real byte is blank. This is a hacky, batch-specific heuristic (it happened to produce a plausible rfe_type value for the specific bad-EFUSE units Sorgelig had in hand), not a principled fix. This matters for anyone considering forward-porting it: it should not be blindly re-implemented as 'read offset N-1 as a generic bad-efuse fallback' without re-verifying against the actual bad-efuse dumps from real hardware.

(3) GIT LOG SEARCH in /mnt/source/linux v5.15.1..v6.18.38 on drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/: `--grep=8821c --grep=efuse --all-match` returns 6 commits (2c17afde9ff6, 74a72c367573, 8fbcaa308591, 0a999d82b782, 64e9d5646535, 5db4943a9d6f) — none add a bad/blank-efuse rfe_option fallback for 8821c. Separate `--grep=8821c` finds e1958f4d66a5 ('rtw8821cu: Add ID for Mercusys MU6H') — adding a new device ID, no efuse quirk. Separate `--grep=efuse` finds 2c17afde9ff6 ('wifi: rtw88: Handle RTL8723D(S) with blank efuse', May 2025) — this IS a real precedent for rtw88 handling blank/near-blank efuse (default TX power/antenna/crystal-cap values when the chip's efuse is missing them) but it is scoped to RTL8723D(S)/RTL8703B only (moved into a shared __rtl8723x_read_efuse()), and does not touch rtw8821c.c. So mainline is aware of and has fixed blank-efuse issues for a sibling chip family, but has never received an equivalent fix for 8821c's rfe_option. Confirms absence with high confidence — not merely an omission I failed to find.

(4) DEVICE BINDING — confirmed 0x7392:0xc811 and 0x7392:0xd811 are both present in the rtw8821cu USB ID table: `drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8821cu.c:36,38` — `{ USB_DEVICE_AND_INTERFACE_INFO(0x7392, 0xc811, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff), ... },` / `{ USB_DEVICE_AND_INTERFACE_INFO(0x7392, 0xd811, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff), ... },`. So a device with these IDs DOES bind to mainline rtw88_8821cu in our build. A bad efuse does NOT block binding/probe (severity is NOT boot-critical) — it produces silent RF/BT-coex misconfiguration as detailed in `impact.effect_if_absent` above. Caveat: I could not independently verify against an authoritative USB ID database that 0x7392:0xc811/0xd811 specifically ARE the EDUP EP-AC1661's registered IDs (no internet access in this sandbox) — that mapping is inherited from the first-pass record. The code-level regression finding (no fallback exists in mainline, for ANY 8821c device with this bad-efuse symptom) stands independently of whether this exact VID:PID pair is verified.

(5) BR2 PACKAGE CHECK — /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/package/rtl8821cu-morrownr/ EXISTS (github morrownr/8821cu-20210916 @ 7f63a9da2e8ed83403f6f920e9b1628a37b38ef4, GPL-2.0). I extracted the actual pinned tarball from dl/rtl8821cu-morrownr/ and read hal/rtl8821c/rtl8821c_ops.c directly: Hal_ReadRFEType() there is the UNPATCHED original — `hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C]; if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type) goto exit;` followed straight by the 'Empty EFUSE with unknown RFE type!!' error path — NO fallback to offset 0xC9. So the morrownr out-of-tree package does NOT carry Sorgelig's workaround either. Additionally this package is OFF by default: `configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:570` — '# BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821CU_MORROWNR is not set -> mainline rtw88_8821cu (8811cu/8821cu)', and `board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:488` confirms `CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m` is what actually ships. CONCLUSION: neither of the two available paths for this hardware in our current build (default mainline rtw88_8821cu, or opt-in package/rtl8821cu-morrownr) carries this fix. This does NOT change the regression story as hypothesized in the task — it STRENGTHENS it: there is genuinely zero coverage anywhere in this repo's build surface today.

SCHEMA/GROUNDING-CONTRACT CORRECTION: the first-pass record paired `upstream.equivalence="contradicted"` with `disposition="dropped-upstream"`. worker-instructions.md's Pilot Lessons explicitly forbid this exact pairing ('Never pair contradicted with disposition="dropped-upstream"' — contradicted must be treated as a carry candidate). Reclassified disposition to `needs-verification` here: the code-level facts are fully grounded (high confidence, no hardware needed to confirm the mainline/morrownr gap), but the disposition enum has no clean 'confirmed gap, pending carry decision' value, and a human decision is still needed on whether/how to carry this forward (most plausible path: a small patch to package/rtl8821cu-morrownr's vendored source for users who hit this specific bad-efuse batch, since that's the only in-repo tree with the matching vendor code structure — patching mainline rtw88 upstream would need re-deriving the fix against the real bad-efuse dumps given point (2)'s antenna_option/rfe_option nuance). Also cleared `dependencies.superseded_by` (previously named mainline rtw88_8821cu as superseding this commit) since 'superseded_by' implies functional equivalence per the pilot lessons, which is contradicted, not established, here.

SILENT REGRESSION: confirmed. failure_mode=silent, severity=feature-loss (not boot-critical — device still binds and functions, just misconfigured). Realistically affects only the small population of 8821c-family USB adapters with this specific bad-EFUSE symptom (evidently at least one EDUP EP-AC1661 batch); most users' hardware is unaffected since ordinarily map[0xCA] is programmed.

sonnet-verified: CONFIRMED with correction — the core claim (no equivalent fallback anywhere in mainline rtw88 or in our build's morrownr alternative) holds up under independent re-derivation, but the first-pass record violated the project's own grounding-contract pilot lesson (contradicted+dropped-upstream pairing) and understated the BR2-package angle (which, once checked, reinforces rather than undermines the regression finding). Disposition corrected to needs-verification; superseded_by corrected to empty; one new technical nuance surfaced (0xC9 = rf_antenna_option, not a backup RFE slot). | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): user decision: stay on mainline rtw88 (newer drivers). Known limitation documented: EDUP EP-AC1661 units with corrupt EFUSE get silently degraded RF/coex config; revisit only on a user hardware report.
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md:389 — grouped row (9 commits: 33ff5146a, 3740d5b88, 2371fb1aa, 143ce187e, 4e98a68d1, 43fbb63ae, 993b82e31, 115b1d1ae, fc09a292a). Text: 'Verify the two local device fixes are present upstream or re-apply as package patches.' Note: this is a conditional TODO, not an assertion that the fix IS upstream — so there is nothing in it to literally 'agree'/'disagree' with; verification below resolves the open question (answer: NOT upstream, re-apply-as-patch branch applies).
subject: rtl8821cu: workaround for bad efuse in EDUP EP-AC1661.

author: Sorgelig

superseded_by: CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m (mainline in-kernel driver, board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:215) -- ACTIVE coverage for 0BDA:8179 and 0BDA:0179 (RTL8188EU), confirmed at /mnt/source/linux/drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtl8xxxu/core.c:8060-8067

package/rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng -- present in tree (commit-pinned, hash-verified) but explicitly DISABLED (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:568, '# ... is not set'); documented inert fallback, NOT the active coverage path (per 0d8641a2b9c9524f83b0d32cd8ef6332a2a324c3.json, which established this for the sibling v5.14 rtl8188eu/rtl8188fu-add commit)

impact.effect_if_absent: None in our build: the RTL8188EU/RTL8188FU device IDs this driver update touches (0BDA:8179, 0BDA:0179) are natively claimed by mainline in-kernel CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:215), which our build ships as a module. The matching Buildroot package (rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng) exists in the tree but is explicitly DISABLED (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:568, '# BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8188EU_AIRCRACK_NG is not set -> mainline rtl8xxxu (8188eu)') specifically so it does not bind-fight rtl8xxxu on the same USB IDs — it is not the active coverage path.

license_provenance.header: Realtek GPL-2.0 boilerplate: 'Copyright(c) 2007 - 2017 Realtek Corporation. This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of version 2 of the GNU General Public License...'

notes:
Huge vendored driver update (695 files, 168K insertions, 273K deletions — confirmed exactly via `git show --stat` against the live fork checkout). v5.13.12 kernel branch only (confirmed via `git branch --contains`, remotes/origin/MiSTer-v5.13.12 exclusively). Per patch-provenance.md §3.6: rtl8188eu and related Realtek drivers were re-sourced from kernel vendor to Buildroot out-of-tree packages. Directive: 'Do not vendor.' This commit's kernel changes are NOT carried forward to 6.18. Disposition: obsolete in current build context (unchanged).

sonnet-verified: corrected. The original record's superseded_by/effect_if_absent claimed our 6.18 build 'ships rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng package instead' — that is stale/wrong. The sibling record 0d8641a2b9c9524f83b0d32cd8ef6332a2a324c3.json (a later Sonnet-corrected record for the closely-related v5.14 'Add rtl8188eu, rtl8188fu WiFi drivers' commit) already established, by direct grep of configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:568, that BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8188EU_AIRCRACK_NG is explicitly DISABLED ('# ... is not set', with an inline comment '-> mainline rtl8xxxu (8188eu)'), and that the actual active coverage is mainline CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:215), which unconditionally claims 0BDA:8179 and 0BDA:0179 in /mnt/source/linux/drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtl8xxxu/core.c:8060-8067. Re-verified that finding independently for this record (same defconfig line, same linux.config line, same core.c citation) and aligned superseded_by/effect_if_absent/severity accordingly: severity changed feature-loss -> none (nothing is actually absent from the build), superseded_by now leads with CONFIG_RTL8XXXU=m and demotes the aircrack-ng package to an inert, present-but-disabled fallback. change_type normalized from 'Driver update' to 'vendored-driver-update' to match this batch's kebab-case convention (vendored-driver-tree / vendored-driver-add / driver-port / compiler-flags / removal-of-wholesale-replacement). Disposition (dropped-obsolete) unchanged — this was a coverage-claim correction, not a disposition change.
subject: Update rtl8188eu driver.

author: gkrzystek

superseded_by: vanilla rtw88_8822bu (drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8822bu.c, CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m, board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:481) -- claims 7392:B822 in-tree

package/rtl8812au (BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y, morrownr/8812au-20210820@8cac6f43316a56cc89cc8cb532cd6c6ae14c4805) -- its device table never included 7392:B822, so the ID-routing bug is absent by construction

impact.effect_if_absent: None. Dropping this commit has zero effect on our build: (1) our BR2 rtl8812au package is sourced from a different, newer upstream line (morrownr/8812au-20210820 @ 8cac6f4) whose usb_intf.c device table never contained 7392:B822 in the first place (only 7392:A822/A811/A834 - verified in the built tree), so the mis-binding bug this commit fixes cannot occur; (2) the rtl88x2bu package that the original fix routes the device TO is present in our tree but explicitly disabled (BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU is not set); (3) the actual driver that claims 7392:B822 in our build is the mainline in-tree rtw88 driver (CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m), which owns this exact USB ID with an explicit '/* Edimax EW-7822ULC */' comment and goes through mac80211/cfg80211 with verified-on-hardware WPA3/SAE/PMF support -- functionally superior to the out-of-tree 88x2bu path this commit was steering the device toward. The commit's secondary cfg80211_ch_switch_* Linux-6.3+ compat shims are also moot: the newer morrownr rtl8812au source we pin already carries its own multi-version compat handling for cfg80211_ch_switch_started_notify (3 ifdef'd call signatures, verified in os_dep/linux/ioctl_cfg80211.c:459-474 of the built package).

license_provenance: Realtek proprietary driver (GPL-2.0 header in source); not applicable to disposition since none of the touched vendored trees (fork rtl8812au/rtl8821au/rtl8821cu/rtl88x2bu) ship in our build -- they were replaced wholesale by independently re-sourced BR2 packages per patch-provenance.md line 389 (P3.1).
notes: CORRECTED from first-pass 'carried'. 'carried' has a precise meaning in this schema: shipped as a 00xx patch file in board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/. There is no such patch (grep of the 25 files in linux-patches/ confirms no realtek-wifi entry), and there could not sensibly be one: our build does not vendor the fork's rtl8812au/rtl8821au/rtl8821cu/rtl88x2bu source trees at all. It re-sources RTL8812AU/RTL8821AU/RTL8814AU from independently-pinned, newer morrownr forks (package/rtl8812au, rtl8821au-morrownr, rtl8814au-morrownr) that already build cleanly against 6.18 and already lack the specific bug this commit fixes, and it moved RTL8822B (this commit's actual subject, 7392:B822 Edimax EW-7822ULC) off the out-of-tree Realtek family entirely onto the mainline rtw88 driver (CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m) -- rtl88x2bu ships as a BR2 package but is deliberately disabled (BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU is not set), documented in the defconfig as a fallback only, with the mainline path preferred because the out-of-tree 88x2bu 'advertised SAE+CMAC but failed WPA3-only association, status_code=1 -- verified on hardware' (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:571-579, see also docs/wifi-parity.md). Net effect: the commit's own fix target (rtl88x2bu / rtl8812au ID table) is either absent-by-construction or inactive in our build, and the device it's about is served by a better upstream mechanism. This is 'dropped-obsolete', not 'carried', and not 'dropped-upstream' either (vanilla doesn't backport this commit's specific diff -- it independently and more completely solves the same real-world problem via a different architecture, hence equivalence='superseded-better' rather than 'equivalent'). license_provenance.author: Synopsys Inc. (2004-2013)
license_provenance.origin_project: Synopsys DesignWare USB2 OTG Controller driver; ported from socfpga-v4.19 (Intel Altera SoC kernel)

notes:
Phase 1 APPENDIX: old-branch sweep of MiSTer-v5.14. This commit is NOT on the stock MiSTer-v5.15 branch; it was replaced by version-bump to v5.15.1 which carried vanilla v5.15 dwc2. Stock now uses vanilla 6.18.38 dwc2. The socfpga-v4.19 port adds 24,080 lines but contains NO socfpga-specific tuning or board-specific parameters — it is a generic dwc2 driver backport. KEY FINDING: Both the v4.19 port (this commit) and vanilla 6.18.38 dwc2 contain an identical bug: the unaligned DMA buffer copy (for non-4-byte-aligned wMaxPacketSize split transfers) is only performed for isochronous transfers in dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in(), silently dropping data for all other split-IN transfer types (interrupt, bulk, control). This is documented in carried patch 0028 (Martin Donlon's unaligned-IN fix), which was added to MiSTer-v5.15 and must be carried to 6.18 (already done as patch 0028-dwc2-fix-unaligned-in-split.patch). The v4.19 port is OBSOLETE for the stock build because: (1) it is on v5.14, not stock v5.15; (2) stock now uses vanilla 6.18 which supersedes this port (v6.18 is post-v5.15 upstream evolution); (3) the critical bug fix (patch 0028) is platform-agnostic and applies to vanilla 6.18. USB on DE10-Nano goes through dwc2 — all USB devices depend on this controller. Without patch 0028, devices with non-aligned wMaxPacketSize split-IN transfers would silently lose data, but this is a general dwc2 bug (not MiSTer-specific), not a regression from dropping this port.

(2) 'No socfpga-specific code' claim: `git show --stat` confirms 17 files / 24080 insertions (matches added=24080 exactly); grepped the entire ported tree (core.c through platform.c) for 'socfpga|altera|intel|de10|arria|cyclone' — zero hits outside the commit message itself; platform.c (552 lines) has no board-specific parameters. This is a generic upstream-derived dwc2 backport, not a SoC-tuned port, exactly as claimed. (3) 'Same unaligned-IN bug as vanilla' claim: this port's hcd_intr.c has the align_buf copy-back at line 952-957, inside the SAME single function (dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in, structurally identical to vanilla's) — confirmed by reading the diff context — i.e. the identical isoc-only-copy bug patch 0028 fixes in vanilla. notes:
LARGE VENDORED COMMIT: 23 files, 12151 lines of Samsung exfat driver (exfat-nofuse lineage, v1.2.11). This is a COMPLETE FILESYSTEM DRIVER REPLACEMENT, not a set of patches. The commit added not just symlink support but a parallel 20-file filesystem implementation that replaces vanilla exfat entirely (commit sequence: 8b6b8c2f5 removes mainline exfat files, df35bdb27 adds Samsung driver). The MiSTer kernel fork remains on this driver through v5.15. HOWEVER: Buildroot_MiSTer's 6.18 build uses vanilla 6.18 exfat (not the Samsung driver), because the Buildroot environment kernels the vanilla Linux tree (/mnt/source/linux v6.18.38), not the fork. Vanilla 6.18 exfat has NO symlink support (grep symlink fs/exfat/*.c → empty). Symlink functionality was restored via carried patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, which re-implements symlinks using vanilla 6.18 infrastructure (page_symlink/page_get_link), reusing ONLY the on-disk format (EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK = 0x0004, EXFAT_ATTR_SYMLINK_OLD = 0x0040) from this commit. The carried patch is ~140 lines and does NOT forward-port the Samsung driver code; it is a fresh implementation. Disposition REFLECTS the MiSTer v5.15 fork context where this commit was made, not the 6.18 build context. In the fork, this commit was live until superseded by the 6.18 migration and ADR 0010/0019 decisions (ADR 0010 dropped the driver entirely; ADR 0019 restored symlinks only via patch 0031). SILENT-REGRESSION FLAG: If dropped without patch 0031, symlink resolution fails silently; Main_MiSTer resolves DT_LNK entries (file_io.cpp:1592) and the arcade organizer's _Organized tree depends on symlinks. The carried patch has high coupling: MiSTer mounts /media/fat with sync,dirsync flags (MiSTer kernel patches init/do_mounts.c), and on-disk exfat symlink format must remain stable across driver updates (existing user cards have symlinks written by the stock kernel's Samsung driver). AGREES WITH PROVENANCE: Patch-provenance.md §3.8 (class G, exfat replacement) and §N1 correctly identify the driver swap and symlink loss, and the resolution via ADR 0010 + ADR 0019. Carried patch 0031 is listed in patch-provenance.md §N1 as the implemented solution (option c: add symlink support to mainline exfat). No ambiguity or misclassification.
